The WBS is a hierarchical decomposition of the total scope of work to be carried out by the project team to accomplish the project objectives and create the required deliverables. The scope baseline is the approved version of a scope statement, WBS, and its associated WBS dictionary, which can be changed only through formal change control procedures and is used as a basis for comparison. Therefore, among the available choices, you should first submit a change request to update the scope baseline. Note, after the change is evaluated, additional components of the project management plan may need to be updated. However, this option is not provided. Therefore, submitting a change request to update the scope baseline is the best response.

questions.prompt: A project manager is leading a large project to develop a new model of smartphone. Early in project execution, to foster the stakeholder involvement, the project manager holds a meeting as specified in the stakeholder engagement plan. What type of meeting does the project manager likely hold?
questions.option_a: A project retrospective to finalize lessons learned register
questions.option_b: A workshop to develop the team charter
questions.option_c: A project kick-off meeting to establish roles and responsibilities of stakeholders
questions.option_d: A meeting to create a comprehensive list of stakeholders
questions.explanation: The stakeholder engagement plan is a component of the project management plan that identifies the strategies and actions required to promote the productive involvement of stakeholders in project decision making and execution. The stakeholder engagement plan is created as an output of the Plan Stakeholder Engagement process and followed during the Manage Stakeholder Engagement process. Thus, the question is asking which type of meeting might be held during the Manage Stakeholder Engagement process. A kick-off meeting is one of the types of meetings that may be held in support of the Manage Stakeholder Engagement process to foster the appropriate level of stakeholder involvement in the project. The incorrect answer choices represent meetings that are exclusive to other project management processes and would not be held as part of the Manage Stakeholder Engagement process. Therefore, of the available choices, the project manager is most likely holding a project kick-off meeting. Note, the scenario describes a large project in its early execution stage. In large projects, the kick-off meeting takes place with processes in the Executing Process Group, thus additionally supporting the correct answer.

questions.prompt: You are managing a manufacturing process improvement project. A key stakeholder suggests a correction to the procedure being used to identify defects in the product. You agree that the modified procedure will better help identify defects. You submit a change request to modify the procedure and have the change approved. When implementing the change request, you should document the updated procedure in all of the following places, except?
questions.explanation: Approved change requests are implemented in the Direct and Manage Project Work process, which is part of the Executing Process Group. Updates to the quality management plan (a component of the project management plan), organizational process assets, and lessons learned register are among the outputs of this process. Updating these project artifacts would help ensure that the improved procedure will be used for defects identification in future project work. However, the question is looking for an exception. The stakeholder register does not contain information about procedures used on the project, and therefore should not be updated. The fact that the improved procedure has been suggested by a stakeholder does not affect any information about the stakeholder themselves making the update of the stakeholder register unnecessary. Hence, of the available choices, the stakeholder register is the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: At the beginning of your project, you were authorized to offer incentives to the designers to work in your company's office building for the duration of the project. You secured a temporary workspace to accommodate the entire project design team in a common area. This strategy allows them to work in proximity to one another to enhance their ability to perform as a unified design team. What tool or technique did you use in this scenario?
questions.explanation: Colocation is one of the tools and techniques used in the Develop Team process. Colocation involves placing many or all of the most active project team members in the same physical location to enhance their ability to perform as a team. Keeping the team members in the same physical location helps create an effective, productive team.

questions.prompt: At a meeting during project initiation, the project manager and the project sponsor present the project charter to the key project stakeholders. Decisions should be made and actions assigned to some of the stakeholders. However, not all meeting participants completely agree with the project vision and objectives. What should the project manager do to gain the support of the stakeholders for the decisions and actions made during the meeting?
questions.option_a: Request the project sponsor force the decisions and actions on the stakeholders
questions.option_b: Try to reach a consensus among the stakeholders
questions.option_c: Do nothing; it is not important to have the support of the stakeholders during project
questions.option_d: Dismiss the meeting participants and terminate the project
questions.explanation: It is unlikely that all project stakeholders will always be 100% in agreement about everything on the project. Often, the stakeholders will have competing goals. The project manager should use their soft skills, such as interpersonal skills and the ability to manage people, to balance the conflicting and competing goals of the project stakeholders in order to achieve consensus. Consensus does not require that everybody is in complete agreement. While there could be a disagreement with some of the project goals, the stakeholders commit to and support the project decisions and actions. Therefore, to foster the stakeholders' commitment and increase the chance of project success, the project manager should try to reach consensus among the project stakeholders.

questions.prompt: All project deliverables are completed and approved by the customer. The sponsor congratulates the project manager for successful execution. What should the project manager do next?
questions.option_b: Transfer the product to the customer
questions.option_c: Take the team to a celebration party
questions.explanation: The question does not say that the project is complete. Rather, it says: "All planned work on your project has been completed." The project is formally complete only when all actions and activities listed in the Close Project or Phase process are completed. The closing actions and activities include: obtaining final acceptance of the project deliverables, obtaining financial, legal, and administrative closure, communicating the final project report to the stakeholders, collating lessons learned that were documented throughout the project, archiving project documents, and transferring the ownership of the project deliverables to the customer or the assigned stakeholders in accordance with the project management plan.

questions.prompt: You are planning a project to develop a new video game. The project will consist of three releases, five iterations per release. The first release will provide the must-have features required for a minimum viable product with additional features added after each subsequent release. The customer wants to know which features will be available at the end of each iteration. What is the best tool or technique to use in this situation?
questions.explanation: Agile release planning provides a high-level summary timeline of the release schedule based on the product roadmap and the product vision for the product's evolution. Since features represent value to the customer, the timeline provides a more easily understood project schedule as it defines which features will be available at the end of each iteration, which is precisely the depth of information the customer is looking for.

questions.prompt: A senior executive shares her opinion with the project manager that the production is too labor-intensive, such that profit margins are shrinking. The company must reduce labor costs significantly through automation if it is to survive. What is the project manager’s best course of action in this situation?
questions.option_b: Do nothing until a project charter has been approved
questions.option_c: Suggest that the senior executive initiate a project
questions.option_d: Ask to be designated as the project sponsor and begin project initiation
questions.explanation: In this scenario, a production line needs to be updated to reduce labor costs for the long-term viability of the company. Thus, the current production process must be transformed. Projects drive change in organizations. From a business perspective, a project is aimed at moving an organization from one state to another state to achieve a specific objective. Before the project begins, the organization is commonly referred to as being in the current state. The desired result of the change driven by the project is described as the future state. In this case, a project should be initiated to transform the organization from the current state to the desired future state. Although a project manager cannot initiate a project, a project manager can make suggestions as to how the organizational objectives can be accomplished. Therefore, of the available choices, suggesting that the senior executive initiate a project is the best course of action in this situation.

questions.prompt: You are managing a state-of-the-art technology design project. You have just implemented an approved change request that required crashing with new resources. This requires all of the following to be updated except:
questions.explanation: Crashing is a schedule compression technique that involves adding resources to project schedule activities so that they can divide up the work and therefore get it done in less time. Implementation of this technique usually requires additional costs to pay for the added resources (the new project team members in this case). Typically, the new team members need to be on-boarded, and the work among them should be divided up and coordinated. Frequently, this increases the risk of errors and the need for rework. However, the resulting work product, or the deliverables, that the team should complete remains unchanged. Therefore, the project scope is unlikely to change when crashing is applied. Since the question is looking for an exception, project scope is the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: As a new project manager, you have kept your project on track by relying on control and focusing on the bottom line. Although the project team is doing what has been asked of them, they feel that they are being micromanaged and lack the autonomy to make independent decisions for the betterment of the project. How might you improve the performance of the team?
questions.option_a: Become a more inspirational leader by improving your management skills
questions.option_b: Ask the project team to focus more on systems and structure
questions.option_c: Develop leadership skills to inspire trust and focus more on the horizon
questions.option_d: Focus your attention more on operational issues and problem-solving
questions.explanation: The question describes a situation where you, as the project manager, are relying exclusively on management to keep the project on track. The consequence of this leadership style is that the project team lacks the autonomy to perform optimally. Developing leadership skills and focusing more on the horizon will inspire trust and improve the performance of the team. All the incorrect answer choices are related to emphasizing management over leadership, which will be counterproductive in this situation. Therefore, of the available options, developing leadership skills to inspire trust and focusing more on the horizon is the best approach to improve team performance.

questions.prompt: You are managing a process improvement project and have interviewed different stakeholders over the past few weeks to understand the various suppliers, products supplied, manufacturing steps, and inputs and outputs. You are now in a very good position to start analyzing the processes. What should you do next?
questions.option_c: Use control charts to analyze the processes
questions.explanation: According to the scenario provided, you are performing the Plan Quality Management process. You have obtained enough information regarding your customer's processes to enable you to start analyzing them, and the next step is to visually document the processes using a data representation tool, such as a flowchart. The SIPOC (suppliers, inputs, processes, outputs, customers) model is an example of a flowchart that is specifically designed for analyzing suppliers and process interactions in a manufacturing environment and, of the choices provided, is the best tool to use.

questions.prompt: A project manager invited several vendors to a pre-bid conference. After a private conversation with one of the vendors who had an inquiry about a new mandatory government requirement, the project manager sent an email to the vendor to address the question. The project manager realizes that the requirement should have been included in the project scope baseline and the statement of work (SOW). What should the project manager do in this situation?
questions.option_a: No further action is needed as the vendor's question have been addressed
questions.option_b: Update the statement of work and the scope baseline, and communicate the changes to all
questions.option_c: Update the statement of work and the scope baseline, and hold another pre-bid conference
questions.option_d: Update the SOW, submit a change request to update the scope baseline, and communicate
questions.explanation: According to the scenario, the project manager is performing the Conduct Procurements process. A bidder conference (also called a contractor conference, vendor conference, and a pre-bid conference) is one of the tools and techniques used as part of this process. The project manager should be fair to all vendors and abide by the value of fairness in the PMI Code of Ethics and Professional Conduct. Project managers should constantly re-examine their impartiality and objectivity, taking corrective action as needed. The project manager, in this scenario, should provide equal access to information to those who are authorized to have that information. The missed requirement should also be added to the project scope baseline, which requires the Perform Integrated Change Control process to be carried out, including an approved change request. Note, a procurement SOW can be revised as required as it moves through the procurement process until incorporated into a signed agreement. After that, if the SOW requires an update, an approved changed request is needed to modify it as it is part of the agreement. While this point is not covered by the correct answer choice, updating the SOW, submitting a change request to update the scope baseline, and communicating the changes to all vendors is still the best answer of those provided.

questions.prompt: You are leading a project with a critical time constraint and cannot afford any project delays. An argument between two project team members turns into a shouting match. The team members draw you into their argument on selecting a report format. What is your best course of action?
questions.option_a: Tell the team members to figure it out for themselves
questions.option_b: Look for an approach that would completely satisfy both team members
questions.option_c: Request the team members to go back to work to avoid any further delays
questions.option_d: Select the report format that you think is best and make the decision
questions.explanation: Your success in managing a project and your team often depends on your ability to resolve conflict appropriately and promptly. You should choose the most appropriate conflict resolution method based on factors such as the importance and the intensity of the conflict and timelines. According to the scenario, you need to address the dispute quickly so that the team members can return to work without further delay. The quickest way to resolve the conflict in this situation, and, therefore, your best course of action, is to use the force/direct method by selecting an approach that you think is the best and make the decision. Note, the collaborative or problem-solving approach, which incorporates multiple viewpoints and insights from different perspectives, is ideal to resolve conflicts. However, such an approach is not always possible. Project managers should use their interpersonal and team skills and situational judgment to determine how best to handle a conflict.

questions.prompt: A project manager needs to use a unique, new technology on a project, so there are numerous unknowns for the project, which will affect project planning. The project manager is currently estimating the costs for the project and needs to be as accurate and detailed as possible. What tool or technique is best for the project manager to use in this situation?
questions.explanation: The question suggests that the Estimate Costs process is underway. Bottom-up, analogous, and parametric are all estimating techniques that may be used during the Estimate Costs process. Both analogous and parametric estimating rely on historical data which is not available for a project with a product that is new and unique as described in the question. Bottom-up estimating is a method of estimating project cost by aggregating the estimates of the lower-level components of the work breakdown structure (WBS). Therefore, without historical data, the bottom-up estimating technique is the best choice.

questions.prompt: A new product development project went exceptionally well, finishing ahead of schedule and under budget. The project manager is just starting project closure and wants to identify the factors that led to the project's success so that the results can be replicated for future projects. What is the best course of action for the project manager?
questions.explanation: It is most likely that a combination of factors was responsible for the remarkable success of the project. The project manager must try to understand if and how multiple actions contributed to the project’s overall success. Regression analysis is an analytical technique where a series of input variables are examined in relation to their corresponding output results in order to develop a mathematical or statistical relationship. For project closure, regression analysis is a technique that analyzes the interrelationships between different project variables that contributed to the project outcomes, which can be used to improve performance on future projects. Therefore, among the available options, performing a regression analysis is the best course of action.

questions.prompt: A project manager has yet to be assigned to a project. The project sponsor asked a group from the project management office (PMO) to get together and discuss the high-level risks of the project and the overall project risk for inclusion in the project charter. After the meeting, the sponsor receives feedback that two of the participants dominated the meeting and others did not have the opportunity to provide their insights. How might this situation have been avoided?
questions.option_a: Delaying project initiation until a project manager was named
questions.option_b: Ensuring that someone in the group was skilled in meeting management
questions.option_c: Designating someone with strong conflict management skills to lead the meeting
questions.option_d: Assigning a skilled facilitator to lead the meeting
questions.explanation: The question suggests that the Develop Project Charter is being performed by the project sponsor. Interpersonal and team skills which may support this process include conflict management, facilitation, and meeting management; all of which are offered as answer choices. The question describes a situation where not all of the participants of the group event had an opportunity to provide their insights. Facilitation is the ability to effectively guide a group event to a successful decision, solution, or conclusion. A facilitator ensures that there is effective participation, that participants achieve a mutual understanding, and that all contributions are considered. Therefore, of the available options, assigning a skilled facilitator to lead the meeting is the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: You are leading a software development project that is running behind schedule. The project team was so inspired and motivated by your vision for the project, a truly exceptional product is being developed, but numerous unaddressed operational issues have delayed the project. What might you have done differently to have kept the project on schedule?
questions.option_a: Relied exclusively on management skills in handling the project
questions.option_c: Struck the right balance between management and leadership
questions.option_d: Selected a more detail-oriented project team
questions.explanation: The question implies that you, as the project manager, have overemphasized leadership at the expense of management. Although you were highly effective at inspiring and motivating the project team to create a truly exceptional product, operational aspects of the project have not been addressed causing the project delays. Project managers need to employ both leadership and management in order to be successful. A project manager must strike the right balance between management and leadership to be successful. Had you focused more on management and the operational aspects of the project, you may have been able to avoid the delays and kept the project on schedule.

questions.prompt: The project cost baseline was revised and updated. What was the most likely reason for this revision?
questions.option_a: Implementation of a planned risk response
questions.option_d: The CPI was outside of control limits
questions.explanation: The project cost baseline is the approved version of the time-phased project budget, excluding any management reserves, which can be changed only through formal change control procedures. It is used as a basis for comparison to actual results. If the baseline is constantly readjusted, then it would be difficult to compare plans to actual results. Hence a revision to the cost baseline would most likely be due to an authorized change.

questions.prompt: Your project is nearing completion. Deadlines are extremely tight. Product testing has been moved up a day to align with resource constraints. Many minor issues are detected by testing; errors which could pose serious delays. What is your best action?
questions.option_a: Speed up testing to meet the completion date and hand off to the quality team
questions.option_b: Halt further testing until the identified issues are fully resolved
questions.option_c: Complete a Pareto chart and triage the issues with the project team
questions.option_d: Inform the customer that the product has issues and assure that everything is to be
questions.explanation: Product testing is typically performed as part of the Control Quality process. Testing is an organized and constructed investigation conducted to provide objective information about the quality of the product or service under test in accordance with the project requirements. The primary goal of product testing is to uncover defects. Once the defects are found, a Pareto chart can present the project team with the frequency of the defects’ appearance per their source and help the team prioritize the sources that cause the greatest number of defects. Addressing these sources would likely reduce the risk of having further delays described in the scenario. While the topic of this question is only briefly covered in the PMBOK® Guide, the Project Management Professional (PMP)® Examination Content Outline, June 2015, specifies knowledge and skills with which PMP aspirants are expected to be familiar. Various quality control tools and techniques, such as Pareto charts, are among these topics.

questions.prompt: You are looking for the information that includes the analysis of the probability of successfully completing the project. This analysis takes into account the legal, economic, technological, scheduling, and other factors when calculated. Which of the following would provide the information you seek?
questions.explanation: You are looking for documentation that includes the analysis of the ability to complete the project successfully. The feasibility study is the economic evaluation of the potential benefits of and the justification for the project. This information is documented in the project business case. The feasibility study, which establishes the validity of the benefits of completing the project, also serves as the basis for the authorization of the project and project management activities.

questions.prompt: An internal project is almost complete. The project manager wants to have a final meeting with the sponsor to discuss how to maximize the value of the deliverables after the project’s deliverable is deployed. What does the project manager use to back up the conversation with the sponsor?
questions.explanation: The benefits management plan is the documented explanation defining the processes for creating, maximizing, and sustaining the benefits provided by a project. The benefits management plan defines how the deliverables should be maintained, so the deliverables can provide the highest benefit after they are delivered and not under the purview of the project. The incorrect answer choices are not likely to have post-deployment information. Therefore, of the choices provided, the benefits management plan is the best response.

questions.prompt: You are managing a large manufacturing pilot project with extensive and highly detailed quality requirements. During planning, it is determined that there will be a high volume of quality control measurements being captured by a fully automated system. Which of the following would you likely use as a guideline on how and where this data be stored and processed?
questions.explanation: According to the scenario, a high volume of quality control measurements will be captured. This implies the project will be in the Control Quality process. Most organizations with complex quality requirements implement an automated, enterprise-level quality management system. This is the organizational framework whose structure provides the policies, processes, procedures, and resources required to implement the quality management program. The typical project quality management plan should be compatible with the organization’s quality management system. This system may include, for example, a description of a database component that will continuously store and process quality control measurements collected during carrying out the Control Quality process.

questions.prompt: You are managing a web and mobile software application development project using an agile approach, planning an early iteration of work. In the table shown, you have a list of risk user stories to add to the risk-adjusted product backlog. Which of the following would not be among the top three risks?
questions.option_a: Compatibility issues with beta Windows Phone
questions.option_b: Compatibility issues with beta Apple iOS
questions.option_c: Integration issues with beta United release
questions.option_d: Compatibility issues with beta Android OS
questions.explanation: Expected Monetary Value is the expected financial loss or gain associated with project risks. It is the product of a risk's probability and impact (EMV=P*I). To rank the risks, you would find the EMV for each risk. The risks with the highest EMV have the highest priority to address first. In the table below, the EMV for each risk is calculated in the last column. In this case, the two risks that would not be among the top three are "Integration issues with the beta Sabre release" and "Integration issues with the beta United release." Therefore, the answer is "Integration issues with the beta United release."

questions.prompt: A project manager has been informed that an identified risk comes from a questionable source. After further investigation, the project manager realizes that the risk was based on an article where the author never cited any sources, has no experience in the industry, and has been contradicted by credible industry experts. The risk will have to be reassessed. What risk analysis technique did the project manager use?
questions.explanation: This is an example of risk data quality assessment – a data analysis technique used as part of the Perform Qualitative Risk Analysis process. The project manager is evaluating the degree to which the risk data is accurate and credible, specifically by assessing the quality of the magazine article as a data source. Since the use of low-quality risk data can lead to a qualitative risk analysis that is of little value to the project, the project manager has realized that it may be necessary to gather better data and reassess the risk.

questions.prompt: You are managing a project to relocate a historic building to a new property that is a safe distance from the rising river basin. You have just finished developing your cost estimate, and are preparing the supporting documentation to accompany it. What should be included in this documentation?
questions.explanation: Based on the scenario described, your project is in planning, and you are performing the Estimate Costs process. One of the outputs from the Estimate Costs process is the basis of estimates. It provides a clear and complete understanding of how the cost estimate was derived and is supporting documentation for the cost estimate. Also included in the supporting details, is documentation of all assumptions made, any known constraints, and an indication of the range of possible estimates (e.g., $100,000 +/- 10%) to show the cost is within a range of values.

questions.prompt: As a project manager, you are leading the effort to construct the cellular towers. Another project manager is in charge of building the data center, and yet another project manager is heading up the development of retail distribution. All three of the projects are being managed in a coordinated manner. For which of the following might you be responsible?
questions.option_a: Assuring benefits realization from the program and component projects
questions.option_b: Capturing lessons learned, which may benefit other program components
questions.option_d: Managing change requests within a shared governance framework
questions.explanation: Program components consist of individual projects that are being managed in a coordinated manner. In this case, the three individual projects are components of a program. Program management focuses on the interdependencies between projects to determine the optimal approach for managing them. All the incorrect answer choices represent interdependencies, which must be addressed at the program rather than the project level. Important lessons learned should be captured in the lessons learned register and then transferred to the lessons learned repository. These lessons learned may improve the performance of the current project, other component projects, or future projects. However, the identification and recording of lessons learned is the responsibility of each project manager. Therefore, of the available choices, capturing lessons learned which may benefit other program components is the best answer.

questions.prompt: The project is developing an update to a software tool. There have been a series of changes made to the product, and more changes are expected. Correspondingly, engineers on the team are modifying documentation frequently to reflect the changes. What is the best course of action to ensure engineers are working on the most current version of documentation?
questions.option_a: Audit the documentation daily to ensure the correctness of the information
questions.option_b: Require the requirements documentation to have the current date on the header
questions.option_c: Assign an accountable engineer to validate all changes and update project plans
questions.option_d: Maintain a configuration management system and communicate appropriately
questions.explanation: In this scenario, the project team needs assurance that they are working with the most current documentation. With many changes occurring – potentially because they are managing the product scope in an agile manner – it is important that the engineers are working off the correct documentation. A configuration management system assists the project team and other stakeholders to track and monitor changes and versions to project artifacts. Having a single source and a collection of procedures for the source, the team is well informed about the correct documentation and can communicate to others when changes have been made. Hence the configuration management system is more ideal for them in this situation.

questions.prompt: Your procurement manager has asked you to compare the current schedule and cost performance of the installations against what was promised in the agreement with the vendor. What comparative process does the procurement manager refer to?
questions.explanation: Based on the scenario described, you are going to perform the Control Procurements process. Performance reviews are a type of data analysis tool used in that process. Performance reviews for contracts measure, compare, and analyze quality, resource, schedule, and cost performance against the agreement. This comparison includes identifying work packages that are ahead or behind schedule, over or under budget, or have resource or quality issues.

questions.prompt: A project manager is leading an infrastructure development project and is in the process of comparing actual overall project performance against the performance objectives described in the project management plan. Which of the following activities is the project manager least likely to conduct during this process?
questions.option_a: Analyzing individuals or groups affected by the project
questions.option_b: Checking the status of individual project risks
questions.option_c: Providing forecasts to update the current cost and schedule information
questions.option_d: Monitoring implementation of approved changes as they occur
questions.explanation: The question implies that the Monitor and Control Project Work is being carried out. Monitor and Control Project Work is the process of tracking, reviewing, and reporting the overall progress to meet the performance objectives defined in the project management plan. All of the incorrect answer choices are activities which are associated with the Monitor and Control Project Work process. Individuals or groups affected by the project is a partial definition of stakeholders. "Stakeholder analysis" is disguised by the correct answer choice. Stakeholder analysis is performed during the Identify Stakeholders process in order to develop the stakeholder register. Note, the question is asking to select the activity that is 'least' likely to be conducted during the Monitor and Control Project Work process. Performing stakeholder analysis is the 'least' likely activity to be conducted by the project manager in this scenario and is, therefore, the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: All the technical work on a project has been completed, and the product has been transitioned to a support team. What should the project manager do before the project can be closed?
questions.explanation: Since all the technical work has been completed, and the product has been transitioned to the support group, one may assume the project is in the Close Project or Phase process. When closing a project, the project manager reviews the project management plan to ensure all project work is completed, and that the project has met its objectives. Measuring stakeholder satisfaction is one of the activities which is necessary to be performed before the administrative closure of the project can take place.

questions.prompt: At this point in the project, the system analysis, design, and development should have been completed. However, according to the table shown below, system development is only 50% complete, and the actual cost of system analysis is higher than planned. You believe that remaining project work will be influenced by these low cost and schedule performance values. Which of the formulas should be used to determine estimate at completion (EAC)?
questions.explanation: The table shows that cost performance is poor as the actual costs are greater than planned values for completed and partially completed work. In addition, since the 3rd task system development is only 50% complete instead of being finished according to the plan, the schedule performance is poor. The scenario indicates that these current cost and schedule performance will influence the remainder of the project work. In other words, both CPI and SPI affect the estimate at completion (EAC). Therefore, one should use the formula that considers both of these indices, i.e., EAC = AC + [(BAC - EV) / (CPI x SPI)].

questions.prompt: As the project manager, you are in the process of developing a draft of a document which authorizes a new software development project. You have reviewed the agreements and both business documents. What is also important to do during this process?
questions.option_a: Identify stakeholders and prepare the stakeholder register
questions.option_b: Study the governance functions and processes to provide guidance and decision making
questions.option_c: Update the business documents to correct any errors
questions.explanation: The scenario describes a project manager who is developing a draft of the project charter. One of the typical activities performed while developing the charter is to review organizational process assets (OPAs), which may influence this process. One of the OPAs is the project governance framework which often provides information about the governance functions, processes, that are important when making decisions that are relevant to the project charter. One may argue that a stakeholder register can be created at the same time the project charter is developed and approved. While it's true, the question is asking to select the answer choice that represents an action performed 'during this process'. Since the scenario describes the Develop Project Charter process, any action that is associated with 'this process' would be a better option than an action associated with 'another process', even if that process can be done in parallel with the one described in the scenario. Therefore, as we always recommend selecting the best answer of those provided, studying the governance functions and processes to provide guidance and decision making is a better answer choice than identifying stakeholders and preparing the stakeholder register since the former is associated with the inputs to the Develop Project Charter process while the latter is not even part of this process.

questions.prompt: A project manager has been asked to create a document that formally authorizes a project to use ground-penetrating radar to map geological conditions along a proposed crude oil pipeline route. The performing organization has agreed to share the data with a local university. The project manager has reviewed the business documents and considered any relevant external environmental factors to create the requested document. What might the project manager do next?
questions.option_a: Research the stakeholder engagement plan for any information to be included in the
questions.option_b: Ensure any relevant information from the memorandum of understanding (MOU) is
questions.option_c: Review the business case for any pertinent information
questions.option_d: Look to see if a benefits management plan was previously created
questions.explanation: The question implies that the project manager is developing the project charter. The project charter is a document issued by the project initiator or sponsor that formally authorizes the existence of a project. The question indicates that the project manager has already included the business documents as inputs into the process of developing the project charter as well as considered any external environmental factors. The business documents include the business case and benefits management plan. Agreements are also inputs into the Develop Project Charter process, and a memorandum of understanding (MOU) is an example of an agreement. Additionally, the agreement to share data with the university in this scenario may be documented as an MOU. Note that organizational process assets (OPA) are also considered inputs into this process but were not mentioned in the scenario or offered as an answer choice. Therefore, of the available options, ensuring that any relevant information from the memorandum of understanding (MOU) is included in the document is the best answer.

questions.prompt: When estimating resources for your project, you have determined that the company does not have enough developers to complete the required work. You can either hire additional developers as permanent employees or bring temporary contractors. The make-or-buy analysis resulted in a return on investment (ROI) of 25% if the project work is completed by adding permanent employees and an ROI of 20% if you bring in contractors. You decide to bring in the contractors. Which of the following was the most likely reason for this decision?
questions.option_a: To avoid expanding permanent employment obligations
questions.option_b: To reduce the project costs by using less expensive contractors
questions.option_c: To protect confidential information and intellectual property
questions.option_d: To complete the project faster by shortening its duration by 5%
questions.explanation: In this scenario, the ROI for using internal resources was estimated to be 25% versus outsourcing with an ROI of 20%. Based on these estimates, it is more profitable to hire the additional developers compared with using contractors to complete the project work. Based on profitability alone, the logical decision is to hire the additional developers and conduct the project internally. However, other considerations beyond profitability should be taken into account. One important consideration includes the financial obligation for providing employment to the newly hired developers after the project is concluded. If the performing organization feels that they will not need additional developers after the current project is complete, then it may make sense to use contractors instead of hiring developers even though the project will be less profitable as a result. Therefore, of the available options, the most likely reason to use contractors in this scenario is to avoid expanding permanent employment obligations.

questions.prompt: As a project progresses, existing knowledge is used, and new knowledge is created to achieve the project's objectives and contribute to organizational learning. Which of the following enterprise environmental factors would contribute to this process?
questions.explanation: Using existing knowledge and creating new knowledge to achieve the project's objectives and contribute to organizational learning is the definition of the Manage Project Knowledge process. The question is asking to find an enterprise environmental factor (EEF) which contributes (in other words, serves as an input) to this process. Organizational, stakeholder, and customer culture is an example of the EEFs that are among the inputs to the Manage Project Knowledge process. The culture of trusting working relationships and a no-blame culture is particularly important in managing knowledge on a project as it allows people to feel comfortable sharing information. The incorrect answer choices, while being inputs to the process, can be eliminated simply because none of them is considered an EEF.

questions.prompt: You are managing a project to develop recreational facilities for a community. You have internal and external groups of stakeholders. You would like to send project performance-related information to internal stakeholders only, but deliverable status information to both internal and external stakeholders. Which of the following processes would you use to actively send this information?
questions.explanation: One may argue that the correct answer should be Manage Communications. However, this option is not listed among the answer choices. Certainly, Project Communications Management and Project Stakeholder Management are two very closely correlated Knowledge Areas. Manage Communications is the process of "ensuring timely and appropriate collection, creation, distribution, storage, retrieval, management, monitoring, and the ultimate disposition of project information." One of the outputs from this process is the actual project communications, which include the performance reports, the status of deliverables, schedule progress, the cost incurred, presentations, and other information required by stakeholders which is what described in the scenario. Manage Stakeholder Engagement is the process of "communicating and working with stakeholders to meet their needs and expectations, address issues, and foster appropriate stakeholder involvement." However, the output artifacts for this process only include change requests and updates to the project management plan and project documents. Eventually, we can say that Manage Communications process is the means by which Manage Stakeholder Engagement process is carried out. Now let us for a moment forget what we have just written. Let’s pretend we are taking the actual PMP exam. What choice would you pick? There is no Manage Communications as one of the answer choices. There is only Manage Stakeholder Engagement and three other choices which are likely incorrect (we assume you do not argue over them). The point we are trying to make here is that on the real PMP exam the questions and the answers could be ambiguous. They may have no any specific clarifications or clear definitions. There may be situations where more than one (or even all) choices will seem to be correct or, on the other hand, incorrect answers. Therefore while taking the exam, it is recommended to make reasonable assumptions based on all 'available' information in the qu

questions.prompt: A project requires consultant services. How much and for how long these services are needed is not known. The project needs to procure these services quickly. Which contract type should be used?
questions.option_d: Fixed fee with economic price adjustments
questions.explanation: The details of the services needed are not fully determined. Since little is known about how much time is needed for the consultant services, then a more logical choice of contract would be to use one that only pays for the time and work needed. Additionally, the materials and equipment needed are less known in this situation. Defining a contract that pays a fixed fee for open-ended services is too risky. Similarly, a cost reimbursable for a service with unknown boundaries could present its own risks – depending on how the contract was written and its terms and conditions. Ergo, in this situation, the best contract type would be the time and material contract.

questions.prompt: The following table contains information about the duration, in days, of project activities and their predecessor activity for a project. What is the total float of activity G?
questions.explanation: Total float refers to the maximum amount of time an activity can be delayed without pushing out the project end date. If an activity is on the critical path, the total float of the activity is zero. The first step to correctly answer this question is to determine the activity paths and their durations for the project. This requires the creation of a project network diagram using the precedence diagramming method (PDM) where you map the values from the data table included in the question. Following is the network diagram for this question's scenario. Once you have created the network diagram, the next step is to determine the specific paths that exist for the activities on the project. This requires following each path that is mapped and determining the duration of each path by adding up the number of days for each activity on that specific path. The longest path through the project identifies the shortest possible length of the project and is, therefore, the critical path. Following is each path through the project and its duration: Start-A-B-C-G-Finish = 10 days Start-D-E-F-G-Finish = 17 days - the critical path because it has the longest length Start-H-I-Finish = 6 days Activity G is on two paths, Start-A-B-C-G-Finish and Start-D-E-F-G-Finish. As a rule, if an activity is present on more than one path, and one of the paths is the critical path, the total float for the activity will always be zero because delaying any activity on the critical path will delay the project. Since activity G is on the critical path, its total float has to be zero.

questions.prompt: You are leading a project to bring a new automotive battery to market. You have just completed an initial run of the production line to test the manufacturing process in preparation for mass production. The knowledge gained from the test run will be used to improve performance during mass production. During the test run, the conveyer belt transporting a subassembly jammed several times for unknown reasons. What should you do next?
questions.option_a: Update the issue log with the resolution
questions.option_c: Record the information in the lessons learned register
questions.option_d: Begin mass production as soon as possible due to the aggressive timeline
questions.explanation: The question suggests that you, as a project manager, are conducting a test run of the manufacturing process to gain lessons learned to be used later in the project during mass production. However, the problem encountered during the test run does not provide any useful information that can be applied later in the project. The problem must first be analyzed to understand the cause of the issue and then determine what corrective actions must be taken to prevent the problem from recurring. A description of the problem, as well as steps necessary to prevent the problem from recurring, can then be recorded in the lessons learned register. A why-why diagram is also known as a cause-and-effect diagram, a fishbone diagram, or Ishikawa diagram. This type of diagram breaks down the causes of the problem statement identified into discrete branches, helping to identify the main or root cause of the problem. This identification of the root cause of the problem described by the question is a necessary first step in resolving the issue and preventing the recurrence during mass production. Therefore, of the available choices, you should first develop a why-why diagram to analyze the problem before the knowledge gained can be documented in the lessons learned register.

questions.prompt: For a project, strict quality specifications must be followed, and all project team members were required to read and sign off on the quality management plan, acknowledging complete understanding of the specifications. The project manager suspects that one or more of the project's primary material vendors is not supplying parts that meet project quality specifications. What should the project manager do first?
questions.option_a: Tell the vendor to replace the parts
questions.option_c: Discuss the issue with the sponsor
questions.option_d: Terminate the contract with the vendor
questions.explanation: An aspirational standard of professional conduct in project management is that project managers must earnestly seek to understand the truth. In this scenario, the project manager only suspects that parts supplied by the vendor or vendors are not meeting the project's quality specifications, but she does not know this for sure. The best way to determine this is to perform a procurement performance review for the vendors. Performance reviews measure, compare, and analyze quality, resource, schedule, and cost performance against the agreement. The results of this procurement performance review will indicate whether or not the vendors are supplying parts that meet project quality specifications specified in the agreement, and if any further action is necessary on the project manager's part.

questions.prompt: A junior project manager wants to understand the inputs, the outputs, the tools and techniques (ITTOs) which will be helpful in developing the change management plan. However, when she reviews the PMBOK® Guide, she cannot find the ITTOs associated with this process. What advice might an experienced project manager provide to the junior project manager?
questions.option_a: The ITTOs are listed in the PMBOK® Guide under the Plan Change Management process
questions.option_b: Review the ITTOs associated with the Develop Project Management Plan process
questions.option_c: The appropriate ITTOs will be listed with the Perform Integrated Change Control process
questions.option_d: The project management office (PMO) provides the change management plan
questions.explanation: The change management plan is a component of the project management plan that establishes the change control board, documents the extent of its authority, and describes how the change control system will be implemented. Although there is no specific project management process for the creation of the change management plan, it is created as part of the Develop Project Management Plan process. Therefore, the best advice the experienced project manager may provide to the junior project manager who wants to develop the change management plan is to review the ITTOs associated with the Develop Project Management Plan process.

questions.prompt: A change request on a project you are leading has just been approved. You communicate the tasks required to implement the change to the project team. A team member suggests an alternative solution that departs from the approved change request but which reduces the time and cost of implementing the change and still meets the scope and quality requirements. What should you do?
questions.option_a: Reject the suggestion as gold plating and implement the exact steps as specified in the
questions.option_b: Implement the alternative suggestion and update the relevant components of the project
questions.option_c: Refer to the change management plan for criteria whether the suggestion requires the
questions.option_d: Refer to the configuration management plan for guidance on whether the content of the
questions.explanation: Approved change requests are outputs of the Perform Integrated Change Control process, and inputs to the Direct and Manage Project Work process. The change management plan provides for a responsible individual or party to review, evaluate, approve, defer, or reject change requests. Either this plan or organizational procedures may authorize the project manager or sponsor as the responsible party, or may formally charter a group to serve as the change control board. The plan may also grant authority for the project manager to approve change requests with a certain level of impact on the scope, schedule, budget, or quality or other constraints before involving the sponsor or the change control board (if it exists). Given an approved change request, the project manager has responsibility for implementing it as specified. In this scenario, however, a suggestion that meets the project scope and quality requirements but saves time and money in implementation should not be ignored. The change management plan should guide the project manager to evaluate whether the effects of the suggestion are significant enough (or unknown enough) to merit a new change request to be evaluated. Perhaps the project manager has the authority to adjust the tactical approach during implementation and may document the updates as needed in the change log. Of the choices available, referring first to the change management plan before implementing the suggestion or before submitting a revised change request is the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: A fellow project manager comes to you complaining about the amount of scope creep that she is experiencing in her project. Which of the following is the most probable reason for her difficulties?
questions.option_a: Lack of historical records from past projects
questions.option_c: Lack of project exclusions in the scope statement
questions.explanation: The degree and level of detail of the scope statement determine how well the scope can be controlled. The scope statement describes the project's deliverables in detail. Explicitly stating what is out of scope, particularly where stakeholders requested scope and it was not approved, helps manage stakeholders, and can reduce scope creep. Therefore, the scope creep mentioned in this scenario is probably due to lack of project exclusions in the scope definition and is the best answer.

questions.prompt: A project manager is leading a retrospective for a project that is running behind schedule and over budget. Feedback from the meeting suggests that the project manager was too focused on problem-solving and operational issues and did not effectively inspire and motivate the project team. What could the project manager have done differently to have been more successful?
questions.option_a: Focused more on the bottom line
questions.option_b: Spent more time developing the project team and providing a vision for project success
questions.option_c: Directed the team using positional power
questions.option_d: Emphasized the administrative aspects of the project
questions.explanation: The question implies that the project manager has been relying too heavily on managing the project rather than leading the project team. Project managers need to employ both leadership and management and strike the right balance between them to be successful. Developing the project team and providing a vision for success represents an emphasis on leadership. All of the incorrect answer choices are examples of greater management rather than leadership. Therefore, of the available options, the project manager could have spent more time developing the project team and providing a vision for project success.

questions.prompt: Due to an increase in actual costs that have not been accounted for in the original budget, the project manager submits a change request to update the cost baseline. To support the request, the project manager compiles all of the costs that have been authorized, incurred, invoiced, and paid. What has the project manager compiled?
questions.explanation: Work performance data are the raw observations and measurements identified during activities performed to carry out the project work. In the scenario, the project manager compiles all of the costs, which have been authorized, incurred, invoiced, and paid. Those are best described as the work performance data. Only after these raw data have been analyzed, will it become work performance information. Therefore, what the project manager has compiled was the work performance data.

questions.prompt: Undertaking a project requires a business case to justify whether the project is worth the investment. Which is the best example of a business case?
questions.option_a: The operational budget is greater than previous years, and we must allocate those funds
questions.option_b: Technology is constantly changing, and we must learn the latest tools
questions.option_c: Carbon emissions can be reduced with the installation of filters on all current machines,
questions.option_d: Projects like this have been done in the past with great success, and we must have more
questions.explanation: A business case is a reason and justification for a business to undertake and fund a project. The

questions.prompt: In the absence of the project sponsor, you are leading a meeting with key stakeholders to aid in the development of the project charter. At the beginning of the meeting, a stakeholder stalls the meeting insisting on discussing an omission in the business case. With the meeting at an impasse, what is your best course of action?
questions.option_a: Update the business case as necessary and proceed with the meeting
questions.option_b: Postpone further discussion on the omission and move on to other items on the agenda
questions.option_c: Submit a change request to document the omission in the project charter
questions.option_d: Dismiss the participants, and reschedule the meeting once the sponsor is available
questions.explanation: The question indicates that the Develop Project Charter process is underway. Interpersonal and team skills including conflict management are among the tools and techniques associated with this process. Withdraw/avoid is a conflict resolution technique which may include postponing the issue to be better prepared or to be resolved by others. In this scenario, the project sponsor is not in attendance at the meeting, but only the project sponsor has the responsibility for the creation and maintenance of the business case. With the meeting at an impasse, the best course of action is to postpone further discussion on the omission raised by the stakeholder and move on to other items on the agenda until the project sponsor can be engaged to render a decision for the modification of the business case.

questions.prompt: You have been commissioned by your company to propose a cost-efficient strategy for a new leading-edge server farm. While researching options, you identify the power source to be a large expense of maintaining servers. You narrow the power source choices down to four options with the following attributes: ● Chemical energy has an investment of $1,000,000 and NPV of $200,000 ● Solar energy has an investment of $1,500,000, a payback period of 3 years, and NPV of $350,000 ● Wind energy has an investment of $2,000,000, a payback period of 3 years, and NPV of $100,000 ● Thermal energy has an investment of $1,800,000 and NPV of $175,000 Which of these power options should you propose based on the net present value (NPV)?
questions.explanation: The four options include extraneous information, namely the investment amount (which is already factored into the NPV calculation), and the payback period (which is another type of project selection technique that can be used by itself or along with NPV calculations). However, the question clearly asks to base the answer on the NPV. You should, therefore, have keyed in only on the term NPV when selecting the correct answer. The correct answer is 'solar energy,' since its NPV is $350,000, which is the largest of all the answer choices.

questions.prompt: As a project manager, you contracted with another company to develop a smartphone app. The company has demonstrated the completed app, and you have verified that it meets all technical and quality requirements. Overall, you are pleased with the performance of the seller and plan to use them again in the future. Now that the project deliverables are complete, who is responsible for declaring the formal closure of the procurement contract?
questions.option_a: The buyer is responsible for closing out the procurement contract.
questions.option_b: The seller is responsible for closing out the procurement contract.
questions.option_c: Both buyer and seller are mutually responsible for closing out the contract.
questions.option_d: A procurement contract is considered closed when the deliverables are accepted.
questions.explanation: The party that awards the contract is responsible for declaring its closure. The buyer, usually through its authorized administrator, provides the seller with the formal written notice that the contract has been completed. Procurement closure only occurs after the deliverables have been accepted and transferred, all claims are settled, and final payments have been made.

questions.prompt: You are in the execution stage of a corporate restructuring project in which the company's sales managers will assume responsibility for certain operational aspects of the business in addition to their role as sales leaders. The sales managers have repeatedly expressed concerns about the impact of the project on their roles, and you need their support for the project to be successful. What is the best course of action in this situation?
questions.option_a: Utilize conflict management skills to diffuse the conflict between the sales managers
questions.option_b: Send the sales managers a copy of the team charter
questions.option_d: Replace the sales managers as necessary to ensure the success of the project
questions.explanation: One of the trends and emerging practices for Project Stakeholder Management involves the concept of co-creation. Co-creation places greater emphasis on including the affected stakeholders on the team as a partner. Working in close collaboration with the stakeholders that are most affected by the work or outcomes of the project can build support for the project and improve the probability of a successful outcome. Of the available choices, the strategy that is most likely to be effective in addressing the concerns of the sales managers and building support for the project is to introduce the concept of co-creation with the sales managers.

questions.prompt: Both supportive and resistant stakeholders are influencing the project team and asking for unapproved changes. You are more concerned about the resistant stakeholders and want to determine if any unapproved features have been added to the project deliverables. What is your best approach?
questions.option_a: Perform the Integrated Change Control process
questions.option_b: Perform the Direct and Manage Project Work process
questions.explanation: The question implies that you, as the project manager, suspect that scope creep may be an issue for the project. Scope creep represents the uncontrolled expansion to product or project scope without adjustments to time, cost, and resources. The detection and prevention of scope creep may be addressed in the Control Scope process. Hence, The Control Scope process should be performed to detect any unapproved changes to the scope baseline. Once unauthorized changes are found, the Perform Integrated Change Control process can be implemented. Therefore, in this scenario, performing the Control Scope process is the best approach.

questions.prompt: You are identifying the specific actions that will be required to complete the project deliverables. As part of this process, you have created a project document which describes portions of work to be performed during the project. Each portion of work specified in the document has various details such as leads and lags, imposed dates, constraints, assumptions, as well as logical relationships. Which project document have you created?
questions.explanation: The question suggests that you are carrying out the Define Activities process. The project documents created during this process include the activity list, activity attributes, and milestone list. Activity attributes include activity codes, predecessor activities, successor activities, logical relationships, leads and lags, resource requirements, imposed dates, constraints, and assumptions. The description provided by the question indicates that the activity attributes have been created. Note, the question disguises the word ‘activities’ by providing its definition from the glossary of the PMBOK® Guide which reads, “Activity. A distinct, scheduled portion of work performed during the course of a project.”

questions.prompt: As the project manager acquires the aluminum for a project to construct a new airliner, a tariff is enacted which raises the cost by 30%. Although the overall project budget will not be affected, the existing contingency reserves are inadequate to cover the increased cost, and the project manager needs to access the management reserves. What should the project manager do next?
questions.option_a: Submit a change request to transfer the management reserves into the contingency reserves
questions.option_b: Submit a change request to transfer the management reserves into the cost baseline
questions.option_c: Access the management reserves since the project budget is not affected
questions.option_d: Request the project sponsor to provide additional funds for the project
questions.explanation: The question suggests that during the Acquire Resources process, a significant increase in material costs have exceeded the contingency reserves for the project and the project manager needs to access the management reserves. The management reserves are part of the project budget but are not included in the cost baseline. An approved change request is required to transfer the management reserves into the cost baseline. Even though the project budget will not be affected, any change to a baseline first requires an approved change request. Therefore, a change request first needs to be submitted and approved before the management reserves can be transferred to the cost baseline, and that is what the project manager should do next.

questions.prompt: To reduce potential conflict and increase productivity, the project manager creates a team charter with clear expectations of acceptable behavior and diligently enforces the terms of the charter. Despite these efforts, team conflict has become a significant issue and threatens the successful completion of the project. What could the project manager have done differently to avoid this situation?
questions.option_a: Have the human resources department provide a preapproved team charter
questions.option_b: Allow the project team to develop the team charter
questions.option_d: Create a team member of the month award
questions.explanation: The question describes a situation where the project manager developed, implemented, and enforced the team charter. The team charter works best when the team develops it or at least has an opportunity to contribute to it. Additionally, all project team members share responsibility for ensuring the rules documented in the team charter are followed. Of the available options, allowing the project team to develop the team charter provides the best way to avoid team conflict.

questions.prompt: In a meeting with a project stakeholder, the stakeholder explains why she has not been engaged with the project as expected. You meet with her in her office. You take notes, ask some clarifying questions, and demonstrate attentiveness through body language, and capturing her input. What interpersonal skill are you leveraging in this exchange?
questions.explanation: Active listening is a powerful interpersonal skill of project managers. It is an important tool and technique when monitoring stakeholder engagement. The ability to take an active role in listening to others and ensuring you have heard them properly and their message reduces miscommunication and misunderstandings. By listening intently and following up with clarification and discussion, you are taking an active role in listening and capturing the communication between you and the stakeholder.

questions.prompt: Where would you look to find what activities should be performed to close out contracts on your project?
questions.explanation: The question surrounds the Control Procurements process which is the process of managing procurement relationships; monitoring contract performance, and making changes and corrections as appropriate; and closing out contracts. The activities to be performed during this process are described in the procurement management plan. This plan is a component of the project management plan. Therefore, to understand what activities should be performed to close out contracts on your project, you should review the project management plan.

questions.prompt: You are drafting a project charter. In order to solicit the required feedback from each department, you are planning to invite all of the functional leaders to attend a meeting except for the director of human resources, since that position is currently vacant. What is your best course of action in preparation for the meeting?
questions.option_a: Schedule the meeting without human resources representation
questions.option_b: Invite a designated human resources representative to attend
questions.option_c: Delay the meeting until a new director of human resources is named
questions.explanation: The question indicates that the project charter is being developed, and feedback is required from each department, but the director of human resources position is currently vacant. The solution to this problem can be provided by effective meeting management, which is one of the interpersonal and team skills associated with the Develop Project Charter process. Meeting management includes preparing the agenda, ensuring that a representative for each key stakeholder group is invited, and preparing and sending the follow-up minutes and actions. Therefore, among the available options, the best course of action is to identify and invite a qualified individual to represent the human resources department at the meeting.

questions.prompt: The performing organization requires the use of a change control board (CCB) for all projects. An influential stakeholder has complained about his requested changes not getting implemented even when using the established change control procedures. What should the project manager do first?
questions.option_a: Record the information in the change log
questions.option_b: Capture the concern in the stakeholder register
questions.option_c: Investigate the issue on behalf of the stakeholder
questions.option_d: Add the stakeholder's complaint to the issue log
questions.explanation: In this scenario, a stakeholder has raised a concern which needs to be addressed. Any project or stakeholder concerns are documented in the issue log, as well as any assigned action items associated with managing the issue. The issue log will help the project manager effectively track and manage issues, ensuring that they are investigated and resolved. In this case, it is not known why the stakeholder's requests have not been implemented. Perhaps the change requests were denied, or they might be scheduled to be implemented later in the project. The issue will need to be investigated, resolved, and then communicated back to the stakeholder. Of the available choices, the stakeholder's issue should first be captured in the issue log to ensure it is tracked and monitored until it can be resolved. Note, an issue log is an input to the Manage Stakeholder Engagement process described in the scenario. Updates to the issue log are among the outputs from that process.

questions.prompt: Due to the complexity of a project, representatives from various departments of your company are invited to participate in seller proposal evaluation including Finance, Real Estate, Engineering, and Sales and Marketing. Based on this situation, what tool or technique are you using?
questions.explanation: The question describes the evaluation of seller proposals by a multi-disciplinary committee with expertise in different areas. Expert judgment is judgment provided based upon expertise in an application area, knowledge area, discipline, industry, etc., as appropriate for the activity being performed. Such expertise may be provided by any group or person with specialized education, knowledge, skill, experience, or training. The inclusion of representatives from various departments to evaluate seller proposals is an example of using expert judgment.

questions.prompt: Working for a company with an organizational structure type that is best described as organic or simple, you have been assigned a project to replace the computers at a retail store. All you need to complete the project on time is for the company's information technology (IT) specialist to load the required software, but this individual states that they are busy with another project. With the deadline now in jeopardy, what is your best course of action?
questions.option_a: Approve overtime for the IT specialist to complete the software update
questions.option_b: Direct the IT specialist to prioritize your project over the other project
questions.option_c: Escalate the issue to the owner
questions.option_d: Discuss the situation with the project management office (PMO)
questions.explanation: The question describes a company with an organizational structure type of organic or simple. In this environment, the project manager is better described as a project coordinator and has little or authority over the project. In this situation, the owner or operator controls the budget and project resources. Therefore, the only viable option for you, as the project manager, is to escalate the issue to the owner.

questions.prompt: The project team reports that they have finished creating the requirements management plan, the requirements documentation, and the requirements traceability matrix. The team informs that they are going to start working on the project work breakdown structure (WBS) next. What has the project team forgotten to do?
questions.explanation: In the scenario described, the project team has completed the requirements management plan, which is an output of the Plan Scope Management process. Additionally, the team has completed the requirements documentation and requirements traceability matrix, which are outputs of the Collect Requirements process. The next step in the Planning Process Group is the Define Scope process and the creation of the project scope statement. Before creating the WBS, the project scope must be well defined and documented in the project scope statement. The completed project scope statement sets the boundaries for the project by defining the work that is included in the project as well as the work excluded from the project. The project scope statement serves as a guide when creating the WBS and should be completed before work begins on the WBS. The project team wants to move on to creating the WBS, but they have forgotten that they need to create the project scope statement before the WBS can be created.

questions.prompt: A company needs to purchase a 3D printer for a project in the planning stage. The project manager assigns a project team member to be responsible for acquiring the 3D printer while two other team members will be trained on operation and maintenance and can then be consulted when needed. What tool or technique is most useful in documenting these responsibilities for future reference?
