questions.explanation: The question implies that the Plan Resource Management process is being carried out, and a responsibility assignment matrix (RAM) is one of the tools associated with this process. A RACI chart is a common type of RAM that uses responsible, accountable, consult, and inform statuses to define the involvement of stakeholders in project activities. In the scenario described, activities such as acquiring the 3D printer, training to operate and maintain the printer, etc., are shown in the left column, while the roles and responsibilities of the team members assigned to these activities are shown in rows. Therefore, a RACI chart is the best tool for documenting the responsibilities for future reference.

questions.prompt: The project manager has scheduled individual discussions with experienced project participants, stakeholders, and subject matter experts. The purpose of these discussions is to obtain a comprehensive list of individual project risks and sources of overall project risk. Which tool or technique is the project manager utilizing in this situation?
questions.explanation: The question states that the project is in the planning stage and implies that the project team is performing the project management process of Identify Risks. The interview is one of the tools and techniques used during the Identify Risks process. The purpose of interviews during the Identify Risks process is to obtain a comprehensive list of individual project risks and sources of overall project risk. The interview is the data gathering technique that is being utilized by the project manager.

questions.prompt: Some stakeholders are disappointed that not all of their needs were included in the final requirements documentation. A senior project manager suggests using a document that relates the planned capabilities with the business objective that each capability satisfies. This document would demonstrate how each requirement adds business value. To what type of document is the senior project manager referring?
questions.explanation: The RTM is a grid that links the product requirements to the business objectives. It provides a means to track requirements throughout the project lifecycle helping to ensure that requirements approved in the requirements documentation are delivered at the end of the project. Using an RTM will assist in ensuring and showing that each requirement adds business value by linking it to the business and project objectives. Each requirement has attributes associated with it that help to define the requirement further. One of the attributes that can be used is the owner of the requirement, who will manage it throughout the project. Based on the scenario described, the RTM is the document to which the senior project manager was referring.

questions.prompt: You took over a project in execution to build a new marine radar system. The project has proceeded smoothly until the quality inspection team reports that most of the high voltage test equipment is badly corroded and will need to be replaced. You remember your predecessor mentioning a surprisingly inexpensive solution for a similar problem earlier in the project. Where should you look for the details of this solution?
questions.explanation: According to the scenario, you are looking for a solution that helped resolve a similar issue earlier in the project. This implies you are carrying out the Manage Quality process. The issue log helps the project manager effectively track and manage issues, and it will typically include the details about issues encountered throughout the project. This may include the issue type, who raised it, date/time, description, priority, who the issue was assigned to, status, and the final solution. Therefore, assuming the previous project manager carried out the project using sound project management practices, the issue log is supposed to include the details of the solution you are looking for.

questions.prompt: You hold a video conference with the stakeholders and the development team to demonstrate the product increment completed during the last iteration. A stakeholder brings up a concern regarding the functionality of the application. To ensure you understand the concern, you are using active listening techniques. Which of the following quotes from the video conference illustrates the active listening skill of confirming understanding?
questions.option_a: "Thank you for bringing up that concern. Good catch."
questions.option_b: "When you said you couldn't tell if the application was doing anything, did you mean that the
questions.option_c: "It sounds like what you're looking for with the page transition is a loading animation to show that
questions.option_d: "We are having trouble hearing. There is background noise. Andy, is that you taking notes? Could
questions.explanation: Techniques of active listening involve acknowledging, clarifying and confirming, understanding, and removing barriers that adversely affect comprehension. Of the choices, the only one that demonstrates confirming understanding is one that reflects the meaning of what was heard, "It sounds like what you're looking for with the page transition is a loading animation to show that the system is working on it" and invites correction or verification, "Am I correct?"

questions.prompt: To create the project schedule, a project manager needs to break down the project scope and develop a comprehensive list of activities, which are needed to complete the deliverable. What tool or technique should project manager use in this situation?
questions.explanation: The question implies that the Define Activities process is being carried out, and decomposition is one of the tools and techniques used during this process. Decomposition is a technique used for dividing and subdividing the project scope and project deliverables into smaller, more manageable parts. Decomposition is the technique that is being used to create the activity list as indicated by the question.

questions.prompt: A new project was chartered. As the project manager, you meet with some of the pre-assigned team members to look over the problems the project seeks to address. The project team feels the requirements provided are vague and unclear. What is the project manager's next best action?
questions.option_a: Assign accountability of the various requirements to each project team member
questions.option_b: Facilitate a benchmarking session to collect more detailed requirements
questions.option_c: Facilitate a discussion meeting between the project team and the customer
questions.option_d: Take responsibility as project manager to rewrite the requirements with more clarity
questions.explanation: Understanding the needs, wants, expectations, and requirements is extremely important for the project team to plan and manage the project to address those needs appropriately. The one best qualified to express those needs is the customer, or a suitable customer representative or business analyst. In the options provided to you in the question, the customer is an option. That is the project manager's best source. Clarifying the requirements and gaining more definition from the customer allows the project team to better fulfill those requirements. Including suitable members of the team eliminates misinterpretation and unanswered questions. Educating more of the team on the needs and the problems to solve enables the project team to better comprehend the requirements and customer need.

questions.prompt: During risk planning, a project manager collects historical information from past projects and populates the risk register with all the risks she can identify. She then records which team member will be the risk owner for which risk. In reviewing the risk register, the project sponsor says that the project manager made a critical mistake and should rethink her entire risk identification process. What is the best corrective action for the project manager to take at this point?
questions.option_a: Create a risk response plan before going to the project sponsor again
questions.option_b: Include the project team and other key stakeholders in the risk identification process
questions.option_c: Create a risk report to share with the project sponsor
questions.option_d: Ask project team members to volunteer as risk owners, rather than to assign them, as a way to get
questions.explanation: Project stakeholders should be encouraged to participate in risk identification activities. This activity makes it more likely that all project risks will be identified and helps project teams develop and maintain a sense of ownership and responsibility for identified project risks and their associated risk response actions. If a project manager creates the risk register without input from subject matter experts, project team members, or other stakeholders, it creates additional risks for the project that could jeopardize its success.

questions.prompt: To manage workflow on a software development project, you are using a Kanban board with strict work in progress (WIP) limits to expose bottlenecks. During project execution, one of the developers leaves the project, and you need to backfill the position. In searching for a replacement developer, which of the following characteristics would be of greatest value to the team in this project environment?
questions.option_b: Expertise in manual and automatic testing
questions.option_c: Diverse skill set with demonstrated versatility
questions.option_d: Proficiency in contract negotiations with vendors
questions.explanation: The question describes a team of developers that uses a Kanban board to manage their workflow and track tasks as they progress through the development process. A Kanban board is made up of columns representing the stages of work. For example, a Kanban board may have columns for the backlog, planning, coding, testing, and deployment. Work in progress (WIP) limits will place a limit on the work that can be in any particular column. Strict WIP limits will expose bottlenecks as a column will reach the limit, and no additional work can be added to that column. With a team of narrowly specializing developers, this can lead to idle time as the team member(s) wait for the bottleneck to clear before they can continue working. However, if the team is comprised of generalizing specialists, the workload can be balanced. The generalizing specialist may have a specialty but can perform any of the necessary project tasks. For example, if the testing stage has reached its WIP limit, a generalizing specialist can switch tasks from coding to testing to help clear the bottleneck. This flexibility will improve velocity as work is not held up by the bottleneck. Therefore, among the available options, it is best to select a candidate with a diverse skillset and demonstrated versatility to join the project team.

questions.prompt: You are a project manager who wants to mitigate the impact of any realized risks for the project. To achieve this goal, you conduct an initial trial at one location before launching to other locations. During the trial, you learn best practices to apply during the national launch and identify pitfalls that should be avoided. Once the trial is over, what should you do with this information?
questions.option_a: Capture the information in the lessons learned register
questions.option_b: Record the implementation of the risk responses in the risk register
questions.option_c: Document issues and their resolutions in the lessons learned repository
questions.option_d: Share the information with the project sponsor and stakeholders
questions.explanation: The question suggests you, as the project manager, are attempting to mitigate the impact of any identified and unknown risks by conducting a trial at one location prior to launching at all locations. The purpose of the trial is to learn best practices as well as identify any pitfalls. The knowledge gained from the trial will be vital to the success of the overall effort. The lessons learned register is a project document used to record knowledge gained during a project so that it can be used later in the project. You should capture the knowledge gained from the trial in the lessons learned register so that it can be used later in the project.

questions.prompt: Due to insufficient historical data, you are applying an approach that factors in uncertainty and risk calculations to determine time estimate of some of your project activities. The resulting time estimates will be based on a range. What tool or technique are you using?
questions.explanation: Three-point estimating factors in uncertainty and risk by using most-likely, optimistic, and pessimistic estimates to calculate the expected duration of an activity. One of the most commonly used formulas, tE=(tO+tM+tP)/3, factors the optimistic or best-case scenario for the activity duration, tO; the most likely duration of the activity, tM; and the pessimistic or worst-case scenario for the activity duration, tP; to arrive at the expected duration, tE. This approach is useful when there is insufficient historical data (as is the case in the scenario described) or when judgmental data is used. Duration estimates based on three points with an assumed distribution provide an expected duration and clarify the range of uncertainty around the expected duration.

questions.prompt: You are developing a charter for a high-risk project. You are aware that the project sponsor has a low-risk tolerance, which requires special consideration. What should you do next?
questions.option_a: Double the size of the management and contingency reserves
questions.option_b: Ensure the risk identification process is extremely rigorous
questions.option_c: Tell the sponsor that you have managed similar projects
questions.option_d: Consult with a PMI Risk Management Professional (PMI-RMP)®
questions.explanation: The question describes a high-risk project in the initiation stage where project risk is a significant concern. Although the identification of individual project risks is not formally performed until project planning, the project charter should contain a summary of the overall project risk. The project manager should consider consulting individuals or groups who have a specialized knowledge or training in risk identification. Seeking assistance from a PMI Risk Management Professional (PMI-RMP)® is an example of expert judgment. Under the circumstances described in the scenario, it is the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: A project is in the execution stage, and the project manager is currently in the process of implementing the stakeholder engagement plan. Which of the following activities is most likely to be conducted during this process?
questions.option_a:
Facilitating a meeting to discuss and address any issue or concern regarding stakeholder
engagement
questions.option_b: Producing work performance information about the status of stakeholder engagement
questions.option_c:
Capturing stakeholders' current and desired engagement levels in the stakeholder engagement
assessment matrix
questions.option_d:
Creating a document that contains identification, assessment, and classification information about
stakeholders
questions.explanation: The stakeholder engagement plan is a component of the project management plan that identifies the strategies and actions required to promote the productive involvement of stakeholders in project or program decision making and execution. The stakeholder engagement plan is created as an output of the Plan Stakeholder Engagement process and implemented during the Manage Stakeholder Engagement process. As part of the Manage Stakeholder Engagement process, meetings may be held to discuss and address any issue or concern regarding stakeholder engagement. The incorrect answer choices describe tasks that are typically associated with processes other than the Manage Stakeholder Engagement process. Therefore, facilitating a meeting to discuss and address any issue or concern regarding stakeholder engagement is the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: As the project manager, once your internal project team members are assigned, you ensure they can log in and have read and write permissions for the project repository, where all project related documents are electronically stored. Selected external stakeholders may be provided logins with limited access and read-only permissions. What type of communication do you encourage in this situation?
questions.explanation: The scenario implies you, as the project manager, are carrying out the Manage Communications process. The choice of media, such as a decision about when to use push/pull options, is among the techniques and considerations for effective communication management. Pull communication is a method that is used for large complex information sets, or for large audiences and requires the recipients to access content at their own discretion subject to security procedures. These methods include web portals, intranet sites, e-learning, lessons learned databases, or knowledge repositories. Of the choices provided, the repository described in the question meets the definition of pull communication closely, making pull communication the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: Due to a labor shortage, the project has endured an increase in costs for recruiting and hiring. Production has slowed considerably, and the original budget is unachievable. With the sponsor willing to invest more money, what should be used to determine the rate at which the project team needs to perform work to meet the original delivery date?
questions.explanation: As the project progresses, the actual results are analyzed against the planned results via the earned value analysis (EVA). At any point in the project, the to-complete performance index (TCPI) can be used to determine the ratio at which the work should be completed in order to meet the target. The target at first is the budget at completion (BAC). In this scenario, that target is considered no longer achievable. Hence, the project team must perform at a rate that meets the new adjusted budget - designated by the estimate at completion (EAC). Using the equation for TCPI that involves the EAC is what the team needs to determine that rate of performance: TCPI = (BAC - EV) / (EAC - AC) Note, to answer this question correctly, no calculation, but the understanding of the TCPI and its interpretation is required. The formula is provided for your reference.

questions.prompt: A project is looking to procure a series of products to be developed in tandem with the project. A single supplier must be able to build out the products and provide services to the project team as the team works on the multitude of other components. To best manage the quality of the long-term procurement need, what is the best practice?
questions.option_a: Require the seller to deliver earlier than needed to accommodate repairs of poor quality
questions.option_b: Reduce the number of metrics to optimize the cost of quality and associated assets
questions.option_c: Tighten the quality standards at various points in the project to ensure the seller is staying aware
questions.option_d: Form a relationship with the seller to build cooperation and mutually lasting solutions
questions.explanation: An emerging best practice for quality management is cooperating with suppliers rather than having it be a unidirectional transaction. This is especially beneficial in situations, like in this question, where the project team and the selected seller must work in tandem to develop a series of products and the underpinning services. Establishing a partnership with the supplier is a good practice. Setting and maintaining a certain set of quality standards for a project and the project's procurement needs over the length of the project is essential to ensure constant and reliable quality and healthy working relationships with the seller.

questions.prompt: Your project is to design a payroll automation system for a client company. You have assured the client that the system contains all of the state-of-the-art features to protect it against modern threats. The client has asked you to perform a penetration test in order to validate your claim. The cost to perform this test is an example of which of the following?
questions.explanation: Penetration testing is a technique to test the deterrence of a system against hacking attempts. This technique is typically a costly activity and requires time and expertise. Testing is performed to assess the quality of a system and is considered appraisal costs. Appraisal costs fall under the cost of conformance category and are an example of the data analysis techniques that can be used as part of the Plan Quality Management process.

questions.prompt: Which of the following represents a point in the project life cycle where stakeholder identification can take place?
questions.option_a: Initiation, planning, execution, monitoring and controlling, and closure
questions.explanation: The process of identifying stakeholders is frequently performed for the first time during the initiation of the project. However, if any point in the project, a person, group, or organization is found to affect, or be affected by, the project, they should be identified as a stakeholder and analyzed as to their impact of the project. Therefore, choosing the choice that includes all points in the project life cycle is the best answer. Note, the question is not about a Process Group or Domain in which stakeholder identification can take place. The question is about a point in a project life cycle. While the names look similar, they are not the same. One may argue that identifying stakeholders during project closure makes little sense. While it is true that towards the end of the project the chances of identifying a new stakeholder are low, it does not mean that new stakeholders should be ignored. Project closure consists of many activities. It is possible that a newly identified stakeholder is supposed to take over the project deliverables to operate and support them in production. If for some reason this stakeholder was not identified on time and ignored during project closure, it is likely that such a stakeholder may become resistant, stall the handover process, and negatively affect project closure. Another example is a multi-phase project. What if the newly identified stakeholder is supposed to be part of the next phase?

questions.prompt: After checking the internal resources, the project manager realizes that the company does not have developers who are experienced in the programming language required for the project. What should the project manager do next?
questions.option_a: Build a virtual team with internal resources
questions.option_d: Seek help from the project sponsor
questions.explanation: The question implies that the project manager is performing the Acquire Resources process. The resources needed for the project can be internal or external to the project-performing organization. However, in the scenario described by the question, the performing organization lacks developers experienced with the required programming language. Therefore, of the choices provided, the logical action for the project manager is to acquire the resources from outside the organization.

questions.prompt: A project team member has left the project and company. The replacement needs to get up to speed quickly and determine what work packages and activities are assigned to her team and department. Which of the following should she reference?
questions.explanation: The organizational breakdown structure (OBS) is an example of the hierarchical chart that can be used as a data representation technique while carrying out the Plan Resource Management process. The OBS presents the work packages and activities and how they are aligned with the organizational units of a project or company, such as team, department, and business unit. Therefore, the new team member should refer to the OBS to determine what work packages and activities are assigned to her team and department.

questions.prompt: A software development team implemented pair programming as a risk response. In a subsequent risk review, it was determined to be highly effective in catching programming errors and dramatically reducing the number of escaped defects. As a result of the risk review meeting, what should the project manager do first?
questions.option_a: Submit a change request to update the risk management plan
questions.option_b: Notify the project management office (PMO) of results from the risk review meeting
questions.option_c: Capture the information in the lessons learned register
questions.option_d: Update the lessons learned repository with the knowledge gained
questions.explanation: A risk review meeting is conducted as part of the Monitor Risks process. The question describes knowledge gained in the form of an effective risk response as the result of the risk review meeting. The lessons learned register is updated with any risk-related lessons learned during risk reviews so the knowledge gained can be used in later phases of the project. At the end of the project, the information contained in the lessons learned register is transferred to the lessons learned repository for use on future projects. Therefore, of the available options, capturing the information in the lessons learned register is the best answer to the question asked. Pair programming is a coding technique that is often used on agile projects. While this knowledge is not needed to answer this question correctly, it is important to understand that some projects can be managed using a hybrid project management approach where the project as a whole is lead using traditional management while some of its elements are carried out using agile methods.

questions.prompt: Midway into project execution, you have been asked to shorten your project duration by three weeks to meet a new deadline but deliver all originally planned functionality. Additional resources are available, and the cost is not as great a concern as schedule. What should you do?
questions.option_a: Crash activities on the critical path
questions.explanation: Provided that resources are available and an increase in cost is acceptable, to shorten your project schedule you can use a schedule compression technique such as crashing. Crashing is adding resources to an activity in order to complete it earlier than scheduled. This technique is typically used during project planning as part of the Develop Schedule process. While the scenario implies the project is already in execution, crashing can still be used as this technique is not limited only to the planning stage of the project. Project planning is an iterative process and can be done as necessary. Note, an approved change request will be required to crash the activities on the critical path as the implication of this action would likely affect some of the components of the project management plan.

questions.prompt: A project is underway, and only the baselines and project management plan are captured in the configuration management plan. A key stakeholder has submitted a formal request to include a new requirement in your project. The change would affect many project constraints. It increases the risk of the project going over budget and behind schedule. The change request has been approved by the change control board (CCB), and you have updated the scope baseline. What should you do next?
questions.option_a: Modify the stakeholder's classification in the stakeholder register
questions.option_b: Deny the request to avoid scope creep and keep your project on track
questions.option_c: Record the attributes of the new requirement in the requirements traceability matrix
questions.option_d: Submit a change request for an update to the requirements documentation
questions.explanation: The requirements traceability matrix is a grid that links product requirements from their origin to the deliverables that satisfy them. Updates to the requirements traceability matrix are among the outputs of the Control Scope process described in the question. Attributes associated with each requirement can be recorded in the requirements traceability matrix. These attributes help to define key information about the requirement. Among the available choices, recording the attributes associated with the stakeholder requirement in the requirements traceability matrix is the best answer to the question asked. Note, the question states that the change will affect many project constraints. The project is at greater risk of being over budget and behind schedule. Since the change request has been approved, one would expect the project manager to update all three baselines (scope, schedule, cost). However, according to the scenario, the project manager updated only the scope baseline. The absence of this information does not change the correct answer. PMP aspirants should expect some questions on the real exam to be incomplete, and, therefore, make reasonable assumptions based on the information available in the question, and, of the choices provided, select the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: During the execution of a project, a change request is submitted and approved. Which of the following should be done next?
questions.option_a: Holding a meeting to review the change requests that have been approved
questions.option_b: Analyzing the impact of the requested change on project constraints
questions.option_c: Implementing the change in the Monitor and Control Project Work process
questions.option_d: Updating the status of the change request in the change log
questions.explanation: The scenario in the questions describes the submission and approval of a change request implying the Perform Integrated Change Control process has been carried out. The change log has to be updated to reflect the change was approved and other information about the change that may affect its implementation in the Direct and Manage Project Work process. According to the reference provided, Figure 4-13 on page 114, it can be seen that the change log is updated as an output of the Perform Integrated Change Control process. Similarly, by reviewing the data flow diagram for the Direct and Manage Project Work process, one can see that the change log is shown as an input into that process. Thus, the change log must be revised before implementing any change.

questions.prompt: A project has been initiated to build a facility next to an estate with a homeowner association (HOA). During the first meeting with the HOA representatives, the project manager listened but did not directly address their concerns that the facility would be noisy and an eyesore. When they next met, the two parties cooperatively worked towards an agreement for the project to plant trees to obstruct the view and reduce the noise of the storage facility. What best describes interactions of the project manager with the HOA?
questions.explanation: In the scenario, the project manager is conducting the Develop Project Charter process. Conflict management is one of the tools and techniques of that process. Conflict management can be used to help bring stakeholders into alignment on the elements of the project charter. During the first meeting with the stakeholders, the project manager listens to their concerns but avoids the conflict because she is unprepared to address it. In other words, the project manager used the conflict management technique of withdraw/avoid. A follow-up meeting with the stakeholders represents a collaborate/problem solve conflict management technique where the parties agree on a win-win solution acceptable to both sides. Avoidance and collaboration are both represented in the scenario making avoidance and collaboration the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: You are managing a project where several key stakeholders feel it is a risky project. Your variance analysis shows you have a negative project float. Your CPI is 1.3. You and your project management team are discussing ways to bring the schedule back into alignment with the project management plan. Which of the following schedule-shortening options would be the best option?
questions.explanation: If your project has negative project float, the estimated completion date will be after the desired completion date. You and your project management team are looking for ways to reduce the schedule and bring it back into alignment with the project plan. In determining a path forward, you need to look at all the potential options for reducing the schedule and choose the option with the least impact on the project. The scenario implies that some of the key stakeholders have low risk tolerances, and fast tracking usually adds risk. Reducing scope or lowering quality will reduce the project schedule but will most likely negatively impact the customer. The project is under budget, however, so crashing the project is the best choice.

questions.prompt: A project team on a construction project have updated the risk register and risk report. One of the high-priority risks is a major hurricane that may occur during construction and could potentially result in massive rework. What should the project manager do next to determine whether or not hurricane barriers should be built as a preventive measure?
questions.option_a: Evaluate the risk breakdown structure (RBS) to figure out if a hurricane is a risk the project is
questions.option_b: Conduct a decision tree analysis to quantify the expense of building hurricane barriers versus the
questions.option_c: Conduct a probability and impact assessment to determine where the hurricane risk ranks in
questions.option_d: Produce risk scores to assess the likelihood a major hurricane will occur during the construction
questions.explanation: The RBS can be ruled out as a correct answer because it is a categorization technique and does not help the project manager decide between alternative options. The probability and impact matrix is an output from the Plan Risk Management process. The matrix is then used in the risk probability and impact assessment tool/technique in the Perform Qualitative Risk Analysis process but is unlikely to help the project manager in determining whether or not hurricane barriers should be built. Risk scores are used to rank risks in the probability and impact matrix and are also of little value in making a decision regarding building the barriers. The fact the hurricane risk has already been identified and ranked means the Plan Risk Management, the Identify Risks, and the Perform Qualitative Risk Analysis processes have already been completed. Therefore, the three answer choices concerning the RBS, the risk scores, and risk assessment can be eliminated. The next process in the Project Risk Management Knowledge Area is Perform Quantitative Risk Analysis. Decision tree analysis is one of its tools and techniques. In deciding whether to build or not to build the hurricane barriers, cost should be taken into account. A decision tree analysis calculates the expected monetary value for each alternative, providing quantitative values that factor into the decision-making process and is, therefore, the best answer choice to the question asked.

questions.prompt: You are managing a project in which the first deliverables received corrective feedback from the client, resulting in change requests that have just been approved. In which of the following project management process groups do you implement these approved change requests?
questions.explanation: There are only three processes that have approved change requests as inputs: Direct and Manage Project Work, Control Quality, and Control Procurements. The first of these is in the Executing process group, and that is when you implement approved change requests. The latter two processes are in the Monitoring and Controlling, where you verify the changes were properly implemented, but this process group was not one of the choices.

questions.prompt: As a project manager, you are in the process of working with the project stakeholders to foster the appropriate level of stakeholder involvement. Which activity are you least likely to perform in support of this process?
questions.option_a: Clarifying and resolving issues that have been identified by the stakeholders
questions.option_b: Developing a matrix that compares current and desired stakeholder engagement levels
questions.option_c: Addressing any risks or potential concerns related to stakeholder engagement
questions.option_d: Managing stakeholder expectations through negotiation and communication
questions.explanation: The scenario implies the Manage Stakeholder Engagement process is underway. Manage Stakeholder Engagement is the process of communicating and working with stakeholders to meet their needs and expectations, address issues, and foster appropriate stakeholder involvement. The question is asking for the activity 'least' likely to be performed as part of this process. The incorrect answer choices all represent activities that should be conducted in support of the Manage Stakeholder Engagement process. The correct answer choice describes the stakeholder engagement assessment matrix which is a data representation technique that is used during the Plan Stakeholder Engagement process. Plan Stakeholder Engagement is the process of developing approaches to involve project stakeholders, based on their needs, expectations, interests, and potential impact on the project. Of the available choices, the activity 'least' likely to be performed in this scenario is the development of a matrix that compares current and desired stakeholder engagement levels.

questions.prompt: A hospital is closing a project that resulted in a new business process for managing oncology patients. The hospital realizes that the same process can be used for all its patients, which can save the hospital thousands of dollars. Where should this information be captured?
questions.explanation: All project documents may be updated as a result of project closure. Of particular interest is the lessons learned register which is finalized to include final information on phase or project closure. The final lessons learned register might include information on benefits management, the accuracy of the business case, project and development life cycles, risk and issue management, stakeholder engagement, and other project management processes. The scenario describes an additional benefit the project created on top of what has been originally planned. This information should be recorded in the lessons learned register and later on transferred to the lessons learned repository for use by future projects.

questions.prompt: One of the project manager's key responsibilities is to motivate the project team to achieve maximum productivity and increase the probability of project success. In this respect, according to Maslow's hierarchy of needs, which of the below is most correct?
questions.option_a: Physiological needs such as food water and sleep must be met before financial compensation,
questions.option_b: All workers fit into two groups, X and Y. The X group avoids responsibility, does the least work
questions.option_c: All motivators can be divided into hygiene factors that must be satisfied, such as working
questions.option_d: There are three universal motivators: achievement, affiliation, and power. In every person, one of
questions.explanation: The question refers to organizational theories which should be considered as part of the Plan Resource Management process. An organizational theory provides information about how people, teams, and organizational units behave - in this case, the project team. While all answer choices represent various organizational theories, only one describes Maslow's theory of human motivation. Psychologist Abraham Maslow grouped motivational factors into a hierarchy, with physiological needs such as air, food, water, and sleep at the lowest and most fundamental level. He argued that these essential needs must be met before people can be motivated by higher order factors, such as financial security, recognition, and social acceptance. In order to best motivate the project team, it is important to ensure that basic needs are indeed met, such as ensuring a standard work schedule and providing reasonable working conditions. Although the PMBOK® Guide comprises the majority of the content you will encounter on your exam, the PMP® Examination Content Outline assumes that the prospective PMP aspirants are familiar with other sources that are used by the Project Management Institute (PMI)® as content material. These include such topics as PMI’s Practice Standards, PMI Code of Ethics and Professional Conduct, PMI’s Practice Standard for Scheduling; commonly accepted statistical analysis techniques such as ROI, IRR, and NPV; organizational behavior theories such as Tuckman’s Ladder, Theory X and Theory Y, Maslow's hierarchy of needs; commonly frowned upon project management practices, such as gold plating; and others.

questions.prompt: You are advising a project manager who is new to your company and has just been assigned to manage a project. The sponsor on his project is very demanding and is asking to see a project schedule developed as soon as possible. The project manager would like to meet the sponsor's request and produce a project schedule as quickly as possible but without jeopardizing work quality. Which of the following represent the best advice for the project manager in this case?
questions.option_a: Develop the activity list, then the WBS and WBS dictionary
questions.option_b: Develop the WBS and activity list concurrently with the help of project team members
questions.option_c: Develop the WBS and activity list concurrently using the project manager's expertise, rather than
questions.option_d: Ask senior management for a new sponsor within the company
questions.explanation: The WBS defines the work package deliverables, which are then decomposed into the activities required to produce those deliverables. Therefore, ideally, the WBS is developed first as part of the Create WBS process, and the activity list is created later on during the Define Activities process to which the WBS, a component of the scope baseline, serves as an input. Under certain circumstances, for example, when the plans are needed urgently as described in this scenario, the project manager may choose to develop the activity list and WBS concurrently. But in such cases, it is important for the project manager to involve the project team members in the Define Activities process to ensure better and more accurate results without compromising the quality of the plans. Therefore, of the choices provided, developing the WBS and activity list concurrently with the help of project team members is the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: A project manager must submit various high-level performance reports to executive managers throughout the organization. The project team broke down the work to align with the reporting needs to integrate costs, resources, and scope. Which level of the WBS should the project manager report?
questions.explanation: A typical project work breakdown structure, or WBS, consists of control accounts, work

questions.prompt: A retailer has an aggressive expansion plan for four new stores. Each of the four projects is led by a project manager. The projects are being managed in a coordinated manner. Three of the stores are ahead of schedule. The fourth store is well behind schedule, and its project manager wants some construction workers from the other locations to be transferred to help out. What should the project manager do first to secure the additional workers?
questions.option_a: Submit a change request to increase the scope baseline
questions.option_b: Direct the other project managers to provide additional resources
questions.option_c: Capture the information in the lessons learned register
questions.option_d: Escalate the issue to the program manager
questions.explanation: A program is a group of related projects that are being managed in a coordinated manner to obtain benefits not available from managing them individually. The question states that all four projects and project managers are being managed in a coordinated manner, which implies that a program manager is leading all four projects. Programs are managed by program managers who ensure that program benefits are delivered as expected by coordinating the activities of a program's components. In this scenario, with three projects ahead of schedule and one well behind, it may make sense for the program manager to reallocate some resources to ensure that all four locations can be opened on time. Therefore, of the available choices, escalating the issue to the program manager is the best answer.

questions.prompt: The following table is taken from the project's current performance report. The current cost performance is expected to remain unchanged for the remainder of the project. The method used to determine the earned value is based on percent complete. Based on this information, what is the VAC for the project?
questions.explanation: The question is asking for the variance at completion (VAC) based on the assumption that the cost performance will not change for the remainder of the project. The VAC can be calculated as BAC - EAC, where BAC is the budget at completion, EAC is the estimate at completion. The BAC for this project is provided as $500,000 (which is shown on the chart as the planned value for the project). However, the EAC must be calculated. When the cost performance is not expected to change, as in the scenario described, the EAC can be calculated as BAC / CPI, where CPI is the cost performance index. The formula for CPI is earned value (EV) divided by actual costs (AC). The EV is the sum of the planned work completed, which, in this case, is 100% of activity 1, 100% of activity 2, and 50% of activity 3. AC is the sum of the costs for each of the individual activities, as provided in the chart. Note, since the scenario mentions that the method used to determine the earned value is based on percent complete, the earned value of a partially completed work package is calculated by multiplying the planned value of the work package by its the percent complete. The calculations are shown below: EV = EV of activity 1 + EV of activity 2 + EV of activity 3 = (1 x $15,000) + (1 x $10,000) + (0.50 x $250,000) = $150,000 AC = AC of activity 1 + AC of activity 2 + AC of activity 3 = $14,000 + $11,000 + $175,000 = $200,000 CPI = EV / AC = $150,000 / $200,000 = 0.75 EAC = BAC / CPI = $500,000 / 0.75 = $666,667 VAC = BAC - EAC = $500,000 - $666,6667 = -$166,667 Therefore, in this case, the VAC is -$166,667. Since the VAC is negative, the project is running over planned costs.

questions.prompt: A project manager is in the planning stage of a project to construct a new water treatment facility. Currently, the project manager is preparing to develop the change management plan. The performing organization has a project management office (PMO), which can best be described as supportive. What role might the PMO play in the development of the change management plan?
questions.option_a: The PMO will prepare the change management plan for the project
questions.option_b: A PMO assists with project execution and would not be involved in project planning
questions.option_c: Supply a template for the change management plan
questions.option_d: The project manager reports to the PMO, and the PMO directly manages the project
questions.explanation: The change management plan is a component of the project management plan that establishes the change control board, documents the extent of its authority, and describes how the change control system will be implemented. A supportive PMO provides a consultative role to projects by supplying templates, best practices, training, access to information, and lessons learned from other projects. Therefore, of the available choices, supplying a template best describes the role a supportive PMO might play in the development of the change management plan.

questions.prompt: A project manager is leading a project in which unseasonal weather conditions have caused the office building construction project to run well behind schedule. What could the project manager have done differently earlier in the project to address the unpredictability of weather conditions?
questions.option_a: Pre-assigned team members during project initiation
questions.option_b: Utilized risk exploiting as a risk response strategy
questions.option_c: Developed a cause and effect diagram
questions.explanation: A variability risk represents an uncertainty, which exists about some key characteristics of a planned event, activity, or decision. Unusual weather conditions occurring during the construction of the office building is an example of a variability risk. A Monte Carlo simulation is an analysis technique where a computer model is iterated many times, with the input values chosen at random for each iteration driven by the input data, including probability distributions and probabilistic branches. Outputs are generated to represent the range of possible outcomes for the project. Variability risks can be addressed using Monte Carlo analysis, with the range of variation reflected in probability distributions, followed by actions to reduce the spread of possible outcomes. Therefore, of the available options, conducting a Monte Carlo analysis is the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: You are facilitating a risk workshop with your project team to discuss, evaluate, and prioritize previously identified project risks based on the probability and impact of each risk. During the meeting, a key stakeholder proposes that as part of the analysis, the team consider other characteristics of risk beyond just probability and impact. Which of the following is the least likely characteristic proposed by the stakeholder:
questions.explanation: The scenario describes a risk workshop, a technique used in conducting a qualitative risk analysis. However, sensitivity analysis is a technique used as part of quantitative risk analysis to determine which project risks or other sources of uncertainty have the most potential impact on project outcomes. Also, a sensitivity analysis focuses on evaluating a risk’s potential impact on achieving project objectives. Since the project stakeholder in this scenario is looking to use the characteristics of risk beyond probability and impact to conduct the analysis, it is reasonable to assume that sensitivity is the least likely characteristic suggested by the stakeholder.

questions.prompt: A seller has completed the work that was formally accepted by the buyer. The key deliverables specified in the statement of work (SOW) are the robot, operational and support documents, specialized tools, and software. The seller has also offered the buyer to purchase a support service, but the buyer has declined the offer. Two weeks after the robot was deployed, the buyer contacts the seller to ask how to adjust the robot's programming. What is the best course of action for the seller?
questions.option_a: Tell the buyer that they should have purchased the support service
questions.option_b: Record the problem in the issue log for the seller's project
questions.option_c: Return to the job site and make the requested adjustments
questions.option_d: Advise the buyer to check their organizational process assets
questions.explanation: The question states that operational and support documents were among the key project deliverables. These documents should have the necessary information on how to make adjustments to the robot. Note, there are two sides to the scenario, the buyer, and the seller. Both are carrying out the project. The buyer in this scenario is the performing organization, while the seller is the one who delivers the work based on the procurement statement of work (SOW) provided by the buyer. The buyer, as the performing organization, is supposed to carry out all processes necessary to complete the project, including the Close Project or Phase process. Updates to the organizational process assets (OPAs) is among the outputs of this process. Documents required for an organization to maintain, operate, and support the product or service delivered by the project (whether the product/service has been produced internally or by an external vendor) are part of the buyer's OPAs updated during the Close Project or Phase process. Even though it was the seller who has produced the documents, it is the responsibility of the buyer to ensure the documents are available. Therefore, of the choices offered, the best course of action for the seller is to advise the buyer to check their organizational process assets to review the operational and support documents which were delivered by the seller to the buyer.

questions.prompt: A project manager is in the process of estimating the costs of a project to construct a 300,000 square foot shopping mall. Based on historical data, the project manager uses a value of $400 per square foot and estimates the cost at $120,000,000. What estimating technique is the project manager using?
questions.explanation: The question implies that the Estimate Costs process is underway. Parametric estimating is an estimating technique in which an algorithm is used to calculate the cost based on historical data and project parameters. The example provided in the question uses an average cost per square foot to estimate the cost, which is a very simplistic example of parametric estimating. While both parametric estimating and analogous estimating use historical data to calculate the cost (or duration), there is a difference between the two. Analogous estimating is used when there is a limited amount of information available for the current project, but the project/activity looks similar to a past project/activity. Thus, the overall cost estimate of the current project/activity is considered similar (analogous) to the past one and, therefore, the cost can be ‘roughly’ estimated. This is the reason analogous estimating is sometimes called top-down estimating. This method is less accurate than parametric estimating. Parametric estimating, on the other hand, uses information from past similar projects/activities AND parameters of the current project/activities because these parameters are already known during project planning. There is a statistical relationship between the parameters, such as cost per unit and the number of units. When cost per unit from the past project/activity as well as the number of units required for the current project are known, an algorithm can be used to estimate cost, thus producing a more accurate estimate. In the scenario described, the number of units and the cost per unit are known - 300,000 square foot, $400 per square foot. The project manager multiplied one parameter by the other and obtained the cost estimate of $120,000,000. Therefore, of the choices provided, parametric estimating is the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: You are in the initiation stage of a project that aims to deploy and then maintain the robotics for an automated production line for an important client. In the project charter, you need to include the agreed mean time to recover (MTTR) in the event of a breakdown. What should you do to find this information?
questions.option_a: Do nothing since agreements are not yet available during project initiation
questions.option_b: Refer to the procurement management plan
questions.option_c: Review any emails or other correspondence with the client
questions.option_d: Check the service level agreement with the client
questions.explanation: A service level agreement (SLA) can serve as an input into the Develop Project Charter process. An SLA may define agreed-upon service levels such as mean time to recover (MTTR). The SLA is likely to be included in the terms and conditions of a contract along with penalties or other recourse for failing to achieve the agreed-upon service levels. Therefore, of the available answer choices, you, as the project manager, should check the SLA for information on the agreed-upon mean time to recover.

questions.prompt: You are the project manager for the initial design phase of a new ergonomic pen. You have conducted multiple interviews with pen users and have tested various design concepts. Your most recent design has been verified and accepted by key stakeholders. The pen design is ready to transition to the team managing the next phase. Which of the following should you consult to find out who will take ownership of the pen design?
questions.explanation: Since the pen design is ready to transition to the next phase, you can infer the scenario is in the Close Project or Phase process. A key action when closing a phase is transferring the ownership of your phase's intermediary deliverables to the proper group or organization tasked with using the output from your phase to meet the project objectives in their phase. You would consult the project management plan, specifically the subsidiary project life cycle description because it describes the series of phases that a project passes through from its initiation to its closure. While the PMBOK® Guide does not explicitly state that the project management plan specifies ‘who’ in the next phase takes ownership of the deliverables from the previous phase, it is reasonable to assume that, of the choices given, only the project management plan is likely to have this information and is, therefore, the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: The following chart was taken from the project's current performance report. The project has encountered some unforeseen obstacles, but those challenges have now been overcome. You are confident that you can hold costs to the budgeted rate for the remainder of the project. The method used to determine the earned value is based on percent complete. Based on the budgeted rate, what is the EAC for the project?
questions.explanation: The question states that the future performance will be at the budgeted rate. Even though the project to date has not performed at the budgeted rate, it is expected that the project will perform at the budgeted rate for the remainder of the project. Therefore, the formula that should be used in this scenario is EAC = AC + (BAC – EV). Note, since the scenario mentions that the method used to determine the earned value is based on percent complete, the earned value of a partially completed work package is calculated by multiplying the planned value of the work package by its the percent complete. The calculations are shown below: EV = EV of activity 1 + EV of activity 2 + EV of activity 3 = (1 x $15,000) + (1 x $10,000) + (0.50 x $250,000) = $150,000 AC = AC of activity 1 + AC of activity 2 + AC of activity 3 = $14,000 + $11,000 + $175,000 = $200,000 EAC = AC + (BAC - EV) = $200,000 + ($500,000 - $150,000) = $550,000 Therefore, the estimate at completion (EAC) is $550,000.

questions.prompt: The following table represents the current project status. According to the project schedule, system analysis, system design, and system development should now be complete. The project manager is confident that the project objectives will be met. The method used to determine the earned value is based on percent complete. What is the project's current schedule performance index, and what does this mean for the project?
questions.option_a: The SPI is -$12,500, and the project is running behind schedule
questions.option_b: An SPI of 1.0 since the project manager is confident that the project objectives will be met
questions.option_c: The SPI is 1.24, and the project is running ahead of schedule
questions.option_d: The SPI is 0.81, and the project is running behind schedule
questions.explanation: The schedule performance index (SPI) can be calculated as the earned value (EV) divided by the planned value (PV). The current total earned value can be obtained by multiplying each activity's planned value by its percentage complete and adding them together. Therefore, the current total earned value is $52,500. The current total planned value is the sum of the planned values of the activities that should have been completed by now according to the approved plan, which is this scenario is $65,000. Note, since the scenario mentions that the method used to determine the earned value is based on percent complete, the earned value of a partially completed work package is calculated by multiplying the planned value of the work package by its the percent complete. The calculations are shown below: SPI = EV / PV EV = ($5,000 x 100%) + ($10,000 x 100%) + ($50,000 x 75%) = $52,500 PV = $5,000 + $10,000 + $50,000 = $65,000 SPI = $52,500 / $65,000 = 0.81 Therefore, the schedule performance index is 0.81. Additionally, since the SPI is less than one, the project is running behind schedule.

questions.prompt: A project is broken down into four phases: design, build, test, deploy. At the end of the design phase, the project manager would like to determine if the project should be terminated or proceed to the build phase. What should the project manager do next?
questions.option_c: Facilitate a meeting with the project team
questions.explanation: A phase gate is held at the end of a phase. The purpose of a phase gate is to compare the project's

questions.prompt: To create a burndown chart at the end of iteration planning, where is the to-be-charted work pulled from?
questions.explanation: Burndown charts are typically used on agile projects. A burndown chart is intended to relay the progress of the project team in any iteration. That progress is based on the work pulled from the iteration backlog. That iteration backlog represents the commitment of the project team for the work to be completed in the upcoming iteration. As the iteration progresses, the expectation is that the committed work is being completed or ‘burned off’. Hence the chart starts with what was committed and if things go as planned, that work is burned down until there is no other work remaining in that iteration.

questions.prompt: You are planning a large, complex, and lengthy project. To identify as many risks as possible, you want input from the sponsor, subject matter experts, vendors, customer representatives, and other project stakeholders. Which of the following will help you the most?
questions.explanation: According to the scenario, you are about to begin the Identify Risks process. A risk workshop is an example of a meeting that can be used as part of this process. A risk workshop a specialized meeting undertaken for the sole purpose of risk identification and is typically used for large, complex projects, and may involve the sponsor, subject matter experts, vendors, customer representatives, and other project stakeholders. Risk workshops often include some sort of brainstorming, but other risk identification techniques may be used depending on the level of the risk process defined in the risk management plan. To increase the effectiveness of the meeting, it is recommended that a skilled facilitator attends the risk workshop.

questions.prompt: You have compiled a list of potential stakeholders for your project, and now you are classifying the stakeholders with regard to their influence. As you work through your way down the list of stakeholders, you have come to the project sponsor. How should you classify the direction of influence of this stakeholder?
questions.explanation: According to the scenario, you are carrying out the Identify Stakeholders process. Directions of influence is an example of a data representation technique that can be used as part of this process. This technique classifies stakeholders according to their influence on the work of the project or the project team itself. Stakeholders can be classified as upward, downward, outward, or sideward. The upward classification includes senior management of the performing organization or customer organization, sponsor, and steering committee. Therefore, the project sponsor should be classified as upward.

questions.prompt: In preparation for an upcoming project status meeting, you have determined the value of the work completed thus far is $100,000 with a budget at completion (BAC) of $1,000,000. At this point in the project, you have spent 12% of the project budget. The value of work planned to be completed at this point is $115,000. What will you report at the project status meeting?
questions.option_a: The project is performing well in terms of cost but not in terms of schedule
questions.option_b: The project is not performing well both in terms of cost and schedule
questions.option_c: The project is currently behind schedule
questions.option_d: The project is currently over budget
questions.explanation: To determine the status of the project, the schedule variance and cost variance (or, alternatively, the cost performance index (CPI) and schedule performance index (SPI)) must be calculated. The earned value (EV), which is the actual work completed in the scenario described, is stated as $100,000. The planned value (PV), which is the value of work planned to be completed at this point in the scenario, is stated as $115,000. The actual cost (AC), which is the portion of the project budget you have spent so far in the scenario described, is 12% of the budget of $1,000,000 which is $120,000. The calculations of the cost variance (CV) and the schedule variance (SV) are shown below: CV = EV - AC = $100,000 - $120,000 = -$20,000 SV = EV - PV = $100,000 - $115,000 = -$15,000 Both the schedule variance and the cost variance are negative at this point in the project. Therefore, at the project status meeting, you should report that the project is not performing well both in terms of cost and schedule. Alternatively, you can calculate the CPI and the SPI: CPI = EV / AC = $100,000 / $120,000 = 0.83 SPI = EV / PV = $100,000 / $115,000 = 0.87 Both the schedule variance and the cost variance are less than 1 at this point in the project. Therefore, the same conclusion can be made: the project is not performing well both in terms of cost and schedule. Note, three answer choices represent correct statements regarding the status of the project described in the scenario. However, the goal of the PMP aspirant is, of the choices provided, to select the best answer to the question asked. Reporting to the meeting participants that the project is over budget would not be wrong. Reporting to the meeting participants that the project is behind schedule would not be wrong either. However, none of these statements fully represents the current project status. Of the available choices, reporting to the meeting participants that the project is over budget 'and' behind schedule, i.e., the project i

questions.prompt: A newly identified stakeholder just informed the project team of changes to regulations that would impact the project. Which of the following is the least likely to be updated in this situation?
questions.explanation: The key to answering this question correctly is to understand the nature and purpose of each of the answer choices. While it may be tempting to think that the change management plan needs to be updated to reflect the change in the regulations, the change management plan describes 'how' the change requests throughout the project will be formally authorized and incorporated. The changes themselves are captured in the change log, not in the change management plan. Therefore, the way the changes are handled on the project would likely remain the same regardless of the changes to regulations. However, changes to regulations will likely require an additional scope, which must be accounted for in the scope baseline. Additionally, anytime new stakeholders are added to the project, as is in the scenario describes, the stakeholder register must be updated. Therefore, since the question is asking to select the option that represents the 'least' likely project artifact to be updated, the change management plan is the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: You are mentoring project stakeholders on processes to be carried out during the project. You are now displaying the slide about the Close Project or Phase process. What should you tell the stakeholders about this process?
questions.option_a: The Close Project or Phase process is not used if the project is terminated before completion.
questions.option_b: The Close Project or Phase process is always the last process to be performed.
questions.option_c: The Close Project or Phase process is performed before the administrative closure.
questions.option_d: Procurements are closed out as part of the Close Project or Phase process.
questions.explanation: The Close Project or Phase is the process of finalizing all activities for the project or phase. During this process the project or phase information is archived, the planned work is completed, and the organizational team resources are released to pursue new endeavors. The Close Project or Phase process is always the last project management process carried out. Of the available options, the only answer choice that is correct in all cases is that the Close Project or Phase process will be the final project management process performed.

questions.prompt: The current project phase is scheduled to conclude on July 29. Today is July 14, and the project team should complete the remaining work well before the July 29 deadline. The project manager proposes a change to move ahead a few tasks from the next phase into the current phase to prevent the team from procrastinating and coasting. Which of the following theories describes the project manager's concern behind this change request?
questions.explanation: The question describes a situation where the project manager, based on an analysis of the velocity of the team's prior work, feels that the remaining work should be able to be completed well before the current deadline. The project manager believes that unless more work is assigned in this phase, the project team will waste time, since the deadline gives them more than the time they need to finish the remaining work of the first phase. According to Parkinson's law, work expands to fill the time available for its completion. In other words, if resources are left idle, time and money are wasted. The project manager, in this case, is concerned with optimizing resource utilization. Therefore, of the choices provided, Parkinson’s law is the best answer to the question asked. Although the PMBOK® Guide comprises the majority of the content that you will encounter on the Project Management Professional (PMP)® exam, the PMP® Examination Content Outline assumes that the prospective PMP aspirants are familiar with other sources that are used by the Project Management Institute (PMI)® as content material. Some of the answer choices presented in this question are based on the sources outside the PMBOK® Guide.

questions.prompt: A project manager is leading a software development project for an important client. The following table represents the current project status. According to the project schedule, system analysis, system design, and system development should now be complete. The method used to determine the earned value is based on percent complete. What is the project's current schedule variance, and what does this mean for the project?
questions.option_a: The schedule variance is -$12,500, and the project performance is unknown
questions.option_b: The schedule variance is -$12,500, and the project is running behind schedule
questions.option_c: The schedule variance is -$12,500, and the project is running ahead of schedule
questions.option_d: The schedule variance is $12,500, and the project is running behind schedule
questions.explanation: The schedule variance (SV) can be calculated as the earned value (EV) minus the planned value (PV). The current total earned value can be obtained by multiplying each activity's planned value by its percentage complete and adding them together. Therefore, the current total earned value is $52,500. The current total planned value is the sum of the planned values of the activities that should have been completed according to the approved plan, which is this scenario is $65,000. Note, since the scenario mentions that the method used to determine the earned value is based on percent complete, the earned value of a partially completed work package is calculated by multiplying the planned value of the work package by its percent complete. The following are the correct calculations for the schedule variance: SV = EV - PV EV = ($5,000 x 100%) + ($10,000 x 100%) + ($50,000 x 75%) = $52,500 PV = $5,000 + $10,000 + $50,000 = $65,000 SV = $52,500 - $65,000 = -$12,500 Therefore, the schedule variance (SV) is -$12,500. Since the SV is a negative value, in this case, the project is running behind schedule.

questions.prompt: A project manager at a shipbuilding firm is managing a marine construction project. The ship is partially complete when a previously undiscovered systematic flaw in the deck welds is detected. As a result, an additional plate will need to be designed and fabricated for every piece of deck equipment. Which of the following must be updated?
questions.explanation: In the scenario, the project team is performing quality management activities. The main focus of the question is to determine what plan or document must be updated as a result of adding a plate for every piece of deck equipment. Since the fabrication and the installation of those plates represent an additional scope, the scope baseline must be updated (assuming approval of the corresponding change request has been obtained). Therefore, of the choices available, the scope baseline is the document that must be updated and is, therefore, the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: After reviewing proposals for a large-scale product development project, vendors X and Y are equally qualified to deliver both the hardware and software. The project manager believes the hardware should be developed with a traditional project management approach, but the software might be better suited to an agile framework. What is the best course of action?
questions.option_a: Contract with vendor X for hardware, with vendor Y for software and use the appropriate
questions.option_b: Contract with vendor X for both hardware and software and require an agile framework for the
questions.option_c: Contract with vendor Y for both hardware and software and require a waterfall approach for the
questions.option_d: Contract with either vendor X or vendor Y for both hardware and software and utilize a master
questions.explanation: Larger projects may use an adaptive approach for some deliverables and a traditional project management approach for other parts. In these cases, a governing agreement such as a master services agreement (MSA) may be used for the overall engagement, with the adaptive work being placed in an appendix or supplement. This hybrid strategy allows changes to occur on the adaptive scope without impacting the overall contract. The incorrect answer choices represent actions that will not permit the selected vendor from using the best project management approach for each aspect of the project. Since both vendors are capable of delivering both the hardware and the software, and since there is not enough information in the question to determine which one of them is better in delivering projects using waterfall or agile project management approach, among the available choices, utilizing a master services agreement (MSA) with either of the vendors provides the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: You are managing a project for a company that in conjunction with another vendor has been contracted by a client to develop a customer relationship management (CRM) system for their business. Early in project execution, the client terminates the other vendor for undisclosed reasons and asks if your company can complete the entire project. You had recognized and analyzed this scenario during project planning, and now this opportunity has materialized. What should you do first?
questions.option_a: Check the risk register for a risk response plan
questions.option_b: Accept the opportunity and ask for an addendum to the existing contract
questions.option_c: Seek an expert opinion to determine whether or not to accept the opportunity
questions.option_d: Submit a change request to update the project management plan and baselines
questions.explanation: The question indicates that you, as the project manager, had recognized and analyzed the possibility of taking over the entire project during project planning, which implies that this risk had been documented in the risk register along with its agreed-upon risk response. Keep in mind that risks can either be threats (negative risks) or opportunities (positive risks). In the scenario described the risk is considered an opportunity. Realized risks (either negative or positive) should trigger the Implement Risk Responses process. The risk register serves as an input to the Implement Risk Responses process and records the agreed-upon risk responses for each individual risk and the nominated owners for each response plan. Therefore, of the available answer choices, checking the risk register for a risk response plan is what you should do first.

questions.prompt: The project is falling behind schedule and is over budget. Nonetheless, there is pressure from the project sponsor to complete the project as scheduled. You must determine what the project’s performance must be from this point onwards to achieve the delivery goals. Which calculation are you most likely to use?
questions.explanation: In this situation, you are faced with the project not earning the value as planned. Nevertheless, you are expected to still meet those planned targets. The rate at which your project must perform to succeed at reaching that target is what you must determine. The current schedule performance index, or SPI, is less than one. The question states that the project is underperforming. If the SPI were 1.0, then the project would be on target. Since it is below the 1.0 target, you must figure out what the SPI rate must be to make up for that lost ground and pull within the target. That calculation is the to-complete performance index or TCPI. The TCPI equation is (BAC – EV) / (BAC – AC).

questions.prompt: A project manager has taken over a project that has been in execution for a few months. He ran into problems right away. He has had difficulty getting sign off on decisions; he doesn't have the authority to acquire the equipment necessary for the project; the stakeholders and the project team have differing opinions about the objectives and the outcome of the project. What is the most likely source of these problems?
questions.option_c: Lack of a project management plan
questions.option_d: Lack of a procurement management plan
