The incorrect answer choices represent activities that can only be performed after the requirements have been determined. Therefore, of the available options, scheduling a requirements workshop is the best response. Note, although the scenario suggests the project is managed using agile methodology where traditional project management processes may or may not be conducted as specified in the PMBOK® Guide, some of the answer choices and the explanations refer to the traditional approach. Regardless of this, scheduling a requirements workshop is still the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: Which of the following integrates scope, cost, and schedule and should be updated to reflect changes to a project?
questions.explanation: The performance measurement baseline (PMB) is a component of the project management plan that integrates scope, schedule, and cost baselines and is used for comparison to manage, measure, and control project execution. In order for the project manager and the team to be able to use the PMB as a measure of the project's performance, the PMB must be updated to reflect the current state of the project, which includes all the approved changes. If the PMB is not updated, it will give an inaccurate representation of the performance and will not be useful when compared to the results of the earned value analysis.

questions.prompt: To better use existing knowledge and to create new knowledge for a project, the project manager is looking for ways to identify any knowledge gaps. Which of the following would help the project manager the most to complete this process?
questions.explanation: Using existing knowledge and creating new knowledge for a project is essentially the definition of the Manage Project Knowledge process. When the knowledge that the project has is compared to its knowledge needs, then the gaps in knowledge can be identified and addressed. Specifically, the resource breakdown structure, as it pertains to the Manage Project Knowledge process, includes information on the composition of the team and may help to understand what knowledge is available as a group and what knowledge may be missing.

questions.prompt: A project manager is in the middle of executing a project when they discover the project is behind schedule. The project deadline cannot be extended, and the project sponsor will not approve any scope or budget changes. Which of the following is the best course of action for the project manager to take to complete the project faster?
questions.option_a: Hire additional resources and work overtime
questions.option_b: Tell the sponsor that the deadline cannot be met
questions.option_c: Look at options for fast tracking the critical path
questions.explanation: The project manager must complete the project faster without making any changes to the project scope or budget. Therefore, the project manager's best course of action is to use a schedule compression technique. Crashing is a schedule compression technique used to shorten the schedule duration by adding additional resources, but this may result in increased cost. Since the project sponsor will not approve any budget changes, crashing is not a good choice. Fast tracking is a schedule compression technique in which activities or phases that are normally done in sequence, are performed in parallel for at least a portion of their duration. Since fast tracking can expedite the schedule without resulting in increased cost, fast tracking is the best answer among the choices provided. Note, fast tracking may result in rework and increased risk.

questions.prompt: In order to support the continuous development of your project team, you schedule monthly skill-building meetings. Lately, the meetings have become contentious and veered off topic. What might you do to make the meetings more productive?
questions.option_a: Covering applicable human resources policies and procedures at the start of the meeting
questions.option_b: Providing individual training opportunities rather than using a group setting
questions.option_c: Canceling the meetings if they become counterproductive
questions.option_d: Reviewing the team charter with the project team
questions.explanation: The team charter establishes clear expectations regarding acceptable behavior by project team members. Discussing areas such as codes of conduct, communication, decision making, and meeting etiquette allows team members to discover values that are important to one another. The team charter can be reviewed and updated periodically to ensure a continued understanding of the team ground rules. Therefore, of the available options, reviewing the team charter with the project team is the best way to make the skill building meetings more productive.

questions.prompt: The project manager on a project to build a manufacturing plant is having trouble getting the attention of the company’s CEO, who is one of the project's stakeholders. However, the project’s architect who is designing the plant and works with the project manager on a daily basis is a close friend of the CEO. Should the project manager exploit the architect’s relationship with the CEO to open lines of communication?
questions.option_a: No. Exploiting the architect's relationship with the CEO is a violation of the PMI Code of
questions.option_b: Yes. The project's architect is part of the team and must cooperate and help manage
questions.option_c: Yes. There is nothing wrong with exploiting the architect's relationship with the CEO.
questions.option_d: No. The project manager should stop trying to get the attention of the CEO.
questions.explanation: Using other people to make a connection with a third party is fully acceptable. Networking is a powerful tool that can be used to influence the actions of stakeholders and increase support for the work and outcomes of the project, thus improving performance. Networking is an interpersonal and team skill that can be used among the tools and techniques of many project management processes, such as Manage Communications, the process implied by the scenario. In this situation, if making that connection requires using a subordinate, care must be taken that no one feels that there is some form of reciprocity required. The person in the middle cannot feel coerced, or that they will get favorable treatment for the action. That said, the project manager could ask the architect to help make the connection with the CEO.

questions.prompt: A complex project to develop the prototype for a new communication satellite is underway. The project team is preparing checklists and detailed traceability matrices to be used later in the project to determine if the quality objectives for the project have been achieved. Which of the following is the project team preparing?
questions.explanation: The question indicates that you are in the Manage Quality process. Test and evaluation documents are often used in complex projects with extensive regulatory or contractual requirements. According to the scenario, the project is complex, and it is reasonable to assume that there are extensive performance requirements. The numerous documents designed by the team to ensure that these requirements are met will be assembled in detailed test and evaluation documents as an output from the Manage Quality process and will be used later in the project as an input to the Control Quality process.

questions.prompt: A company is about to undertake a project that will use new technology. It is expected that the customer will request a very large number of changes throughout the entire project life cycle. The project sponsor indicated that delivering incremental value to the customer frequently while maintaining the cost baseline under control is of the utmost importance for the project. Which of the following would help the most in addressing the frequent changes?
questions.explanation: With the new technology used on the project, many changes anticipated from the customer, keeping the cost baseline under control, and the need to deliver incremental value frequently, selecting the right project management approach is essential. Such a project environment is best addressed using the combination of agile and predictive methodologies. As far as project scheduling is concerned, applying iterative schedule with a backlog to develop the product is a common agile practice. The requirements are documented in user stories that are then prioritized and refined just prior to their development, and the product features are built using time-boxed periods of work, typically called iterations. This approach is often used to frequently deliver incremental value to the customer, which is what was required by the project sponsor in the question. Adaptive methodologies are better suited to projects with frequently changing project requirements, such as in the scenario described. When using an adaptive life cycle, the time-boxed periods for releases, waves, and iterations are specified. Time-boxed periods are durations during which the team works steadily toward the completion of a goal. Therefore, of the choices provided, using time-boxing to address frequent changes and delivering value to the customer would help the most, making time-boxing the best answer to the question asked. Note, agile projects typically have a fixed budget. Therefore, when functional product increments are delivered frequently, the risk the project budget will be exceeded is low. The reason being is that when the project runs out of budget, the project can be terminated at any given moment, but the customer will still have the latest functioning product increment delivered in the most recent iteration. This is the most likely reason the sponsor indicated that the cost baseline should be kept under strict control.

questions.prompt: There are many projects under the management of one program. The program manager requests that before one of the projects wraps up that the project manager and the team review and analyze the relationships between project variables that may have contributed to the project's success. The program manager would like to apply those findings to other projects in the program. What technique are the project manager and team to use?
questions.explanation: The program manager is requesting the project manager and team to identify relationships between project variables to help predict, forecast, or understand outcomes in their project. Regression analysis does just that. If relationships are modeled, they could be used by other projects in the program to improve performance. Regression analysis estimates relationships between independent variables (predictor or explanatory) and a dependent variable (response or outcome). Knowing the connection between an independent variable(s) and a dependent variable lets the project team predict what might occur based on the interrelations. This information may help the program manager understand what project variables have contributed to the project's success in order to apply those findings to other projects in the program.

questions.prompt: You have been hired to take over a project in which the process of numerically analyzing identified risks has just been completed. As you are reviewing the risk register, you see this project has a lot of risks, and you are trying to quickly determine where to focus your efforts. You want to use a graphical representation to compare the importance and impact of risks. What tool would best meet your needs?
questions.explanation: The tornado diagram is a graphical display of a sensitivity analysis. The risks are ordered vertically from the highest degree of uncertainty to the most stable. This type of graph assists the project manager with determining which risks are most critical and need the most focus.

questions.prompt: Your project is installing WiFi stations in rural areas. The response rate for the surveys sent out to residents in the affected areas has been extremely low. Expectations were to have a majority of them returned by this point in the project. What most likely is the reason for this issue?
questions.option_a: This possibility was not entered as an identified risk in the risk register.
questions.option_b: There was not enough budget for the WiFi stations.
questions.option_c: There is a misunderstanding of the culture of the affected stakeholders.
questions.option_d: The stakeholders do not have WiFi internet access.
questions.explanation: When executing a project, a project manager and the project team must utilize various skills. Being culturally aware in order to communicate effectively with stakeholders is one of those skills. When increasing stakeholder engagement, one must consider cultural differences, preferences, and communication styles of stakeholders. The low response rate from the affected stakeholders may be a result of the poor cultural awareness and carrying out of the project plans, making it the most likely reason for the issue described in the scenario. Note, the question does not imply the residents of the rural area are unaware of the technology. It's the project manager who is unaware of the culture of the residents.

questions.prompt: To ensure that units produced by a new manufacturing line meet customer specifications, a project team has been requested to conduct attribute sampling on the first 100 units from the line. What should the team do to comply with this request?
questions.option_a: Ensure that the correct number of units was produced
questions.option_c: Check the existence of a specific characteristic in each sample unit
questions.explanation: The scenario suggests the project team is about to carry out the Control Quality process. Depending on the project and the industry, the project team may need a working knowledge of statistical control processes to evaluate data contained in the outputs of this process. Attribute sampling is a method of measuring quality that consists of noting the presence (or absence) of some characteristic (attribute) in each of the units under consideration. The scenario does not specify what attribute should be tested. However, to answer the question correctly, this information is not required. Basic understanding of attribute sampling may help in selecting the correct answer choice: to comply with the request of conducting attribute sampling, the team should check the existence of a characteristic in each unit.

questions.prompt: Your project team is about to begin the adjourning stage of team development. How can you best support your team during this process?
questions.option_a: Reward the team with a celebration meeting and ensure that the team members are
questions.option_b: Conduct a kick-off meeting so the team can learn about the project and their roles and
questions.option_c: Provide support as needed so the team can continue to function as a well-organized unit
questions.option_d: Utilize conflict management skills to ensure the environment does not become
questions.explanation: The question is in reference to the Tuckman ladder, which is one of the models used to describe team development. This model includes five stages of development that teams may go through including forming, storming, norming, performing, and adjourning. During the adjourning phase of team development, the team completes the work and moves on from the project. This stage is when staff is typically released as deliverables are completed or as part of the Close Project or Phase process. Therefore, of the available choices, rewarding the team with a celebration meeting and ensuring that the team members are reassigned is the best way for you, as the project manager, to support the project team with the adjourning stage of team development.

questions.prompt: You are leading a meeting with a group of stakeholders to prioritize the product requirements for your project. After several rounds of voting, no prioritized ranking of product requirements attains more than 50% of the votes. With the meeting at an impasse, what is your best course of action in this scenario assuming you don't want to decide for the group?
questions.option_a: Continue the meeting until unanimity can be reached
questions.option_b: Allow the majority to determine the prioritization of the requirements
questions.option_c: Permit the plurality of voters to prioritize the requirements
questions.option_d: Use autocratic decision making to set the priority of the requirements
questions.explanation: You, as the project manager, are using the decision-making technique of voting to determine a prioritized ranking of product requirements. Without being able to establish a majority for the requirements ranking, settling for a plurality may be the only viable option at this point. In the plurality decision-making technique, the largest voting block in the group decides even if a majority is not achieved. Permitting the plurality of voters to determine the prioritized ranking of product requirements will appease the greatest number of stakeholders participating in the meeting. Therefore, of the available options, the best course of action is to permit the plurality of voters to determine the ranking of product requirements.

questions.prompt: You are managing a multi-phase project to design and build a new attraction for an amusement park. During the build phase, the project manager learns that a permit that was required before the start of the build phase had been denied in the design phase and all work must stop until this issue is resolved. What did the project manager likely miss?
questions.option_a: Nothing, the project manager was informed too late, and it wasn't his fault this was not
questions.option_b: Holding a phase gate review meeting at the end of the design phase
questions.option_c: Allocating sufficient contingency reserves to allow for such issues if they arise
questions.option_d: Including permitting experts as key stakeholders on the project team
questions.explanation: A phase gate, sometimes called phase review, stage gate, kill point, phase entrance or phase exit, is held at the end of a phase. The project's performance and progress are compared to project and business documents. A decision, such as go/no-go, is made based on this review to continue to the next phase, continue to the next phase with modification, end the project, remain in the phase or repeat the phase or element of it. The question indicates that a project should have been stopped before allowing the build phase to start when a key requirement, the approval of the permit had been achieved. Had the project manager held the phase gate review, the information about the denial of the permit would have been communicated to the project stakeholders and a decision not to proceed would have been made.

questions.prompt: You are a project manager who is currently in the closing stage of a project. You want to make sure that the project objectives are met before considering the project closed. Which of the following should be reviewed before declaring the project closed?
questions.explanation: The question states you are carrying out the Close Project or Phase process. All of the answer choices describe inputs to this process. However, only the project management plan contains the three baselines (scope, schedule, cost) against which project performance is measured. The project management plan, including its subsidiary plans and the baselines, are updated throughout the project to include the latest understanding of project objectives and project approval requirements. Therefore, among the choices provided, to make sure that the project objectives are met before considering the project closed, you should review the project management plan.

questions.prompt: In your organization, the Manage Quality process has been a weakness in past projects. You want to ensure that this process is implemented properly on your current project. On which of the following would you focus the most to achieve this goal? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Design an optimal, mature product and build confidence that future outputs will meet specified
questions.option_b: Confirm that quality processes are used in a consistent manner and maximize project profitability
questions.option_c: Design an optimal, mature product and build confidence that future outputs will exceed specified
questions.option_d: Improve the effectiveness of processes and confirm that quality processes are used by a majority
questions.explanation: The four goals of the Manage Quality process are: ● Design an optimal and mature product by implementing specific design guidelines, ● Build confidence that a future output will be completed in a manner that meets the specified requirements and expectations, ● Confirm that the quality processes are used and that their use meets the quality objectives of the project, and ● Improve the efficiency and effectiveness of processes and activities. Maximizing profits and exceeding expectations may be corporate goals outside the project, but neither are project management goals.

questions.prompt: You are leading a project in a small startup company with decentralized purchasing. A supplier informs you that they have excess inventory that can be purchased at a reduced price. You review the project's inventory needs and find that most of the excess inventory offered by the supplier can be used for the project. What is your best course of action? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Refer the supplier to the project sponsor for procurement
questions.option_b: Negotiate and sign a procurement contract with the supplier
questions.option_c: Refer the supplier to the procurement department
questions.option_d: Refer the supplier to the project management office (PMO)
questions.explanation: Decentralized purchasing is found in smaller organizations or startup companies, and those without a purchasing, contracting, or procurement department. In such organizations, the project manager may assume the purchasing authority role to negotiate and sign contracts directly. For larger organizations with centralized purchasing, the actual procurement and contracting functions may be carried out by a separate department whose role is to purchase, negotiate, and sign contracts. In the scenario described, your company has decentralized purchasing. When a supplier offers you discounted goods, you have the authority to complete the purchase. Therefore, the next thing you should do is negotiate and sign the contract for the procurement of the discounted supplies.

questions.prompt: A project has been running smoothly and is well ahead of schedule. Despite a forecast which indicates that the project will finish within budget, the available capital for the project has been exhausted, and the project activities must be suspended. What might have prevented this situation? Top of Form
questions.option_b: Including sufficient contingency reserves in the cost baseline
questions.option_d: Borrowing at a fixed interest rate rather than a variable rate
questions.explanation: A funding limit reconciliation is a process of comparing the planned expenditure of project funds against any limits on the commitment of funds for the project to identify any variances between the funding limits and the planned expenditures. The expenditure of funds should be reconciled with any funding limits on the commitment of funds for the project. A variance between the funding limits and the planned expenditures will sometimes necessitate the rescheduling of work to level out the rate of expenditures. This is accomplished by placing imposed date constraints for work into the project schedule. In this scenario, the project is running well ahead of budget, which suggests that project funds are being expended faster than anticipated. Project funding often occurs in incremental amounts. While the project budget may be adequate for the project, if capital is expended faster than the rate it is being provided for the project, the project could run out of 'available' capital. Therefore, performing a funding limit reconciliation might have prevented this situation.

questions.prompt: The third iteration of the project has started. The team begins every day with a 15-minute stand up meeting. What is the primary value in such an arrangement? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Forces the team members to be at the office at the beginning of each working day
questions.option_b: Aligns the iteration with overall project road map to ensure appropriate velocity
questions.option_c: Assists the team to communicate evolving details, needs, and status with greater frequency
questions.option_d: Aids the team in assigning resources and responsibilities to the work scheduled for the day
questions.explanation: Stand up meetings are a valuable element of any project's communication plan. Regardless of the determined frequency, length, and structure, conducting a reoccurring meeting amongst project stakeholders can increase communication about issues, evolving trends, status, and many other relevant discussions. Typically, in agile projects, these meetings are held daily for a timeboxed 15-minute time period and conducted standing up in a physically shared space.

questions.prompt: You are preparing to perform multiple iterations of your quantitative cost-schedule risk analysis to evaluate the effects of individual project risks and other uncertainty sources to determine a range of probable outcomes and risks for your project. What analysis type will you use? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The Monte Carlo analysis uses iterative analysis to simulate the likelihood of a particular outcome or risk associated with the project. Computer software is typically used to simulate project activity several thousand times using random input for each iteration resulting in a range of possible outcomes. This allows project managers the ability to evaluate and address risks based on the probability distribution.

questions.prompt: As a new project manager, you have just been assigned to a project and asked by the project sponsor to create a draft of the project charter. The company has a project management office (PMO), which is best described as supportive. Unsure of the format the project charter should take, what should you do first? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Since every project is unique, you should create the project charter from scratch
questions.option_b: Download a project charter template from the Internet
questions.option_c: Ask the PMO to prepare the project charter
questions.explanation: The question states that you, as the project manager, need to create a project charter and are unsure of the correct format. You should search for an approved version of a project charter template that can serve as a starting point for the development of the project charter. Your organizational process assets, e.g., the organizational knowledge bases would be a good place to search for an approved version of a project charter template.

questions.prompt: A project manager is working with the team to ensure that all open issues are resolved and all deliverables have been accepted by the customer. What process is the project manager conducting? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The question indicates that the project manager is ensuring all issues have been resolved and the deliverables have been accepted. Accepted deliverables are an output from the Validate Scope process, and the formal documentation of acceptance is forwarded to the Close Project or Phase process. The project manager then verifies that all deliverables have been accepted as part of project closure. Additionally, during project closure, the project manager reviews the issue log to ensure that there are no open issues. Therefore, of the available choices, the Close Project or Phase process is the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: A project team is identifying and documenting the actions necessary to produce the project deliverables. Which of the following may be created as a result of this process? Top of Form
questions.explanation: Define Activities is the process of identifying and documenting the actions necessary to produce the project deliverables. In this process, work packages are broken down into schedule activities that provide a basis for estimating, scheduling, executing, monitoring and controlling project work. The question reads, "Which of the following may be created as a result of this process?" In other words, the question is asking to identify an output from the Define Activities process. Of the choices provided, only the milestone list is one of the outputs of that process and is, therefore, the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: The sponsor informs you that the project charter you developed has been approved. What should be your top priority at this point? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The fact the project charter has just been approved implies the project is in initiation. Unless stated otherwise in the scenario, the next logical step is stakeholder identification. Successfully identifying, analyzing, and engaging stakeholders and effectively managing their expectations and participation throughout the project life cycle is critical to project success. The ability of the project manager to correctly identify and engage stakeholders in an appropriate way can mean the difference between project success and failure. To increase the chances of success, the process of stakeholder identification should commence as soon as possible after the project charter has been approved and the project manager assigned. In the scenario described, the project charter that was developed by the project manager has been approved. Therefore, of the choices provided, Identifying project stakeholders is the top priority for the project manager.

questions.prompt: A team leader informs the project manager that she does not have enough resources to fix a defect by the deadline. In what project document would a project manager first record the lack of resources? Top of Form
questions.explanation: A lack of resources is an issue that should first be recorded in the project's issue log. The issue log is used to identify issues such as lack of resources, delays in supplies, low grades of raw material, or any other condition or situation that may have an impact on the project objectives. Corrective action as a result of a lack of resources may include updates to project management plan or project documents, such as a risk register, physical resources assignments, and project team assignments. Therefore, among the choices offered, capturing the lack of resources in the issue log is the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: A project manager has gathered and reviewed the inputs necessary to begin the development of the scope management plan for a project to build a new residential complex. What is the best course of action for the project manager to complete this process? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Analyze stakeholders using the stakeholder engagement assessment matrix
questions.option_b: Schedule a meeting with subject matter experts who have knowledge in construction
questions.option_c: Define the actions and activities necessary to satisfy the exit criteria for the project
questions.option_d: Have the procurement department send the bid documents to prospective sellers
questions.explanation: The question suggests that the Plan Scope Management process is being performed with the development of the scope management plan. Two of the tools and techniques associated with the Plan Scope Management process include expert judgment and meetings. Expertise should be considered from individuals or groups with specialized knowledge or training in the industry, discipline, or application area relevant to the project. For example, in the scenario provided, the project is supposed to build a new residential complex, implying construction is the relevant industry. Therefore, holding a meeting with subject matter experts who have knowledge in construction covers both tools and techniques mentioned above, expert judgment applicable to the project and meetings. Therefore, of the available options, to complete the Plan Scope Management process process described in the scenario, scheduling a meeting with subject matter experts who have knowledge in construction is the best course of action for the project manager.

questions.prompt: A project manager is about to start the development of the project charter and needs to identify the high-level assumptions and constraints as part of this process. How might the project manager obtain this information? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Review the assumption and constraints log
questions.option_b: Conduct interviews with the project sponsor and other relevant stakeholders
questions.option_d: Perform the Qualitative Risk Analysis process since the assumption log is an output of this process
questions.explanation: The question states that the project manager is about to start the development of the project charter, which implies that the Develop Project Charter process is just getting started. Interviews, which is a data gathering technique, may be used during the Develop Project Charter process to gather information on high-level requirements, assumptions or constraints, approval criteria, and other information from stakeholders by talking to them directly. Therefore, of the available choices, conducting interviews with the project sponsor and other relevant stakeholders is the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: You need to hire one contractor. You are leaning toward one who has an 80% chance of delivering in one month with a cost of $5,000, and a 20% chance of delivering in two months with a cost of $10,000. Another contractor has a 90% chance of delivering in one month with a cost of $6,000, and a 10% chance of delivering in two months with a cost of $12,000. What technique would you use to select the contractor? Top of Form
questions.explanation: Of the data analysis techniques used for the Perform Quantitative Risk Analysis process, only decision trees are used to support the selection of several different courses of action, each of which has potential opportunities and threats. The decision tree is evaluated by calculating the expected monetary value of each branch, which involves multiplying the probability by the impact of each chance and adding the results together. In this scenario, you have the quantified probabilities and impacts of the chances that two different vendors will deliver in one month or two. You can calculate the expected monetary value (EMV) of each vendor's risks, and select the vendor with the highest EMV.

questions.prompt: A company has been awarded a contract to design and build a new drone. The project manager is in the process of collecting product and project requirements. Which of the following actions is least likely to be performed during this process? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Determining if the performing organization has the infrastructure required to construct the drone
questions.option_b: Studying the scope baseline for detailed product requirements
questions.option_c: Researching knowledge gained from previous similar projects
questions.option_d: Reviewing the contract for any product or project requirements
questions.explanation: The question suggests that the Collect Requirements process is being conducted. The incorrect answer choices all represent actions that may be associated with inputs for the Collect Requirements process. Note that the question is asking for the activity which is 'least' likely to be performed during the Collect Requirements process. The scope baseline is the approved version of a scope statement, work breakdown structure (WBS), and its associated WBS dictionary. The scope baseline is created as an output of the Create WBS process which occurs after the Collect Requirements and Define Scope processes have been completed. Thus, the scope baseline is not yet available to serve as an input into the Collect Requirements process. Therefore, among the available options, studying the scope baseline for detailed product requirements is the 'least' likely action to be performed as part of the Collect Requirements process.

questions.prompt: According to the original plan for a project, the first two activities (Select Site and Negotiate Lease) should have been 100% complete, and the third activity (Perform Civil Works) should have been 50% complete at this point in the project. However, only the first two activities have been completed, and no work has been done on the third activity. The method used to determine the earned value is based on percent complete. Based on the following Gantt chart, what is the project's current CPI? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The cost performance index (CPI) is earned value (EV) divided by actual cost (AC): CPI = EV / AC. The EV is the sum of the planned value of the completed work. In the scenario described, the first two activities with $5,000 value have been completed so far in the project, so the EV = $5,000 + $5,000 = $10,000. The actual cost for each activity is provided in the chart and can be calculated as the sum of the actual costs of the first two activities valued at $6,000 each, so the AC = $6,000 + $6,000 = $12,000. Note, since the scenario mentions that the method used to determine the earned value is based on percent complete, the earned value of a partially completed work package is calculated by multiplying the planned value of the work package by its the percent complete. The calculations are as follows: CPI = EV / AC EV = EV of activity 1 + EV of activity 2 = $5,000 + $5,000 = $10,000 AC = AC of activity 1 + AC of activity 2 = $6,000 + $6,000 = $12,000 CPI = EV / AC = $10,000 / $12,000 = 0.83 Therefore, the correct answer for CPI is 0.83.

questions.prompt: You are managing a project with extensive regulatory oversight. The project is behind schedule, and a team member has proposed a way to automate the key inspections of the deliverable, saving considerable time and likely putting the project back on schedule. Unfortunately, only one team member is certified to run the automated test equipment, and certifying additional people will take a long time. What should you do? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Reject the automation option because it is unacceptable to add risk to a project in a highly
questions.option_b: Exploit the opportunity by taking advantage of the automation option, update the risk register,
questions.option_c: Get the project back on schedule by requiring the inspection team to increase productivity by
questions.option_d: Inform the sponsor that the organization has failed to provide enough key staff and that the
questions.explanation: The scenario implies that the Manage Quality process is being performed. Your team has identified a way to exploit the inspection issue by introducing automation. However, this introduces a secondary threat posed by having a single certified operator. The project manager should handle this like any other risk: record it in the risk register, determine the best response option, and move forward proactively.

questions.prompt: A client has just rejected the deliverables stating that they do not meet the intended need. The project manager realizes that the customer's requirements were not clearly understood. How could the project manager have avoided this situation? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Conducted an inspection of the deliverables during the Validate Scope process
questions.option_b: Validated the deliverables before they are verified
questions.option_c: Conducted the Validate Scope process before the Control Quality process
questions.option_d: Collaborated with a business analysis professional
questions.explanation: Eliciting, documenting, and managing stakeholder requirements takes place within the Project Scope Management processes. Trends and emerging practices for Project Scope Management include a focus on collaborating with business analysis professionals to elicit, document, and manage stakeholder requirements to meet project objectives. When a business analyst is assigned to a project, requirement-related activities become the responsibility of that role. Utilizing the expertise of a business analyst, in this scenario, most likely would have created better alignment between the project requirements and the business need for the client. The incorrect answer choices represent actions, which would not have influenced how the requirements were gathered. The scenario describes the Validate Scope process in action. In terms of sequence, typically the Control Quality occurs prior to Validate Scope. Control Quality has verified deliverables as outputs. Validate Scope in turn, utilizes verified deliverables as inputs and results in accepted deliverables or change requests in case defect repair or other changes are required. Therefore, of the available options, collaborating with a business analysis professional is the best response.

questions.prompt: An agile software development project consists of four releases, with features incrementally added with each release. Due to an excessive number of defects found by users after the first release, the risk response plan was implemented, which involved more rigorous testing by the project team. After the second release, the client has reported two bugs. What must first be done to determine the effectiveness of the implemented risk response? Top of Form
questions.explanation: Technical performance analysis compares technical accomplishments during project execution to the schedule of technical achievement. It requires the definition of objective, quantifiable measures of technical performance, which can be used to compare actual results against targets. Such technical performance measures may include weight, transaction times, number of delivered defects, storage capacity, etc. In this scenario, technical performance analysis should be used to compare actual results, such as delivered defects, against performance targets. The next logical step is to conduct technical performance analysis.

questions.prompt: A project manager is in the process of determining the budget for a new project that will acquire specialty parts from an overseas vendor to reduce the cost. Recently, the government of the country where the vendor is located announced a change in economic policy that involves sharply increasing their money supply to stimulate their economy. What should the project manager do in this situation? Top of Form
questions.option_c: Account for changes in the exchange rates
questions.explanation: Any significant changes in a country's economic policy would likely affect the currency exchange rates. As a result, the cost of goods bought from that country would also change. When relying on goods from abroad, the fluctuations of currencies need to be understood and built into the Determine Budget process. Therefore, in this situation, the project manager needs to account for changes in the exchange rates when determining the project budget.

questions.prompt: As part of the planning process, the project manager conducts a meeting with some of the key stakeholders. The purpose of the meeting is to identify the risks associated with the new project. The meeting is highly productive with all members making valuable contributions. At the conclusion of the meeting, a list of risks and potential risk owners has been identified. Where should the project manager capture this information? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The question describes the project management process of Identify Risks. The risk register is an output of the Identify Risks process. This document contains a list of identified risks along with the potential owners of those risks. The risk register is the document that is created as a result of the project meeting.

questions.prompt: With the project charter approved, you now need to identify the high-level strategic and operational assumptions and constraints and document them in the assumption log. What should you do? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Ask the project sponsor to provide the information
questions.option_b: Perform the Identify Assumptions and Constraints process
questions.explanation: High-level strategic and operational assumptions and constraints are normally identified in the business case before the project is initiated and is used to inform the development of the project charter. Lower-level activity and task assumptions are generated throughout the project such as defining technical specifications, estimates, risks, etc. The assumption log is used to record all assumptions and constraints throughout the project life cycle. Therefore, of the available options, you should check the business case to identify the high-level strategic and operational assumptions and constraints.

questions.prompt: A multiyear construction project is scheduled to purchase tons of steel over the course of the project. After the first year, a large tariff was unexpectedly placed on imported steel, sending prices up. The risk response plan is being implemented with little success. It is deemed that the procurement agreement needs to be revised to an FP-EPA contract. What would be the most likely resulting output of implementing the risk response? Top of Form
questions.explanation: As work is being done on the project, risks that become realized must be addressed. The Implement Risk Responses process addresses the risks. Sometimes, the response is not completely effective or results in requiring changes to components of the project management plan. In this scenario, the procurement documents, including the agreement, possibly need to change. Changes to an agreement are done using the contract change control system. Hence as an output of the process, a change request is required to revise the procurement agreements. The change request would then be reviewed as part of the Perform Integrated Change Control process. The FP-EPA (Fixed Price with Economic Price Adjustment) contract is a fixed-price contract with a special provision allowing for predefined final adjustments to the contract price due to changed conditions, such as inflation change or cost increases or decreases for specific commodities.

questions.prompt: You are thirteen weeks into a project that was planned to be completed in one year. From a schedule perspective, one-quarter of the work has already been completed. Based on earned schedule theory, what is your best course of action? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Submit a change request to revise the schedule baseline
questions.option_b: Continue to monitor progress to ensure the project stays on track
questions.option_c: Perform a reserve analysis to determine if there are adequate schedule reserves to cover the
questions.option_d: Crash the schedule to bring the schedule back into alignment with project goals
questions.explanation: Earned schedule (ES) is an extension of the theory and practice of earned value management (EVM), which is one of the trends and emerging practices in project cost management. Earned schedule theory replaces the schedule variance measures used in traditional EVM (earned value - planned value) with earned schedule (ES) and actual time (AT). Using the alternate equation for calculating schedule variance ES - AT, if the amount of earned schedule is greater than 0, then the project is considered ahead of schedule. In this scenario, the project was scheduled for one year (52 weeks), and one-quarter of the scheduled work has been completed which represents the earned schedule (ES) of 13 weeks. The actual time (AT) is presented as 13 weeks. The schedule variance is the earned schedule (ES) minus actual time (AT) or SV = 13 - 13 = 0. Thus, using earned schedule theory, the schedule variance indicates that the project is on schedule. The incorrect answer choices represent actions that might be taken if the project is running behind schedule. Therefore, of the available options, the best course of action for you, as the project manager, is to continue to monitor progress to ensure the project stays on track.

questions.prompt: A company runs dozens of types of projects covering a wide range of technical areas in multiple countries. They are losing track of who has worked on various projects and might have highly specialized knowledge that can be used in other projects. Which of the following would address this issue? Top of Form
questions.option_d: A centralized person responsible for knowledge management
questions.explanation: Some organizations assign a team or individual as a specialist in knowledge management. A centralized person or group responsible for knowledge management may allow the company to keep track of who in the organization has critical skills that can help on other projects as well as coordinate those activities. Video conferencing and face-to-face meetings may help identify those people and skills but would not address the long-term needs. A common change control process has little or no relevance to knowledge management. Therefore, of the available responses, having a centralized person responsible for knowledge management is the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: You have just taken over a project in the middle of the fourth of five phases. The project is running behind schedule which is attributed to poor performance of the project team. You want to improve their performance and avoid repeating past mistakes. What should you do first? Top of Form
questions.option_d: Meet with the team and ask for their input
questions.explanation: The question implies that you are about to carry out the Develop Team process. Since you are new to the project, it is best to familiarize yourself with any knowledge gained thus far in the project concerning team development. The lessons learned register is a project document used to record knowledge gained during a project so that it can be used to improve performance in the current project and entered into the lessons learned repository. The best source for understanding what has not worked well thus far in the project, so the mistakes are not repeated, is the lessons learned register. Note that you should also review the lessons learned repository in addition to the lessons learned register, to understand what has been effective and ineffective for past projects, but the lessons learned repository was not listed as an answer choice. Therefore, of the available options, examining the lessons learned register to avoid repeating past mistakes is the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: You are leading an infrastructure development project, which is about halfway complete. In accordance with the risk management plan, you hold regular risk review meetings. Which of the following are you least likely to do during this meeting? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Examine and document the effectiveness of risk responses
questions.option_b: Discuss the merits of a request submitted to modify the risk management plan
questions.option_c: Identify secondary risks that arise from risk responses
questions.explanation: Risk reviews are regularly scheduled meetings that examine and document the effectiveness of risk responses in dealing with overall project risk and with identified individual project risks. Risk reviews may also result in the identification of new individual project risks (including secondary risks that arise from risks responses), reassessment of current risks, the closing of risks that are outdated, issues that have arisen as the result of risks that have occurred, and identification of lessons to be learned for implementation in the current project or future projects. Risk reviews are commonly used as part of the Monitor Risks process. Keep in mind that the question is asking for the activity that is 'least' likely to be conducted during a risk review meeting. The merits of a change request submitted to modify the risk management plan is a topic of discussion that is more appropriate for a meeting of the change control board (CCB) than a risk review meeting. During a CCB meeting, change requests are examined to determine whether to approve, deny, or defer the request. Therefore, of the available choices, a discussion of the merits of a request submitted to modify the risk management plan is the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: A project manager is drafting a document which will formally authorize an expansive corporate restructuring project and needs to gather high-level information from various stakeholders. Due to the sensitive and confidential nature of the project, the project manager feels the stakeholders will speak more candidly if the discussions are conducted privately. What is the best course of action? Top of Form
questions.option_b: Schedule an interview with each of the relevant stakeholders
questions.option_d: Ask each of the key stakeholders to complete a survey
questions.explanation: The question implies that the project manager is drafting the project charter, the document which formally authorizes the existence of a project. Data gathering techniques that may be useful in the Develop Project Charter process include brainstorming, focus groups, and interviews. Interviews may help obtain information on high-level requirements, assumptions or constraints, approval criteria, and other information from stakeholders by talking to them directly, in a one-on-one setting. Since the question describes private discussions as the most appropriate approach, of the available options, scheduling an interview with each of the relevant stakeholders provides the best approach.

questions.prompt: You have tasked the project team with identifying and documenting the specific activities required to produce a quality door panel. The team's work will result in schedule activities that can provide a basis for estimating, scheduling, executing, monitoring and controlling the project work. Which of the following tools and techniques is least likely to be used during this process? Top of Form
questions.explanation: In the scenario, the project is in the Define Activities process where work packages are decomposed into schedule activities that provide a basis for estimating, scheduling, executing, monitoring and controlling project work. Decomposition, rolling wave planning, and expert judgment are tools and techniques used in the Define Activities process. The question is asking which of the answer choices is the 'least likely' to be performed during the Define Activities process. Since all of the answer choices except leads and lags are tools and techniques used in the Define Activities process, leads and lags is the correct answer.

questions.prompt: You are facilitating a meeting with several subject matter experts and the project team. The goal of this meeting is to obtain a comprehensive list of individual project risks and sources of overall project risk. The free-form style of the meeting is proving to be highly productive with active participation from all of the meeting participants. Which tool or technique is being used in this scenario? Top of Form
questions.explanation: Based on the scenario described, the project team is carrying out the Identify Risks process. Brainstorming is one of the tools and techniques used during the Identify Risks process. The goal of a brainstorming meeting during the Identify Risks process is to obtain a comprehensive list of individual project risks and sources of overall project risk. Brainstorming is the data gathering technique that is being utilized by the project team.

questions.prompt: During a demonstration of progress on the development of a new sales tool, a regional salesperson expresses concern that a requested feature was not part of the demo or the requirements. The project manager points out the exclusion of the feature in the project charter. What was the project manager trying to do in documenting the exclusion? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Trying to eliminate the features that the project manager did not like
questions.option_b: Pointing out areas where change requests would be accepted
questions.option_c: Defining the in-scope items of the project's next phase
questions.explanation: Project exclusions are recorded in the project charter to identify what is excluded from the project. Explicitly stating what is out of scope for the project helps manage stakeholders' expectations and can reduce scope creep. Project exclusions may be used for requirements in which stakeholders have expressed interest, but are explicitly excluded from the project for technical, business, or other reasons. Therefore, of the choices available, preventing scope creep is the best response.

questions.prompt: A website refresh was supposed to be released in tandem with another project’s deliverable. However, that deliverable is now three weeks behind schedule. The risk response plan was updated a few months ago to address this possibility, though some project team members now feel that the delay does not impact the website refresh as once thought. What is the best course of action for the website refresh project team? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Re-evaluate the risk and modify the planned response if needed
questions.option_b: Implement the risk response plan immediately
questions.option_c: Submit a change request to the change control board
questions.option_d: Avoid the risk by moving forward in the project as scheduled
questions.explanation: When risks are identified and planned for/against, there are plenty of unknowns. Risks are uncertainties. Once a potential risk is realized, that risk is no longer uncertain but certain. That realized risk may or may not differ from expectations. Verifying to confirm the risk is what was expected and validating that the planned risk response is still the best option as it increases the chances that the implemented response is effective and appropriate. Some modification could be needed in light of new information, variables, and conditions.

questions.prompt: The company has been sold. Consequently, there is a major shift in direction and strategy. A multiphase project has been canceled during execution of its final phase, and no more budget or work is to be spent on the project. The PMO has assigned the project manager to manage another project. Before moving over to the other project, what must the project manager do? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Communicate the status of the project to the team and produce a final report
questions.option_b: Gain senior managers' approval to manage another project
questions.option_c: Procure the rest of the items on the contract agreement with the supplier
questions.option_d: Complete the final phase of the project
questions.explanation: When a project is terminated prematurely, then all the stakeholders must be made aware of the situation and the remaining activities. It is extremely likely that the project sponsor(s) are already aware of the project's closure. Nonetheless, they should be provided a final report of what has occurred up to this point of the project and other relevant information. The project team and other stakeholders should also be communicated with, and other closing actions should be performed.

questions.prompt: After a thorough inspection, the team member is about to mark a finished product deliverable as passing verification. However, a customer executive passes by, examines the product, and starts angrily screaming how its dials only go to '10' when they were supposed to go to '12'. The team member looks up the specification, which indicates the maximum number on the dial should be '10'. What should the team member do next? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Reject the deliverable and send it to rework
questions.option_b: Mark the deliverable as passing verification
questions.option_c: Do nothing and escalate the verification to a manager
questions.option_d: Tell the customer executive to stop screaming and that the customer executive is wrong
questions.explanation: The scenario describes a team member who is conducting inspections as part of the Control Quality process. The team member's job in this situation is to ensure the deliverable conforms to the specification. With an angry stakeholder, an appropriate proactive approach would have been for the team member to listen, try to calm the executive down rather than risk further aggravating the situation by saying outright that the customer executive is wrong. Perhaps the team member can note the customer executive’s concerns and explain the Validate Scope process. However, such an approach was not provided as an option. Based on the information in the scenario, the finished product deliverable conforms to the specifications, and therefore, it is best that the team member mark the deliverable as passing validation. The customer executive would have the chance to formally reject the deliverable and provide reasons for the rejection. If necessary, there may need to be a change request process required if there is a genuine need for the dials to go up to '12'. There is not many details and information on these 'dials'. However, this information is not required to select the correct answer. Prospective PMP aspirants have to keep in mind that questions on the real exam can be short and vague. In such situations, we recommend making reasonable assumptions based on the information available in the scenario and, of the choices given, selecting the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: To complete some of the project activities, the project manager supplements the physical resources assigned to the project with those outside the control of the project team but available from within the organization. What should the project manager do? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Share the resource usage reports with project stakeholders
questions.option_b: Conduct an assumption and constraint analysis
questions.option_c: Send the procurement statement of work to prospective sellers
questions.explanation: The question suggests that the project manager has performed the Acquire Resources process. Enterprise environmental factors are conditions, not under the immediate control of the team, that influence, constrain, or direct the project. Enterprise environmental factors that may be updated during the Acquire Resources process include resource availability within the organization and the amount of the organization's consumable resources that have been used. Company resources are generally not under the immediate control of the project team until they have been assigned to the project. Thus, the necessary supplemental resources in this scenario are considered enterprise environmental factors. Therefore, of the available options, updating the enterprise environmental factors is the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: You have just performed a variance analysis for your project and determined that the CPI is 0.9 and the SPI is 1.2. This project is considered high profile, and it is critical that you meet all project objectives. Based on the variance analysis, what should you do next? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Reduce the product features to bring costs back into alignment with the project budget
questions.option_b: Crash the schedule to bring the schedule in line with project objectives
questions.option_c: Conduct further research to isolate and understand the variance from the cost baseline
questions.option_d: Release one of the developers to reduce costs
questions.explanation: The question states that the cost performance index (CPI) is 0.9 and the schedule performance index (SPI) is 1.2, which indicates that the project is running over budget and ahead of schedule. Thus, you, as the project manager need to ensure that costs are brought back into alignment with the cost baseline by the end of the project. Having just performed the variance analysis, you should now seek to isolate and understand the variance in order to ensure the appropriate action is taken. For example, it is possible that the cause of the cost variance is the result of a one-time unexpected expenditure early in the project with recent trend analysis indicating that the project should finish within the cost baseline and no action is necessary. In any case, you should not take action until the issue is fully understood along with the impacts of any efforts to further control costs. Therefore, of the available choices, conducting further research to isolate and understand the variance from the cost baseline is the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: A project manager is in the process of developing cost estimates. Many risks have been identified on this project, and the project manager understands that cost estimates should account for the contingency reserves associated with these risks. What should the project manager do as part of this process to account for the cost of uncertainty? Top of Form
questions.explanation: This question involves two project management Knowledge Areas - Risk Management and Cost Management. The scenario suggests that the project manager is conducting the Estimate Costs process. Reserve analysis is an example of a data analysis technique that can be used in this process. Cost estimates may include contingency reserves to account for cost uncertainty. Contingency reserves are the budget within the cost baseline that is allocated for identified risks. Contingency reserves are often viewed as part of the budget intended to address the known-unknowns that can affect a project. Hence, the project manager should first perform a reserve analysis to determine the amount of contingency reserves needed to develop the cost estimates.

questions.prompt: A project produces pistons and connecting rods with extremely tight tolerances. The first few pistons were scrapped, as they were not within the limits specified by the contract. The identified risk response was the acquisition of equipment that could machine pistons more precisely. With the new equipment in place, what should be done next to determine the effectiveness of the risk response? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Make a test run to gather work performance information
questions.option_c: Produce a test batch to gather work performance data
questions.option_d: Produce all of the pistons required by the contract and then scrap any that fall outside the
questions.explanation: During project execution, work performance data may be produced which can then be analyzed as part of the Monitoring and Controlling Process Group to produce work performance information. Work performance data serves as an input to various monitoring and controlling processes including the Monitor Risks process. In this case, a batch of pistons must first be produced using the new equipment, and then measurements must be taken. This raw data represents work performance data. The work performance data can then be analyzed to determine if the new equipment can produce pistons with the required precision. In this manner, the effectiveness of the risk response can be determined. Therefore, of the choices offered, producing a test batch to gather work performance data is what you, as the project manager, should do next to determine the effectiveness of the risk response.

questions.prompt: You have recently taken over a project from someone who is on leave. You have concerns about scope creep on the project. You want to understand if there are any variances in scope and if the variance is within the threshold amount or if corrective or preventive action is appropriate. With which of the following should you compare the actual results to make this determination? Top of Form
questions.explanation: In order to control scope, the project manager uses the scope baseline to find variances to scope, which may represent scope creep. The scope baseline is the approved version of the scope statement, work breakdown structure (WBS), and its associated WBS dictionary, that can be changed using formal change control procedures and is used as a basis for comparison to actual results. The scope baseline represents the planned scope of the project. During the Control Scope process, the scope baseline is compared to actual results to determine if a change, corrective action, or preventive action is necessary. The incorrect answer choices are also inputs to the Control Scope process, but they are not the primary document used to determine if a variance in the project scope has occurred. Therefore, of the available options, the scope baseline is the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: A project has recently started the design phase and there are multiple change requests per week altering the requirements. The project manager feels there is a bigger issue to address. Which of the following would be the least logical approach in this situation? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Collect and analyze the change request data
questions.option_b: Suggest switching the project to an agile methodology
questions.option_c: Increase the length and cost of the project to accommodate the changes
questions.option_d: Raise a risk that if the changes continue at the current rate, the project is at risk of failing
questions.explanation: The question depicts a project that has numerous and frequent change requests, which then result in ever altering requirements. It is an issue that falls under the Control Scope process. The question alludes to a "bigger issue" but does not define what that problem might be. It would be reasonable to collect and analyze the change request data to gain a better understanding of what is driving the excessive change requests. Additionally, it would be a good idea to notify the stakeholders of the impact of all the requested changes. However, requesting a revision to the baselines is not a reasonable action until the "bigger issue" is clearly understood. It is likely that the issues driving the excessive change requests can be addressed without revising the baselines. Therefore, increasing the length and cost of the project to accommodate the changes is a premature action at this point and the 'least' logical approach to addressing the problem described in this scenario.

questions.prompt: You are leading a project to build a production line for the engine parts in a new electric vehicle. The inspection of the parts created by the initial run of the line has resulted in an unacceptably high failure rate. However, an audit conducted by an independent quality team reports that it is the inspection routine that was flawed and that almost all of the questionable parts do in fact meet the specifications. What is your next step? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Meet with the production line design team to determine what changes need to be made to the
questions.option_b: Update the quality management plan to remove the flawed inspection routine
questions.option_c: Meet with the quality team to determine what changes are required in the quality management
questions.option_d: Update the scope, schedule and cost baselines to incorporate an improved inspection routine
questions.explanation: The scenario implies you are carrying out the Manage Quality process. The problem identified by the audit is not the production line, but the inspection routine. Therefore, changes will be needed in the quality management plan. Before you can initiate changes, you first need to determine what those changes should be, and the team will be the best resource to accomplish this. Then you can use the Perform Integrated Change Control process to update the appropriate plans and documents.

questions.prompt: You are in the process of collecting detailed requirements for a user interface (UI) redesign project. Your stakeholders include business subject matter experts (SMEs) as well as the software development team. Both groups have vastly different ideas on product requirements, and the interactions between the groups become tense. You want to build trust, foster relationships, and achieve stakeholder consensus during requirements collection. Which method would be best in this situation? Top of Form
questions.explanation: In this scenario, you have two groups of stakeholders who have a difference of opinion on the requirements of the redesigned UI, and yet you need to elicit requirements from them both. Facilitation is a technique that can be used in cross-functional workshops to define requirements and reconcile stakeholder differences. Well facilitated sessions can build trust, foster relationships, and improve communication among participants, which can lead to increased stakeholder consensus. While some of the answer choices provided are tools and techniques that can be used during the Collect Requirements process described in the question, under the circumstances presented, facilitation would be the best technique as it can assist with collecting requirements as well as build relationship among the stakeholders who have vastly different ideas on the product requirements.

questions.prompt: A project is running a month ahead of schedule partly because many of the identified project risks did not occur. The project is expected to finish within budget but does not have the money to pay vendor invoices upon its early completion. The project manager informs the vendors that their invoices will be paid later, on the originally planned project completion date. What is the most likely reason? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Risk register did not account for the positive risk
questions.option_c: The project schedule had too much buffer
questions.explanation: There is no indication that the project has overspent. In fact, the question states that the project is expected to finish within budget. The only other way the project could be low on capital is to have not gotten funding. Funding is typically allocated to a project in increments rather than the entire budget being made available at the start of the project. It is most likely that the funding requirements were not adjusted to account for needing the funds to be released earlier. Therefore, of the available choices, funding requirements is the most likely reason for the project to be unable to pay vendor invoices.

questions.prompt: While performing project work, a team member notices that there is a potential new risk, which could impact the project's critical path. What should the team member do immediately? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Recommend the project be placed on hold for a day to avoid the risk
questions.option_b: Document the risk in the risk register
questions.option_c: Draft an emergency fallback plan since the critical path is in jeopardy
questions.option_d: Generate a risk report using quantitative and quantitative analysis of the identified risk to alert
questions.explanation: By recording identified risks in the risk register, the project manager and team are able to keep track of all potential threats and opportunities. Furthermore, once a risk has been analyzed using qualitative and or quantitative risk analysis methods, the risk register serves as the central repository for planned risk responses and lists risk owners. As such, any time a new risk emerges on a project, the first step to take is to immediately document it in the risk register.

questions.prompt: As you prepare to gather requirements for a project you are leading, a review of the lessons learned repository suggests that improvements need to be made in the software development process. What is your best course of action? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Invite the development team to a project retrospective
questions.option_b: Schedule a JAD session with the development team and subject matter experts
questions.option_c: Review and update the requirements management plan as needed
questions.option_d: Analyze the project business documents and modify as required
questions.explanation: The question suggests that the Collect Requirements process is being performed. Joint application design/development (JAD) sessions are used in the software development industry. These facilitated sessions focus on bringing business subject matter experts and the development team together to gather requirements and improve the software development process. To efficiently conduct the JAD sessions, the project manager should use their interpersonal and team skills such as facilitation which is among the tools and techniques of the Collect Requirements process. The incorrect answer choices represent actions that cannot be completed based on the sole authority of the project manager or would not affect the performance of the current project. Therefore, of the available options, scheduling a JAD session with the development team and subject matter experts is the best course of action.

questions.prompt: A project to build a new bridge is nearly complete. A city council member tells the painting contractor to paint the bridge green instead of blue, but the vendor continues painting the bridge blue. The council member confronts the project manager and demands that the painting stop or payment will not be authorized. The project manager checks the change log and then with the CCB, but no information is available. What is the project manager's most likely next step? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Instruct the painting contractor to change the color
questions.option_b: Escalate the issue to the executive sponsor
questions.option_c: Use the change management plan to educate the city council member on the correct process
questions.option_d: Determine the cost of the change and ask the city council member to approve the change in cost.
questions.explanation: The question implies that the city council member went directly to the vendor to implement a change to the project's deliverable, rather than following the established change control process. The project manager needs to start by explaining the change request process to the city council member to avoid the issue from recurring. If the city council member still wants the color changed, then a change request needs to be submitted to the change control board (CCB) along with any supporting information including estimates for cost and time. Additionally, since it is reasonable to assume that the work is being completed as contracted, the city council member would not have legal grounds to withhold payment.

questions.prompt: On a construction project, the project manager finds that the framers are showing up too soon after the concrete foundation is poured. After a quick look, the project manager figures out that the curing time was accidentally left out of the schedule. What part of the schedule model has a problem? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The project management team selects a scheduling method, such as the critical path method or an agile approach. Then the project-specific data, such as the activities, planned dates, durations, resources, dependencies, and constraints, are entered into a scheduling tool to create a schedule model for the project.
