questions.option_a: Compile the lessons learned documents to hand over to the customer
questions.option_b: Transfer the lessons learned documents to a repository
questions.option_c: Distribute the printed lessons learned documents to the project team
questions.option_d: Publish the lessons learned documents to the end user's repository
questions.explanation: Lessons learned from the project are useful during the project and for future projects to learn from. As the project is closing it is important to collect and save these lessons for others to learn from. The most appropriate location for all across an organization to access and contribute to the knowledge base is in a repository. A repository managed by the project management office (PMO) is an example of a logical location.

questions.prompt: A project manager has just been hired to lead a project, which is already in the execution stage. Even though the project team members demonstrate strong social relations and collaborative working environment, the project manager wants to improve the performance of the team and provide the training necessary to achieve the project's objectives. Among the following, what should the project manager do first? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Evaluate the team members' current skills and competencies
questions.option_b: Provide one of the courses offered by the company
questions.explanation: The question does not provide information on why the project manager wants to improve the team performance. The question only suggests that the project manager is planning to carry out the Develop Team process. The question also indicates that the project manager is new to the performing organization, and, as a result, does not have a working knowledge of the project team's current skills and competencies. Team member skills, competencies, and specialized knowledge represent some of the enterprise environmental factors that may influence the Develop Team process. All of the answer choices describe actions which may provide some benefit; however, to identify training needs, the project manager should first seek to understand what skills and competencies currently exist with the project team members. With this understanding, the project manager is then equipped to look for those training and development activities which address the specific needs of the team members. Therefore, of the available options, before deciding on a team developmental strategy, the project manager should first evaluate the team members' current skills and competencies.

questions.prompt: A project manager recently lost some key subject matter experts due to a business reorganization, and most of the work on the deliverable needs to be subcontracted out. Which of the following best describes how project communications will be affected? Top of Form
questions.option_a: The formality of the communication will increase.
questions.option_b: The number of communication channels will increase.
questions.option_c: The communication needs to be more frequent.
questions.option_d: The number of communication channels will decrease.
questions.explanation: There are contractual agreements with subcontractors that require certain types of communication. Hence, the project team and the subcontractor are likely to engage in more formal communications. However, this arrangement does not necessarily result in more volume or frequency in communications. Nor does it imply a net change in the number of communication channels. Since the contract with the vendor will have significant legal implications, the communications will need to be more formal.

questions.prompt: The project was a tremendous success. The project exceeded expectations by coming in under budget and exactly on schedule. To celebrate the success, a party was announced and scheduled for the project team. The party will be financed from cost savings that have been added to the contingency reserves. But as the project is closing, the project manager and other stakeholders are feeling it is better to use the funds allocated for the party to shore up future business with the customer and appease the project sponsor. What should be done with the funds allocated for the party? Top of Form
questions.option_d: Spent on the party as planned
questions.explanation: Closing a project encompasses a lot of activities. One is to celebrate the hard work and success of the project team. If funds are available and allocated to an event to thank the project team for meeting their objective, then the project manager should honor that announced, scheduled, and promised event. According to the scenario, the cost savings have been added to the contingency reserves which is a common practice when opportunities are captured and resulting in cost savings. Celebrating success is an example of a meeting that can be used among the tools and techniques of the Close Project or Phase process described in this scenario. We are often asked about the practice of adding cost savings to contingency reserves. According to the reference provided, page 265, as work on the project progresses, contingency reserves may be used as planned to cover the cost of risk responses or other contingencies. Conversely, when opportunities are captured and resulting in cost saving, funds may be added to the contingency amount, or taken from the project as margin/profit. The scenario describes a project in which costs have been saved thanks to the project coming in under budget and exactly on schedule. According to the scenario, it has been decided to add funds (that represent these cost savings) to the contingency amount to cover the cost of the party. In other words, in this particular project, the decision has been made to add funds to contingency reserve rather than take them from the project as margin profit. And since these funds have been allocated for the party, spending them on the party as planned is the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: You are in the midst of project execution. You have identified new stakeholders and updated the stakeholder register with the identification, assessment, and classification information. What should you do next as part of this process? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Ask the project sponsor to add the new stakeholders to the project charter
questions.option_b: Submit a change request to update the project management plan
questions.option_c: Assign the new stakeholder as a risk owner for the risks on the watch list
questions.option_d: Perform an analysis of the stakeholders that you have identified
questions.explanation: The question suggests that you are performing the Identify Stakeholders process in the middle of project execution. The stakeholder register is created as an output of the first iteration of the Identify Stakeholders process and is updated as necessary as the project progresses. The question indicates that the stakeholder register has already been updated. In addition to updating the stakeholder register, the subsequent iterations of the Identify Stakeholder process may result in an update to various components of the project management plan such as the requirements management plan, communications management plan, risk management plan, and stakeholder engagement plan. With new stakeholders being introduced into the project, it is likely that each of these components will need to be revised. However, since the project is already in execution, an approved change request is required to modify the project management plan. Therefore, of the available choices, submitting a change request to update the project management plan is what you should do next.

questions.prompt: One of your project team members indicates that she is missing some project communications due to being left off of a group email distribution list. You document the problem and assign it to a responsible party. The following week the team member asks you for an update. What document should you check to find the name of the responsible party and a target date for resolution? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The issue log is a project document that can serve as an input to the Manage Team process. This document is used to record and monitor who is responsible for resolving specific issues and includes a target date for resolution. Therefore, the issue log is the document where you should find the name of the owner of a particular issue along with a target date for resolution.

questions.prompt: A project manager oversees a team consisting of a business analyst and three software developers. The team released a prototype to the customer for review and just finished gathering new requirements for the next project iteration. After reviewing the requirements documentation, the project manager discovers that some of the requirements were never documented. In this scenario, who would be the person responsible for gathering and documenting business requirements? Top of Form
questions.explanation: On projects that have an assigned business analyst, eliciting, documenting and managing stakeholder requirements is the responsibility of that role. The business analyst has the unique skill set and expertise to do the requirements-related work, whereas the senior programmers would likely be responsible for executing project deliverables to meet the business requirements. The project manager may work closely with the business analyst, especially on such a small project team, but is focused on overseeing the requirements management process and its integration with the overall project. The project manager in this scenario is fulfilling that responsibility by reviewing the requirements documentation, but it is the business analyst who is responsible for gathering and documenting the business requirements.

questions.prompt: You are managing a proof-of-concept project that is developing a new tunnel boring machine. A small-scale prototype has been designed and is undergoing a thorough test regime. During the tests, your team discovers that the original test equipment will have to be updated with new probes appropriate for the small prototype. This will substantially increase the cost of the project. What is your next step? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Have the team determine the full extent of the changes needed, and update the change
questions.option_b: Determine the full cost of new test gear, then find a way to hide the change by economizing in a
questions.option_c: Determine the cost of the new components, and submit a change request to update the cost
questions.option_d: Review the requirements traceability matrix to determine why the appropriate cutting tooth alloy
questions.explanation: The question implies you are carrying out the Manage Quality process. The change in cost is the specific impact highlighted in the scenario, so you need to address this proactively. First, the actual cost difference must be determined, and then you need to follow the project's change control process to modify the cost baseline. Ideally, the impact on all project constraints should be analyzed to determine if other baselines or configurable elements need to be updated. However, the scenario mentions only a cost increase. Therefore, of the available choices, determining the cost of the new components and submitting a change request to update the cost baseline is the best answer.

questions.prompt: A project manager is in the process of identifying and categorizing stakeholders for a project with a large and complex community of stakeholders and needs to determine the relative importance of the identified stakeholders. What is the best course of action? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Use a salience model to describe the classes of stakeholders
questions.option_c: Apply directions of influence to classify the stakeholders
questions.explanation: The question implies that the project manager is performing the Identify Stakeholders process. The salience model, an example of the data representation technique that can be used during this process, describes classes of stakeholders based on assessments of their power, urgency, and legitimacy. The salience model is useful for large complex communities of stakeholders described in the scenario or where there are complex networks of relationships within the community. The model is also useful in determining the relative importance of the identified stakeholders which is what the question is asking, making the salience model the best answer to the question.

questions.prompt: Services per the contract agreement with the vendor were delivered and signed off by the project manager. What else must the project manager do? Top of Form
questions.explanation: Any procurement needs that are to be fulfilled by an outside party must have all the procurement documents - e.g. agreements, contracts - formally closed once all services and/or products have been delivered and accepted by the project. Additional procurement closure policies and procedures may be outlined and defined in the procurement management plan.

questions.prompt: Your project team inspected a random sample of the work products. The resulting control chart showed that any process variance was within acceptable limits. After additional testing, the project team has reported that the deliverables have been verified. What should your team do next? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Create a scatter diagram and a Pareto histogram
questions.option_b: Benchmark the samples against your competitors' products
questions.option_c: Compare the work performance data to the scope baseline
questions.option_d: Ask the customer if the deliverables meet their acceptance criteria
questions.explanation: The scenario implies the project team has completed the Control Quality process which has resulted in verified deliverables as its output. After the deliverables have been verified internally, they become an input to the Validate Scope process where they should be formally accepted by the customer or sponsor. The customer or sponsor may inspect the deliverables, referring to the requirements documentation, the scope baseline, the contract, or the statement of work for requirements specifications. If the verified deliverables are found to be satisfactory, they are accepted and become accepted deliverables as an output of the Validate Scope process. Therefore, of the choices provided, after the deliverables have been verified, asking the customer if the deliverables meet the customer's acceptance criteria is the next logical step for the project team to take.

questions.prompt: To help improve the communication of a virtual team, the project manager deploys an online chat tool with channels for various topics that everyone uses while working. Often, all is quiet, but sometimes, there are sudden long streams of chat about many topics, both related and unrelated to the project work. The chat stream can be quite distracting, and it is often difficult to find important points that relate to work. What is the best course of action for the project manager? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Nothing. Online chat is a release for people getting to know one another.
questions.option_b: Eliminate the chat tool and instruct the team to use email and phone instead.
questions.option_c: Join in on the fun and encourage the team to keep on with this type of conversation.
questions.option_d: Request the team to use another channel for non-work related conversations.
questions.explanation: People need time to build relationships and learn about each other. There is a time a place for relationship building and providing a way for casual conversation is important. To avoid distracting the other team members, these off-topic conversations need to be incorporated in a manner that does not interfere with project work. Establishing a separate channel for topics unrelated to the project and politely asking the team members to use it, represents an appropriate balance between the project work and team building.

questions.prompt: You are new to the organization and have been given a few weeks to get yourself settled before starting your project in a role of a project manager. During this period, you realize that you have no authority over resources, the staff members are grouped by specialty, the budget is under the full control of those group's managers, and you as a project manager are only required to dedicate part of your time to perform the project work. What type of an organization are you working in? Top of Form
questions.explanation: Organizational structure can affect the availability of resources and influence how projects are conducted. In a functional organization, the project manager's authority over resources is little to none, the project budget is managed by the functional managers, and the project manager works on project tasks part-time. Other project characteristics in a functional organization include little to no resource availability and part-time project management administrative staff.

questions.prompt: You and your project team have recently discovered a negative risk to the project and determined that it could result in a loss of $50,000 if it occurs. You have also determined that the probability of this risk occurring is 50%. What is the expected monetary value for this risk? Top of Form
questions.explanation: You can calculate the expected monetary value of a risk by multiplying the impact amount by the probability of occurrence. You would use a positive impact amount if the risk is a positive risk (opportunity), and a negative impact amount if the risk is a negative risk (threat). In this scenario, EMV = -$50,000 X 0.50 = -$25,000.

questions.prompt: Your project team has just finished identifying potential project risks and is now scheduled to conduct a risk workshop to begin categorizing and prioritizing those risks and assigning risk owners. You decide to bring in a skilled facilitator to help lead the discussion. To enhance the effectiveness of the workshop, the facilitator asks you to bring a few key project documents to the meeting. Which of the following documents would be the least valuable to bring? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The scenario describes a risk workshop, which is a technique used in the Perform Qualitative Risk Analysis process. The risk register, stakeholder register, and assumption log are all project documents that can be considered key inputs for that process and, therefore, would be valuable resources to bring to the meeting. However, the milestone list is an input for the Perform Quantitative Risk Analysis process which typically follows qualitative risk analysis. Therefore, among the choices given, the milestone list the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: In an organization, project teams develop and follow their quality management plans. However, almost all quality activities are focused on the effective use of project processes with very little time spent on product design and process improvement. What should you recommend to improve the way quality is approached in the organization? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Focus on the quality assurance activities rather than on the Manage Quality process
questions.option_b: Focus on the Manage Quality process rather than on quality assurance activities
questions.option_c: Increase the budget allocated to team members for rewards and recognition
questions.option_d: Outsource all quality management related activities to external vendors
questions.explanation: The question implies that the project teams are performing the Manage Quality process. Manage Quality is sometimes called quality assurance, although in project management Manage Quality has a broader scope. Quality assurance is focused on the project processes and ensuring that they are used effectively. Manage Quality includes all the quality assurance activities but is also concerned with project design aspects and process improvements. According to the scenario, almost all quality activities are focused on the effective use of project processes, with very little time spent on product design and process improvement. This means only one area of the Manage Quality process is covered. Therefore, to help the company to improve project quality, you should recommend that their project teams cover all of these quality components by refocusing on the Manage Quality process.

questions.prompt: A project manager has been asked to draft a new project charter. The project manager has reviewed the existing agreements and studied the project business documents. What might the project manager do next? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Participate in the creation of the business case
questions.option_b: Schedule a meeting with key stakeholders to identify summary information
questions.option_c: Ask the project sponsor to formally authorize the project
questions.option_d: Meet with key stakeholders to identify project risks and develop individual risk responses
questions.explanation: The question states that the project charter is the document currently under development. For the creation of the project charter, meetings may be held with key stakeholders to identify the project objectives, success criteria, key deliverables, high-level requirements, summary milestones, and other summary information. The incorrect answer choices all represent actions that have either already been completed or do not contribute to the development of the project charter. Therefore, of the available options, scheduling a meeting with key stakeholders to identify summary information is the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: A project involves a diverse group of stakeholders and team members from different cultures all across the world. The project manager decides to use a social media tool to enhance communications on the project. However, this would require everybody to create an account for the tool to interact with others. What is likely to be the biggest issue that the project manager will face? Top of Form
questions.option_a: The cost of the tool may be so high that it would negate the benefits of using the tool
questions.option_b: The fact that everyone uses different computers and mobile devices for access
questions.option_c: Social media tools should never be allowed as a platform for project communications
questions.option_d: Some project stakeholders and team members would be reluctant to use the tool
questions.explanation: Social media can be an effective tool for communication among stakeholders. Although social media may be very prevalent in some regions, not all people embrace the use of social media, especially when the stakeholders come from different cultures all across the world as described by the scenario. Cultural awareness is an understanding of the difference between individuals, groups, and organizations and adapting the project's communication strategy in the context of these differences. While some of the project's stakeholders or team members may be interested in using the social media tool, the requirement to create an account for the tool may be challenging for others to get everyone engaged with the idea. Therefore, in this scenario, people's reluctance to use the tool will likely be the biggest issue for the project manager.

questions.prompt: You are currently in the process of determining and documenting the requirements for a project. During this process, which of the following activities are you least likely to conduct? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Glean any relevant information from the project charter
questions.option_b: Use the stakeholder register to identify stakeholders that can provide information on
questions.option_c: Gain an understanding of required, desired, and optional criteria for meeting the business needs
questions.option_d: Determine how requirements activities will be planned, tracked, and reported
questions.explanation: Collect Requirements is the process of determining, documenting, and managing stakeholder needs and requirements to meet objectives. Thus, the question implies that the Collect Requirements process is being performed in this scenario. However, the question is asking to select an activity which is 'least' likely to be conducted during the Collect Requirements process. The incorrect answer choices describe the inputs into the Collect Requirements process. The choice that describes how requirements activities will be planned, tracked, and reported is captured in the requirements management plan, which is produced as an output of the Plan Scope Management process and not the Collect Requirements process, making this choice the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: You are in the process of determining stakeholder needs and requirements for the project you are leading. There is a large number of stakeholders who are geographically dispersed, and you need their inputs quickly. What tool or technique would be the most effective in this situation? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The scenario implies you are involved in the Collect Requirements process. This process is performed to determine, document, and manage stakeholder needs and requirements to meet project objectives. Questionnaires and surveys are an example of a data gathering technique that can be used as part of this process. Questionnaires and surveys are written sets of questions designed to quickly accumulate information from a large number of respondents. Questionnaires and/or surveys are most appropriate with varied audiences when a quick turnaround is needed and when the respondents are geographically dispersed.

questions.prompt: You have just taken over a project in which some of the deliverables will be produced by external vendors. You want to familiarize yourself with those who are involved in the project from the sellers' side. Where would you look to obtain this information? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The stakeholder register is a project document that contains relevant information regarding stakeholder interests, involvement, interdependencies, influence, and potential impact on project success. Vendors are project stakeholders and as such should be included in the stakeholder register. The project manager and project team members should refer to this document when obtaining pertinent information about project stakeholders whether the stakeholders are internal or external to the performing organization.

questions.prompt: You are working with your project team to identify and document the specific actions to be performed to produce the project deliverables. Which of the following do you need before you can start this process? Top of Form
questions.explanation: To answer this question correctly, you should determine what process is being described. Identifying and documenting the specific actions to be performed to produce the project deliverables is the definition of the Define Activities process. The question asks, "Which of the following do you need before you can start this process?" In other words, the question asks which of the following answer choices represents an input to the Define Activities process. Of the choices provided, only the scope baseline is one of the inputs to the Define Activities process.

questions.prompt: A project manager and team lead meet with the customer to discuss project progress. The project manager notices that when the team lead communicates with the customer, she tends to mirror the customer’s body language. Nearly every time the customer folds their arms, sits back in their chair, or puts their hands together on the table, so does the team lead. What should the project manager do? Top of Form
questions.option_a: In private, tell the team lead stop mocking the customer
questions.option_b: Call this behavior to the team lead attention and apologize to the customer
questions.option_c: Do nothing. There is nothing wrong with the team lead's behavior
questions.option_d: Establish communication classes for the project team
questions.explanation: The question describes a communication concept called mirroring. When two people are communicating well, they will often subconsciously mirror the other person's behavior and body language. This action is typically a sign of a comfortable conversation. The project manager should do nothing in this scenario as there is no issue to correct. Note that "do nothing" is typically used as an incorrect answer choice, but this may not always be the case. In some circumstances, taking no action is the most appropriate approach.

questions.prompt: The software release update has been developed and is scheduled for the switch over to the live environment at midnight tomorrow. Per the agreement, the closing of the project requires knowledge sharing and end-user readiness in coordination with the product launch. Which of the following best represents these closing activities? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Communicating project status to the sponsor; issuing the final report; notifying the project team
questions.option_b: Training end users in the receiving organization; finalizing documentation; conducting final
questions.option_c: Backing up software files; uploading project documents to PMO repository; verifying all asset
questions.option_d: Communicating the project status to the end users; releasing the project team; verifying product
questions.explanation: As a project is transferring the ownership and operation of the project's product to another team or the customer, transferring knowledge is important. This transference is not exclusively collecting the knowledge and handing it over. It requires training, educating, and communicating the contents, organization, and purpose of the knowledge and associated wisdom to the receiving entity.

questions.prompt: A project manager goes into the monthly steering committee meeting and says the project has a cost variance of $25,378.22. Instead of being praised for running under budget, one of the executives at the meeting expresses frustration at the number. What is wrong with the variance? Top of Form
questions.option_b: The level of precision is not correct.
questions.option_d: The variance should be a negative value.
questions.explanation: The level of precision for costs is defined in the cost management plan and can be described as the degree to which costs estimates will be rounded up or down, based on the scope of the activities and magnitude of the project. Reporting a cost variance to the penny implies a level of precision that is not realistic, as the project's cost variance is constantly changing. Additionally, although the money spent might be obtained for any point in a project, the earned value needed to calculate the cost variance is more subjective. Therefore, providing a cost variance to the penny implies a level of precision that is not obtainable. In this case, it would be more appropriate to report the cost variance as $25,000. As an analogy, imagine if a scientist stated that a particular dinosaur bone was 100,000,127 years old. However, the test used to determine the age may only be accurate to a few million years. Stating the age to the exact number of years implies that the scientist has determined the age to an exact year when that is not the case, regardless of the test results.

questions.prompt: A project manager is drafting a charter for a software development project. In support of this process, the project manager has scheduled a brainstorming meeting to gather relevant information. Which of the following activities is the project manager least likely to conduct? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Inviting relevant stakeholders to attend the meeting
questions.option_b: Ensuring that a software engineer joins the meeting
questions.option_c: Arranging for a facilitator to lead the meeting
questions.option_d: Following the communications management plan to determine meeting attendees
questions.explanation: The question states that the project charter is being developed, which occurs during project initiation. Brainstorming is a data gathering technique which may be useful in the development of the project charter. Brainstorming is a specialized meeting led by a facilitator which can be used to gather data and solutions or ideas from stakeholders, subject matter experts, and team members when developing the project charter. Note, the question is asking for the activity which is 'least' likely to be conducted during the development of the project charter. The communications management plan is not created until later in the project during the Plan Communications Management process. Therefore, the project manager would not be able to follow the communications management plan in determining the meeting attendees, making this the 'least' likely activity to be conducted in this scenario.

questions.prompt: You are attempting to coordinate activities between your project, another related project, and operations, all of which are being managed as a group. A conflict has arisen over control of a key resource that is vital to both projects as well as operations. The conflict has reached an impasse and needs to be resolved by a higher authority. What is your best course of action? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Discuss the problem with the portfolio manager
questions.option_b: Escalate the issue to the operations manager
questions.option_c: Raise the issue with the other project manager
questions.option_d: Request help from the project management office
questions.explanation: The question describes a situation where a problem needs to be addressed by someone that is responsible for projects, programs, and operations being managed as a group. A portfolio is a collection of projects, programs, and operations managed as a group to achieve strategic objectives. Only a portfolio manager has direct responsibility for projects, programs, and operations. Therefore, discussing the problem with the portfolio manager is the best course of action in this scenario.

questions.prompt: A large and complex multinational project is underway with stakeholders of diverse cultures and ages. The project manager has experienced issues communicating with such a diverse stakeholder community. What is the best course of action? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Take a multifaceted approach to communication
questions.option_b: Rely on push forms of communication
questions.option_d: Use interactive communication for all project communications
questions.explanation: Multifaceted approaches to communication represent one of the trends and emerging practices in Project Communications Management. Multifaceted approaches to communication embrace and select from all technologies and respect cultural, practical, and personal preferences for language, media, content, and delivery. When appropriate, social media and other advanced computing technologies may be included. Multifaceted approaches such as these are more effective for communicating with stakeholders from different generations and cultures. The incorrect answer choices represent limitations in the methods of communication, which may not be appropriate for all stakeholders and circumstances. Therefore, of the available choices, the best course of action is for the project manager to take a multifaceted approach to communication.

questions.prompt: Due to the complexity and many diverse teams involved, there is confusion amongst the project stakeholders on where to find materials and information for the project. What is the best response to address this issue? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Assign one project team member to retain and distribute all the project materials and information
questions.option_b: Distribute all the project documents, materials, and updates to each stakeholder
questions.option_c: Require all stakeholders to attend every meeting to ensure everyone receives the handouts and
questions.option_d: Set up an information management system to store, share, and codify the project's knowledge
questions.explanation: As a project progresses, there can be a lot of information and systems - especially in a large, complex project with many stakeholders. The root issue in this scenario is the inability of stakeholders to locate the materials and the information they need. Building up the information management tools would assist the project stakeholders to access and locate the information needed or requested. All other options describe infeasible, counterproductive, or unmanageable responses.

questions.prompt: You are one of several project managers working for a construction company, and you are obtaining the equipment and supplies for a project to build a new convention center. The performing organization owns much of the construction equipment, which must be reserved in advance per company policy. Once you have confirmed the equipment's availability for your project, what should you do to ensure the equipment is available when you need it? Top of Form
questions.option_b: Reserve the equipment by updating the organizational process assets
questions.option_c: Modify the project schedule with the required equipment
questions.option_d: Record the equipment in the resource breakdown structure
questions.explanation: The question implies that you, as the project manager, are carrying out the Acquire Resources process. Organizational process assets that are updated as a result of the Acquire Resources process include documentation related to acquiring, assigning, and allocating resources. Depending on how company policies and procedures govern the assignment of company resources to projects, an organizational process assets update may be required to reserve a resource. Examples of organizational process assets that might serve this purpose might include an electronic sign-up sheet, an organizational level resource calendar which reflects all of the individual project resource calendars, resource management software, a master schedule of all projects, etc. The incorrect answer choices reflect project documents that only influence the project for which they have been created, and not the other projects being conducted by the performing organization. Therefore, of the available choices, reserving the equipment as an organizational process assets update is the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: You are managing a large project with several major deliverables with deadlines spread over time. Each deliverable has a distinct focus. The project involves different organizations, locations and skill sets. You anticipate that you will use processes from all five process groups. Once a deliverable is completed and handed over to the customer, a decision on whether to continue or terminate the project should be made. What approach would work best for this project? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Outsource each deliverable to a different vendor
questions.option_c: Use Agile as it works best for large projects
questions.explanation: When the nature of the work to be performed is unique to a portion of the project, and each portion can be linked to the development of a specific major deliverable, phased project approach can be used. Typically, dividing the project into phases works best when:  The work has a distinct focus that differs from any other phase. This often involves different organizations, location, and skill sets;  Achieving the primary deliverable or objective of the phase requires controls or processes unique to the phase or its activities;  The close of a phase ends with some form of transfer or hand-off of the work product produces as the phase deliverable. This phase end represents a natural point to reassess the activities underway and to change or terminate the project if necessary. The project characteristics described in the question are covered by the above points. Therefore, dividing the project into phases is the best answer among the choices given.

questions.prompt: Halfway through a project, the project sponsor left the organization, and a new sponsor was appointed. You meet with the project sponsor to bring her up to speed and ensure she has the appropriate level of support for the project. The new sponsor is excited about the project, and she decides to expand the project slightly. What should you do first? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Submit a change request to expand the existing project
questions.option_b: Terminate the existing project and initiate the expanded one
questions.option_c: Request additional funds for the project
questions.explanation: The question describes a situation in which the project sponsor is requesting an expansion of the project scope. In addition to the scope baseline, this change will likely impact the cost and schedule baselines as well. An approved change request is required to modify any of the project baselines. Additionally, even though the project sponsor wields great power and influence, the sponsor may not be the sole decision-maker. The configuration management plan will describe what approvals are necessary to approve the change request, but it is likely that others will be involved in this decision as well. Therefore, of the available choices, the first thing for the project manager to do in this situation is to submit a change request.

questions.prompt: A project manager is about to start the processes associated with the Initiating Process Group. The project manager needs to know who will be responsible for ensuring that the value to the project is preserved a year after the project deliverables are handed over to the customer. Where will the project manager find this information? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The question states that the project is about to start the processes associated with the Initiating Process Group. Hence, the only project artifacts that likely exist at this point are the business documents, which include the business case and the benefits management plan. To find out who will be responsible for value and benefits monitoring a year after the project provides the deliverables, the project manager needs to turn to the benefits management plan. The benefits management plan contains the details of the process for creating, maximizing, and sustaining the benefits provided by a project. Therefore, of the available responses, the benefits management plan is the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: A dashboard viewable by all project stakeholders displays various statuses using the industry standard of red, yellow, and green colors. Throughout project execution, to meet the needs of the project and some of the project stakeholders, the project manager has been adding more colors to reflect additional statuses. This situation has created confusion among some of the stakeholders over the status naming conventions and meanings. What should the project manager do?
questions.option_a: Revert the status to an industry standard of red, yellow, green
questions.option_b: Explain the meaning of the new statuses to the stakeholders
questions.option_c: Remove all the statuses from the dashboard to avoid confusion
questions.option_d: Add a simpler status for the stakeholders who are confused
questions.explanation: The Monitor Communications process ensures the project communications are fulfilling the needs of the project and its stakeholders. In this scenario, the statuses on the project dashboard have evolved over the life of the project to meet the needs of the project. In the process, it has met the needs of some of the project stakeholders but has confused others. The project manager must close that misunderstanding gap so that the communications meet the needs of all project stakeholders. Educating the stakeholders on the purpose, naming, and use of the statuses is the best option from those presented in the question.

questions.prompt: A product review to demonstrate what has been worked on during this first month is scheduled for Friday. Should the project manager communicate to the customer about this meeting and why or why not?
questions.option_a: Yes, the customer is a mandatory participant in every product review.
questions.option_b: No, it is too early in the project for the customer to see the product.
questions.option_c: Yes, the customer can join and provide feedback on the work in progress.
questions.option_d: No, only internal stakeholders should attend the product review.
questions.explanation: Managing the communications and communication strategy of the project includes getting the most effective and timely information passed between parties involved in the success of the project. An emerging practice that has become a best practice in agile approaches to project management is to regularly include the customer, product owners, and other appropriate stakeholders in the product reviews, retrospectives, and other project meetings. This tighter connection amongst stakeholders helps align the team with the product vision, roadmap, as well as the other needs and desires not explicitly communicated in the project charter, project scope statement, and other project plans and documents. Encouraging opportunities for dialog between customers and project teams builds and reinforces a communication bridge to support a better product; which in turn makes for a more successful project. Note, this question intersects with inspections which are among the tools and techniques of the Validate Scope process. Inspections are sometimes called reviews, product reviews, and walkthroughs.

questions.prompt: A project is chartered to install an IT data center in a foreign country where the political climate is highly unstable. To address the threat to the data center due to the political instability, the project manager has negotiated with the U.S. State Department to establish the data center in a high-security basement of the U.S. Consulate. This is an example of:
questions.explanation: This is an example of risk mitigation. Risk mitigation involves taking action to reduce the probability of occurrence and/or the impact of a threat. The project manager is taking action to reduce the probability and impact of a potential threat to her project by negotiating a more secure location for her IT data center. She is not avoiding or eliminating the threat entirely, nor is she shifting ownership of the threat to a third party. Therefore, risk mitigation is the best answer choice.

questions.prompt: Four developers have been acquired with a time and materials contract on a software development project. During project execution, the project manager notices that the developers work fewer hours than anticipated, resulting in the project to fall behind schedule. The project manager contracts two more developers, who help bring the project back on track. What should the project manager do after adding the contractors to the team?
questions.option_b: Invoke a disciplinary action against the four developers
questions.option_d: Revise the project team assignments documentation
questions.explanation: Manage Team is the process of tracking team member performance, providing feedback, resolving issues, and managing team changes to optimize project performance. To bridge the gap in project performance described in the scenario, the project manager has contracted two additional developers, thus changing the composition of the project team. When changes to the team are required, those changes are recorded in the project team assignments documentation. This document lists the team members and their roles and responsibilities for the project. Updates to the project team assignments are one of the outputs of the Manage Team process, making the revision of the project team assignments documentation the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: You are looking for the factors, constraints, and potential dependencies among project activities that can influence the way the activities are sequenced. You also want to understand the relationship between the activities and the need for leads and lags. Where could you find this information?
questions.explanation: The scenario implies that you are performing the Sequence Activities process. The assumption log, an input to this process, contains information that can influence the way activities are sequenced, as well as the relationship between activities, and the need for leads and lags. This information may result in the identification of new project risks that may impact the project schedule. Assumptions that affect activity sequencing may be specified in several places: the project charter, the WBS dictionary, the activity list, etc. The assumption log is used to centralize this information into one project document and capture all assumptions and constraints throughout the project lifecycle, making the assumption log the best answer to the question asked. The incorrect answer choices represent project artifacts that are not among the inputs to the Sequence Activities process described in the scenario.

questions.prompt: The project manager is trying to get sign off for a series of marketing materials. The work is rejected by the stakeholders responsible for sign off because of incorrect use of marketing standards. What should the project manager do before sending materials to the stakeholders again?
questions.option_a: Perform quality control checks before validating the scope
questions.option_b: Validate the scope before performing quality control
questions.option_c: Create a work breakdown structure before validating the scope
questions.option_d: Validate scope before communicating the project status
questions.explanation: The scenario involves two processes: the Validate Scope process and the Control Quality process. The two processes are "linked" by verified deliverables. Verified deliverables are an output from the Control Quality process and an input into the Validate Scope process. The Control Quality process ensures the project outputs are complete, correct, and meet customer expectations. The Validate Scope process formally accepts the completed project deliverables and results in accepted deliverables as its output. In the scenario, the work is being validated by the stakeholders, but there are quality concerns noted by the incorrect use of standards. Ideally, the Control Quality process should be completed before the scope can be validated. It is unclear from the scenario whether or not this process has been carried out at all. Regardless, now, when the issues have been found during scope validation, it is essential that before repeating the Validate Scope process for this work, the project manager and the project team conduct the Control Quality process to ensure correctness and proper adherence to the marketing quality standards. Then the scope can be validated based on both correctness and completeness.

questions.prompt: You are conducting the source selection analysis for a project that will procure services from a vendor. Since the value of the procurement is relatively small, you have decided that a full selection process does not make sense. With three prospective vendors listed as pre-approved sellers, what is the best course of action?
questions.option_a: Utilize a sole source for the procurement
questions.option_b: Use a firm fixed price contract
questions.option_c: Establish a qualifications only selection method
questions.explanation: Source selection analysis is one of the tools and techniques associated with the Plan Procurement Management process and is used to determine the appropriate vendor selection method. The qualifications only selection method applies when the time and cost of a full selection process would not make sense because the value of the procurement is relatively small. The buyer establishes a short list (represented by the three preapproved sellers in this scenario) and selects the bidder with the best credibility, qualifications, experience, expertise, areas of specialization, and references. Therefore, of the available choices, establishing a qualifications only selection method to select the vendor is the best course of action.

questions.prompt: The project has proceeded smoothly over the last six months. The project team is asking you to ease up on your authoritative style. What should you do?
questions.option_a: Recognize their request, but inform them that this is your style and it works
questions.option_b: Allow the team to make some of their own decisions and facilitate more
questions.option_c: Transition the role of the project manager to another person on the project team
questions.option_d: Maintain the tight control and steady progress of the project and the team
questions.explanation: Managing the project team requires a variety of management and leadership skills to fulfill project objectives and create high performing teams. Project managers need to understand the project needs, but also the needs of the project team. Adjusting one's management style and leadership style accordingly supports the project manager and team to meet those needs. There are many styles a project manager can employ. Experienced project managers apply different approaches at different times during a project. In this scenario, the project team members are requesting you to alter your style to help them and the project be more successful. The project manager needs to be sensitive and understanding of those requests and how their actions affect the team and the team's performance. Allowing the team to make some of their own decisions and facilitating more would likely address the needs of both the team and the project without compromising your authority, making this course of action the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: A project manager is facilitating a first brainstorming session to identify individual risks as well as sources of overall project risk. The meeting attendees are struggling with the task, and the meeting is becoming unproductive. What action might the project manager take to get the meeting back on track?
questions.option_a: Use the RBS as a prompt list
questions.explanation: The question suggests that the Identify Risks process is underway. Prompt lists represent one of the tools and techniques associated with this process. A prompt list is a predetermined list of risk categories that might give rise to individual project risks, and that could also act as sources of overall project risk. The prompt list can be used as a framework to aid the project team in idea generation when using risk identification techniques. The risk categories in the lowest level of the risk breakdown structure (RBS) can be used as a prompt list for individual project risks. Of the available options, using the RBS as a prompt list is the option that would most likely help the meeting attendees generate ideas for risk identification thus assisting the project manager in getting the meeting back on track. Note, the question indicates that the project manager and the team are involved in the 'first' brainstorming session to identify risks. This nuance has been added to the scenario to make sure that the risk register does not yet exist. Because otherwise, one may argue that Identify Risks as a singular event at the start of the project. That is not the case. Risks are identified frequently throughout the project. As such, there is a risk register that can be and should be referenced whenever the team is performing that process unless this is the first session as presented in the question.

questions.prompt: A project to construct a new subway line is underway, and the tunnel boring machine has slowed due to unexpected changes in rock composition. It has been decided to implement the planned risk response by increasing the speed of the machine to 100% of rated capacity. The team believes that the additional strain on the machine will now cause more downtime for its maintenance thus negating the benefit of faster boring. What should the project manager do first?
questions.option_b: Order another machine as a backup
questions.explanation: Control Resources is the process of ensuring that the physical resources assigned and allocated to the project are available as planned, as well as monitoring the planned versus actual utilization of resources and performing any corrective action as necessary. In this scenario, the resource availability may or may not be limited by additional downtime. This situation presents an additional assumption for the project. The assumption log is a project document used to record all assumptions and constraints throughout the project life cycle. According to the scenario, the project team believes that the additional strain on the machine will now cause more downtime for its maintenance thus negating the benefit of faster boring. This is an assumption. Assumptions should be captured in the assumption log. Therefore, of the available choices, revising the assumption log is what the project manager should do first.

questions.prompt: The project team is virtually spread all across the country. With an over-budget project nearing a deadline, emails are being sent so often that they are becoming hard to track, read, and respond to. What should the project manager do?
questions.option_a: Colocate the team to encourage in-person face-to-face conversations
questions.option_b: Review the lessons learned repository for a method that works better
questions.option_c: Submit a change request to update the communications management plan
questions.option_d: Stop using email until another solution is implemented
questions.explanation: In the Manage Communications process there are times during the project must adjust and adapt to accommodate the changing needs of the project and its stakeholders. In this scenario, the stakeholders - which are only the project team here - communication needs are not being met. There are too many communications coming from too many directions via one medium, email. The quick succession of emails is causing the team to be unable to receive and respond to the communications. The current method is not working and counterproductive. As such, the project manager and team can seek out alternatives or solutions to help them out in their time of communication need. Reviewing the lessons learned is an ideal starting point. The lessons learned may identify some worthy alternatives. Or it may identify alternatives that are not helpful or worth the effort. Or both. Or little direction at all. Nonetheless, the lessons learned repository and the information it contains could give them wisdom on how to potentially proceed. Based on those initial reviews, they can then apply solutions, research more, or continue the current method - even if less than ideal - if another solution is not deemed a greater benefit than the work and disruption it may require. One may argue the lessons learned repository should have been consulted during project planning when the communications management plan was under development. While it is true, the scenario suggests the issue with the email started recently implying it was not identified earlier in the project.

questions.prompt: The quality management has been perfect for the project up to this point. Suddenly, the quality assurance (QA) lead who developed and performed most of the quality activities for the project has been temporarily reassigned to help another project. What should the project manager do?
questions.option_a: Submit a change request to adjust the cost baseline and the schedule baseline
questions.option_b: Request the team to develop replacement quality activities
questions.option_c: Assign another team member to carry out the originally planned quality activities
questions.option_d: Pause quality activities until the QA lead returns to the project
questions.explanation: The scenario describes the Manage Quality process. This process is sometimes called quality assurance. Manage Quality is the process of translating the quality management plans into executable quality activities. These activities can ideally be performed by anyone on the team with the proper training, guidance, and tools. In this scenario, the tools and methods used by the quality assurance lead for the quality activities have been working well. Any data captured from the activities can be used and analyzed to report the status of the project quality and control the quality, as needed. It is important for the project to continue these activities as part of the Manage Quality process throughout the project. Unfortunately, the project can not use its current subject matter expert (SME) but should seek alternatives, such as reassigning another resource to the task(s). In any case, the quality activities should not cease or pause due to a resource change. Therefore, of the available options, assigning another team member to carry out the originally planned quality activities is the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: You have recently been assigned to lead a project that is halfway into execution. The project has many stakeholders, and you are trying to determine how to engage them effectively. Which of the following will best help you in this effort?
questions.explanation: The scenario implies you are performing the Manage Stakeholder Engagement process which is concerned with communicating and working with stakeholders to meet their needs, address issues, and foster involvement. One of the inputs to this process is the project management plan which provides guidance regarding stakeholder communications, risk management, change management, and stakeholder engagement. Specifically, the stakeholder engagement plan contains information on how to manage stakeholder expectations thus helping to determine how to engage the stakeholders effectively.

questions.prompt: You have a discussion with the finance department manager about the acceptable cost performance index range for the project you are leading. The manager argues that the current CPI of 0.9 is unacceptable and should be within +/- 5%, while you insist that an acceptable CPI range is +/- 10%. Where should you look to determine the acceptable metric?
questions.explanation: Among the answer choices given, only Quality Metrics is a valid term specified in the reference provided. A quality metric describes a project or product attribute and how the Control Quality process will verify compliance to it. Cost performance measured by CPI is an example of a quality metric. Reviewing the quality metrics established in the project management plan or quality management plan will allow you to verify the tolerance value (or allowable variations) for CPI on this project.

questions.prompt: You have just completed the Develop Schedule process and you are now preparing information for the project sponsor to highlight the activities that may create a delay in the overall project schedule. Which of the following would be most useful in this situation?
questions.explanation: The question states the Develop Schedule process has just been completed. This implies that all project artifacts associated with the schedule planning processes, such as the project network diagrams, have been created. Project network diagrams would best serve to provide information concerning activities that would possibly create schedule delays. A project network diagram is one of three suggested project schedule models. At a minimum, a project schedule should include planned start dates and planned finished dates for each activity. Network diagrams which include activity date information and critical path activities, display how those activities logically relate to other activities and the overall project timeline. A detailed network diagram that includes critical path activities, durations, milestones, and resources could be useful in this situation to highlight the activities that may create a delay in the overall project schedule.

questions.prompt: Up until now, your project team has allowed the use of flextime where project team members may take time off during regular business hours and make up the time during the evening hours or weekends. Recently, excessive use of flextime has become a roadblock as some team members are not available when needed. The team feels they should set a standard that flextime is only to be used for personal emergencies. How should you handle this situation?
questions.option_a: Contact the human resources department to update the corporate policies to prohibit
questions.option_b: Submit a change request to update the team charter based on the team's inputs
questions.option_c: Discuss the issue with the team and update the team charter accordingly
questions.option_d: Do nothing as the team has already agreed to use flextime for personal emergencies only
questions.explanation: The team charter is a document that records the team values, agreements, and operating guidelines, as well as establishing clear expectations regarding acceptable behavior by project team members. The team charter may be updated to reflect changes to the agreed-upon team operating guidelines that result from team development. The team charter works best when the team develops it or at least has an opportunity to contribute to it. Of the available options, discussing the issue with the team and updating the team charter accordingly is the best course of action in this situation.

questions.prompt: A project has just been formally authorized, and the project manager is currently identifying stakeholders for the new project. What should the project manager do to find information on the responsibilities of key project stakeholders?
questions.explanation: The question states that the project has just been formally authorized implying the project charter has been approved. The scenario suggests that the project manager is now starting the Identify Stakeholders process to create the stakeholder register. The stakeholder register will eventually contain information on the roles of stakeholders on the project which is the information the project manager is looking for. However, the stakeholder register is not yet ready. The project charter is an input to the Identify Stakeholders process and has the high-level information about the roles and responsibilities of the key project stakeholders. This information will be used in the development of the stakeholder register. Hence, to find information on the responsibilities of stakeholders, reviewing the project charter is what the project manager should do, and is, therefore, the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: The comparison between the current engagement levels of the plant floor workers with their desired engagement level shows that the workers are becoming more negative about their support for the new process and policy your project is rolling out. Which of the following is best for the project manager to evaluate to understand the reason for the decreased support?
questions.option_a: The performance records of the workers for changes in behavior and production
questions.option_b: The reporting breakdown based on updates to the resource management plan
questions.option_c: The communications based on the updates to the stakeholder engagement assessment
questions.option_d: The earned value calculations for potential trends, patterns, or root causes
questions.explanation: Stakeholder engagement and communications management on a project are often interconnected. The Monitor Communications process seeks to ensure that the information needs of the project stakeholders are met. In this scenario, the engagement of some of the stakeholders (the plant workers) has changed. This change in engagement is typically reflected in the stakeholder engagement assessment matrix and may, in turn, require a change in the communication means and needs. Monitoring the communications requires reviewing a variety of project artifacts and leveraging various methods to control the project communication needs appropriately, which includes the evaluation of changes in stakeholder engagement. Other reasons for the plant workers discontent or changes in assessment may later be attributed to other causes, such as low production levels, poor communications, reporting relationships, earned value reports, and many other potential reasons. However, of the options provided, the best initial step for the project manager is to evaluate the current communications based on the changes in the stakeholder engagement assessment matrix.

questions.prompt: You were informed that one of your project team members falsified the certifications on her resume. You verified the information as accurate and want to terminate the team member's employment. What should you do first?
questions.option_a: Reprimand the employee in front of the team
questions.option_b: Share your findings with the informant
questions.option_c: Immediately terminate the employee for cause
questions.explanation: The performing organization's human resource management policies regarding hiring and termination represent one of the enterprise environmental factors (EEFs) which may influence the Develop Team process implied by the scenario. The human resource management policies and procedures should provide guidance on the criteria as well as the steps which must be taken in order to terminate an employee's employment. Therefore, of the available choices, before taking any action regarding the team member's employment, you should first review the enterprise environmental factors, specifically, the human resource management policies that specify hiring and termination procedures. One may argue that policies and procedures are organizational process assets (OPAs). While this is correct, the list of the OPAs and EEFs provided at the beginning of the PMBOK® Guide is not exhaustive. Specific examples of the OPAs and EEFs are given in the chapters relevant to a specific process. For example, the human resource management policies regarding hiring and termination mentioned above are not listed among the EEFs in the EEFs chapter. However, as specified in the reference provided, the Develop Team process chapter provides these policies as one of the inputs to the process. We believe the reason being is that policies and procedures that specify how the organization hires and terminates their employees are not under the control of the project team. Being not under the control of the project team is what EEFs typically are.

questions.prompt: You are attending a meeting where the project manager, sponsor, vendors, and several other stakeholders are capturing lessons learned. The attendees are in a celebratory mood, the sponsor is happy, and the vendors are asking if there is the potential for any follow-on work. What type of meeting are you attending?
questions.explanation: A project retrospective is a meeting held during project closure to record lessons learned during the project and transfer the information captured in the lessons learned register to the lessons learned repository, where it can be used to improve the performance of future projects. Since the project has concluded, the atmosphere is likely to be celebratory, especially if the project was a success. Additionally, it is not surprising that vendors, who just had their contracts closed, could be inquiring about follow-on work during such a meeting. Note that the question describes three different meetings including a lessons learned meeting (retrospective), a celebration meeting, and a customer wrap-up meeting. However, these meetings can be held independently or in combination. Since the scenario implies that the meeting is occurring as part of project closure, the project retrospective is the best answer to the question asked. The incorrect responses would not likely occur at the end of the project and be celebratory in nature.

questions.prompt: A hydroelectric project is running ahead of schedule and under budget, and the customer is pleased with the progress. Following the resource management plan, you have used the appropriate tools and techniques to identify the strengths and weaknesses of the project team. What should you do next?
questions.option_a: No action is required since the project is expected to exceed project objectives
questions.option_b: Select topics and develop the agenda for a team development meeting
questions.option_c: Submit a change request to modify the resource management plan
questions.option_d: Utilize individual and team assessment tools and techniques
questions.explanation: The question implies that you are conducting the Develop Team process and have used individual and team assessment tools to identify the strengths and weaknesses of the project team. The next logical step is to build upon the project team's strengths and address the identified weaknesses. Meetings are one of the tools that may be used during the Develop Team process. Meetings are used to discuss and address pertinent topics for developing the team. Attendees include the project manager and the project team. Types of meetings include project orientation meetings, team building meetings, and team development meetings. The incorrect answer choices represent actions which will not utilize the identified strengths and weaknesses to develop the project team further. Therefore, of the available options, selecting topics and developing the agenda for a team development meeting is the best response.

questions.prompt: You are leading a project to develop a new gaming console. During a weekly status meeting, you report that the project is over budget with a CPI of 0.9. The project sponsor asks you what level of performance will need to be maintained for the remainder of the project to cover the gap. What tool or technique should you use to address the sponsor's question?
questions.explanation: The question suggests that the Control Costs process is being carried out. The to-complete performance index (TCPI) is one of the tools and techniques that can be used during this process. The to-complete performance index is a measure of the cost performance that is required to be achieved with the remaining resources in order to meet a specified management goal. Therefore, to answer the sponsor's question on what level of performance will need to be maintained for the remainder of the project to cover the gap, using the TCPI would be your best choice.

questions.prompt: A historic building restoration project requires consultation with a historian. The project also requires specialized equipment along with period correct materials and construction techniques to faithfully reproduce the original construction. Where should the project manager look to find information on the equipment, tools, materials, and supplies that need to be acquired for the project?
questions.explanation: The question implies that the Acquire Resources process is being carried out, and the resources requirements document is one of the inputs for this process. The resource requirements is a project document that identifies which resources need to be acquired for the project. The resource requirements document is where the project manager should look to find information on the equipment, tools, materials, and supplies that need to be acquired for the project.

questions.prompt: Defects have been found in some of the project deliverables. The change request to repair the defects has been submitted and approved, and the deliverables repaired. What should the project manager do next?
questions.option_a: Invite the customer to validate scope
questions.option_b: Conduct the approved change requests review
questions.option_d: Request the team to carry out a root cause analysis
questions.explanation: The defects that have been found in the deliverables imply the Control Quality process was underway. Change requests can be among the outputs of this process. According to the scenario, the change request to repair the defects has been submitted and approved, and the defects repaired. Once the defects are repaired, the deliverables should be inspected again to ensure they comply with the quality objectives established for the project. An approved change requests review is an example of a meeting that can be conducted as a tool or technique during the Control Quality process. Therefore, of the choices available, conducting the approved change requests review is the best answer to the question asked. Note, the defects have been found during the Control Quality process, and the repaired deliverables re-tested as part of the same process.

questions.prompt: Working as a project manager for a drug manufacturing project, you are performing a schedule network analysis to generate the project schedule model. You have already determined the critical path and float, optimized resources, and calculated a 90% probability of completing the project by May 28. What else is left to do?
questions.option_a: Using a Monte Carlo simulation to calculate possible outcomes of the project schedule
questions.option_b: Adjusting the start and finish dates to balance the demand for resources with their
questions.option_c: Assessing the need to aggregate schedule reserves to reduce the possibility of schedule
questions.option_d: Applying precedence diagramming to arrange the activities according to their logical
questions.explanation: Schedule network analysis is an umbrella term for several tools and techniques used to develop a project schedule. It includes the critical path method, resource optimization (including resource leveling and resource smoothing), schedule modeling (including what-if scenario analysis and simulation such as the Monte Carlo simulation). In the scenario described, all three of these techniques have been completed. Additional analysis includes assessing the need to aggregate schedule reserves to reduce the possibility for schedule slip, making this the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: A project team has just completed estimates for both the activity durations and the activity resources for the project you are managing. Tomorrow morning, you need to present a preliminary schedule to the key stakeholders. What is your best course of action?
questions.option_a: Pre-assign the resources required for the project
questions.option_c: Present the project schedule network diagram
questions.explanation: Having completed duration and resource estimates in the scenario presented, you have completed the Estimate Activity Durations process. The next logical step is to begin the Develop Schedule process. However, performing this process may be time-consuming, while according to the scenario, you have to present the preliminary project schedule tomorrow. The precedence diagramming method (PDM) is a technique used during the Sequence Activities process to construct a schedule model in which activities are represented by nodes and are graphically linked by one or more logical relationships to show the sequence in which the activities are to be performed. A project schedule network diagram is an output of the Sequence Activities process, which is typically carried out before the Estimate Activity Durations process, and therefore, should have been completed prior to the activities described in the scenario.
