Agile teams working in a collocated space that supports communication is ideal. Planning communications management that hinders or places boundaries on those conversations is counterproductive. A project manager needs to encourage more communication, not less. A project manager also needs to support the capturing and flow of communication between stakeholders. Scheduling more communication checkpoints, such as daily standup meetings, increases the communication exchanges. And, when possible, posting and distributing information to the team aids communication flow - whether in physical locations, digitized, or both. Withholding conversations or enforcing extended quiet times is the antithesis of good communication flow.

questions.prompt: An alert on the project's dashboard indicates that the project's CPI has gone above the upper control limit on the control chart. What should the project manager do? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Communicate the results as a positive outcome
questions.option_c: Communicate the results as a negative outcome
questions.option_d: Wait for the CPI to fall back within the control limits
questions.explanation: The scenario describes the Control Costs process. Earned value analysis (EVA) is an example of the data analysis tools and techniques that can be used as part of this process. When monitoring and controlling the costs of the project, the cost performance index (CPI) can be used to measure the project's cost performance in a ratio using the earned value (EV) against actual costs (AC). The CPI is not a steady, constant value. What is an acceptable fluctuation over time is determined by the project. The ebbs and flows of the project's CPI can be tracked, measured, and charted over time in a control chart. Any measurement that falls within the established upper and lower control limits are within controls. Any value outside those limits indicates that something is out of control. The reason why it is out of control must be found and assigned to a probable cause. Note, a CPI value greater than 1 means the project is below budget. Typically, this situation is perceived as good for the project. However, this could also mean that costs estimates were padded intentionally during planning to make project performance look better during execution. Any variance is not a good thing and should be investigated. Additionally, the scenario does not say the CPI is greater than 1. Rather, it says the CPI has gone above the upper control limit. While it is reasonable to assume the upper control limit has been set above the value of 1, it could be that the control limits have been set between 0.7 and 0.9. So if the CPI has been measured at 0.95 (which is above the upper control limit), the project is still over budget.

questions.prompt: An infrastructure upgrade project is in the initial planning stage, and the project manager is facilitating a risk workshop focused on prioritizing individual risks for further analysis or action. Which of the following might lead to the development of more effective risk responses for this project? Top of Form
questions.explanation: Perform Qualitative Risk Analysis is the process of prioritizing individual project risks for further analysis or action by assessing their probability of occurrence and impact as well as other characteristics. This description reflects the scenario provided in the question. Risk categorization is one of the tools and techniques which may be beneficial for this process. Risks to the project can be categorized by sources of risk using a risk breakdown structure (RBS). Grouping risks into categories can lead to the development of more effective risk responses by focusing attention and effort on the areas of highest risk exposure, or by developing generic risk responses to address groups of related risks. Therefore, of the available choices, categorizing risks using RBS is the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: A software development project is being chartered. The sponsor informs the to-be-assigned project manager that while the project will be performed using the traditional predictive project management approach, the deliverables will be developed incrementally, over the course of several iterations, by a team of agile practitioners. How does that directive alter the expectations of the project manager's role? Top of Form
questions.option_a: More reliance on the project manager to plan out the work
questions.option_b: Nothing changes for the project manager.
questions.option_c: The role of the project manager is replaced by the product owner.
questions.option_d: More servant leadership and team delegation
questions.explanation: The scenario describes a hybrid project, which will be managed using the traditional project management approach; however, the deliverables will be developed in an agile manner. Initiating a project where the project team will be comprised of agile practitioners compared to a project lead in a more traditional approach does alter the role and expectations of a project manager. On projects where deliverables are developed using agile practices, the project teams are smaller and more heavily involved in the planning and decision making. The teams are typically more independent, self-organizing, and self-managing. Therefore, project managers must transition from a top-down command-and-control management style to servant leadership which fosters involvement and focuses on building a collaborative decision-making environment.

questions.prompt: During a recession, a project manager posts a positive risk that in the current economic environment, cheaper resources may become available. On the way into work, the project manager hears about a competitor going out of business and contacts the competitor to acquire some equipment, materials, and supplies at a fraction of their normal value. What is the project manager doing? Top of Form
questions.option_b: Reacting to a contingency plan trigger
questions.explanation: Some risk responses are designed for use only if certain events occur. For some risks, it is appropriate for the project team to make a response plan that will only be executed under certain predefined conditions. Risk responses identified using this technique are often called contingency plans or fallback plans and include identified triggering events that set the plans in effect. The question describes a positive risk (opportunity) that was identified and captured in the risk register along with the risk trigger and responses. Part of the responsibility of the project manager and the risk owner is to understand what events may cause (or trigger) a risk to occur. In this case, the project manager heard of a competitor going out of business which triggered the action of contacting the competitor to acquire resources much cheaper than they otherwise could have been purchased. Note that the project manager also exploited an opportunity in this situation. However, exploiting the realized risk was not offered as an answer choice. Therefore, of the available options, reacting to a contingency plan trigger is the best response.

questions.prompt: The project team has worked for months on an open-ended concept product for their organization. Some team members are ready to push it out to users to get feedback. Others are not sure. What is the best course of action for the project manager? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Release the product then ask the team to validate scope
questions.option_b: Consider the options then go with what feels right
questions.option_c: Hear the competing arguments then ask all to vote
questions.option_d: Refer to the stakeholder register then decide
questions.explanation: Performing the Validate Scope process requires some decisions as to whether the scope is, in fact, complete and acceptable. There are many tools and techniques a project manager can use with stakeholders to make decisions. In this scenario, the stakeholders involved in validating the scope are members of the project team. As such, there is some interpretation as to whether the project that delivers on an open-ended concept product may or may not have clear directives and well-documented scope and requirements. The decision making requires more input and consultation with those involved. Before any decision is made, it is advisable to concern all inputs, perspectives, and arguments. After an appropriate time to discuss those inputs relative to the project and its timeline, a more informed decision can be made.

questions.prompt: One of the risks that has been identified during project planning is that the company will be unable to retain the planned number of qualified developers for the software development project you are leading. You need to determine the likelihood of this risk as well as its impact on the project schedule. What should you do? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Meet with the developers and get their input
questions.option_b: Create a probability and impact matrix
questions.option_c: Perform a risk data quality assessment
questions.option_d: Conduct a risk probability and impact assessment
questions.explanation: The question suggests that you are conducting the Perform Qualitative Risk Analysis process. Perform Qualitative Risk Analysis is the process of prioritizing individual project risks for further analysis or action by assessing their probability of occurrence and impact as well as other characteristics. A data analysis technique associated with this process is the risk probability and impact assessment. Risk probability assessment considers the likelihood that a specific risk will occur, such as the risk that the company would be unable to retain some of its developers planned for the project. Therefore, to determine the likelihood of this risk as well as its impact on the project schedule, conducting a risk probability and impact assessment is what you should do first.

questions.prompt: A project manager is in the process of obtaining the necessary equipment and team members for her construction project. She has just learned that the grader originally planned for use on the project is not available and has decided to rent two smaller graders instead. The project manager will also add a new team member to run the second grader. What is the project manager least likely to do next? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The question suggests that the project manager is conducting the Acquire Resources process by obtaining equipment and team members for the project. All of the answer choices represent items that may be updated in this scenario. While the resource management plan may be 'updated' during the Acquire Resources process to reflect actual experience in acquiring resources for the project, it is 'created' earlier in the project as part of the Plan Resource Management process. Thus, the resource management plan would already exist at this point in the project. Note, the question is asking for the activity that the project manager is 'least' likely to perform in this scenario. Therefore, of the available options, creating the resource management plan is the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: The project is required to notify the public at various stages. A public relations specialist was involved in planning the communications for the project. Now, when the project is in execution, the communications with the public up to this point do not appear to be effective. A survey revealed only 2% of the public was aware of the project. Whom should the project manager contact first? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The scenario surrounds the Monitor Communications process. Expert judgment is among the tools and techniques that can be used as part of this process. Expertise should be considered from individuals or groups with specialized knowledge or training in communications with the public, the community, and the media. When monitoring and controlling the project communications there are often many people involved in the process. A project manager's role is not always to be the one performing all the project activities, but assuring that they are being done. For the benefit of the project and all involved, activities, such as in this scenario where decisions need to be made regarding the communications with external stakeholders (the public), the project manager is better off consulting with those who know most and can suggest the most effective solutions. That is the public relations specialist in the company. S/He is the subject matter expert (SME) in this case. They are most equipped to recommend what to do next. The project manager and relevant stakeholder can make an informed decision based on the advice from the SME. The project manager is the one responsible for turning those decisions into actionable plans and results. The project sponsor provides the authority and support - typically in the form of resources and finances - to commission those actions. All the identified roles are involved, but the best advice can come from the one with the most expert judgment. While it can be argued for the effectiveness (or the lack thereof) of the public relations specialist, project communications failure cannot be blamed on only one person. The specialist was involved in project planning, however, it is unclear if the specialist's inputs have been properly documented. And even if yes, the failure could have been caused by the improper implementation of the Manage Communications process. There are too many assumptions to be made as to why the communications up to this point do n

questions.prompt: The previous project was a failure, and its objectives have not been achieved. However, since the organization still needs the deliverables that were to be produced by that project, the organization initiates a new project. What should the project manager do to ensure that the new project starts with a higher probability of success? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Include the assumption in the project charter that this project may also fail to set stakeholder
questions.option_b: Make certain that the team members from the failed project are not involved in the new project
questions.option_c: Review the lessons learned from the failed project to incorporate this knowledge into the project
questions.option_d: Gather information from the project manager and the team of the failed project and use this
questions.explanation: Based on the scenario, it can be inferred that the project is in initiating. When initiating new projects in any organization, examining and assessing the successes and failures of past projects can serve as excellent information to support the project(s) under consideration. Learning from failed projects can provide valuable information towards the success of another project. Repeating the same mistakes should not occur if the organization reviews past projects, assesses the lessons learned, and applies them to the next project, as in the form of the project chartering in this scenario.

questions.prompt: After completing eight iterations, the team realizes the mistakes they made in the first iteration. What is the next best action for the project team to do? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Proceed to the next iteration as planned
questions.option_c: Apply the lesson learned to the next iteration
questions.explanation: The scenario is designed around the Manage Project Knowledge process which is the process of using existing knowledge and creating new knowledge to achieve the project's objectives and contribute to organizational learning. Managing the vast amount of project knowledge and the lessons that are constantly learned by all project stakeholders throughout the project enables the project team to leverage that knowledge and apply it. Applying lessons learned and project knowledge supports the project organization, the project outcomes, and operation of the current and future projects. Documenting the knowledge into a lesson learned register is one method of capturing that knowledge to ensure it is not lost. Applying that knowledge to the project is even more beneficial. There is a misconception that projects must wait until the end of a project in order to document it. Just accepting mistakes happened is not enough. Knowledge is fluid and best shared and used at the point it is most needed.

questions.prompt: You are managing the development of a new product. The customer has provided the objectives and description of the product. The project team is decomposing the customer's high-level requirements to the level of detail needed to design the product and define its scope. In which activity is the project team involved? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The scenario implies you are in the Define Scope process. Product analysis is among the tools and techniques of this process and can be used to define products and services. By asking questions about a product and forming answers to those questions, a description of the use, characteristics, and other relevant aspects can be determined about the product that is going to be delivered. In product analysis, requirements are captured at a high level and decomposed to the level of detail needed to design the final product which is what described by the scenario, making product analysis the best answer to the question asked. Examples of product analysis techniques include product breakdown, requirements analysis, systems analysis, systems engineering, value analysis, and value engineering.

questions.prompt: A project manager is getting ready to transition the final product to the customer. Which of the following should the project manager issue as part of this process? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The final product, service, or result transition is one of the main outputs of the Close Project or Phase process. The question states that the project manager is getting ready to transition the final product to the customer implying the project is in the Close Project or Phase process. The final report provides a summary of the project performance and may include information such as the summary level description of the project or phase, objectives of scope, quality, cost as well as the criteria used to determine if the project has met these objectives. Of the choices provided, the final report is the only document 'issued' during the Close Project or Phase process and is the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: The product trade show is approaching. The project team must produce functional prototypes for the event. The team is also tasked with many other competing project tasks. What can the project manager do in the next iteration planning? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Remove the other competing tasks from the schedule
questions.option_b: Reprioritize the remaining work in the product backlog
questions.option_c: Crash the project schedule by adding more resources
questions.option_d: Move the prototype work to be on the critical path
questions.explanation: The scenario implies the project manager is concerned with the Control Schedule process on a project managed using agile approaches. Such projects leverage the prioritization of the work on the product backlog to influence and control the project's schedule and iterations. Due to the iterative nature of agile managed projects, the schedule is controlled in some manner during iteration planning - which occurs between iterations. In the scenario, the prototype work and the other project tasks may all worked on concurrently and with equal importance. Reprioritizing the items on the product backlog allows the project manager and the project team to control the work scheduled for the upcoming iteration. So if the prototype work is of more focus in the near team schedule, the backlog should be prioritized accordingly.

questions.prompt: A team member responsible for tracking the costs of a project increases the budget to accommodate the unexpected rise in expenditures over the last three weeks. What should the project manager say to the project team member? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Any increase to the authorized budget can only be approved via the Perform Integrated Change
questions.option_b: If the increase in the budget is to cover an unexpected rise in expenditures, the project sponsor
questions.option_c: Adjustments to the project budget need to occur closer to the point when expenditures go above
questions.option_d: Any increase in the authorized budget requires the development of an earned value analysis
questions.explanation: The scenario implies the Control Costs process is underway. Controlling the costs of the project requires the project to stay within the bounds of the budget set for the project. At times, the project may need to adjust the authorized budget, but this can only be done upon approval through the Perform Integrated Change Control process. The request to make budgetary changes comes as an output from the Control Costs process. The request(s) are evaluated per the Perform Integrated Change Control process suitable for the project. If accepted, the approved change to the budget is what the project team monitors and controls the cost of the project against. A project team member or project manager cannot make such a change to the budget without following the process.

questions.prompt: Your project is trending to meet or exceed all of its objectives, but the spend rate is higher than planned. Which of the following most accurately describes the project's performance? Top of Form
questions.option_a: The cost performance index (CPI) is less than 1.0
questions.option_b: The schedule performance index (SPI) is greater than 1.0
questions.option_c: The earned value is less than the planned value
questions.option_d: The to-complete performance index (TCPI) is greater than 1.0
questions.explanation: The question indicates that the project is trending to meet or exceed all of its objectives. This means that if the current performance is maintained for the remainder of the project, the entire project scope will be completed on time or sooner and within or under budget. The spend rate only indicates that project costs have exceeded what was planned at this point in the project. The spend rate does not indicate if the actual costs have exceeded the value of the work completed. If the project work is being completed faster than planned, then it is possible to have a higher than expected spend rate with the project still trending to complete within budget. Thus, just because a project is spending money at a faster rate than planned, does not necessarily mean that the project's objectives are in jeopardy. All of the incorrect answer choices can be eliminated because they indicate that the project is trending to miss a project baseline. An SPI of greater than 1.0 indicates that the project is trending ahead of schedule, and is the only answer choice that is aligned with the scenario, according to which the project is trending to meet or exceed all of its objectives.

questions.prompt: Sharing of project information is of utmost importance to the organization. Project knowledge is shared on multiple systems, services, and apps. The array of locations is becoming overwhelming for some of the project team members. What is the best option for the project manager to manage the project knowledge in this situation? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Close down some of the systems that the project team notes as burdensome
questions.option_b: Consolidate or streamline sharing processes where possible
questions.option_c: Compromise with the team to allow each to post in the tool they find most productive
questions.option_d: Coordinate a weekly meeting to post all the team's knowledge at once
questions.explanation: The scenario surrounds the Manage Project Knowledge process. Managing the vast array of project knowledge is challenging, but important. Often, projects and their organizations use a variety of tools and systems to collect, distribute, and access that knowledge. Juggling the volume of the knowledge and its flow adds to the challenge. Ensuring the project team members and other stakeholders use the tools well enhances the timeliness and accessibility of project information. The project manager and others can and should continuously seek out ways to improve project processes. This includes the managing of project knowledge and the tools. If there are ways to consolidate, streamline, et cetera, then efforts should be made to do so. Ignoring or not following processes does not make the process better, it only makes it worse.

questions.prompt: During execution of a hotel construction project, the person responsible for implementing a particular risk response strategy has left the performing organization, and the project manager has identified a new stakeholder to assume those responsibilities. After updating the stakeholder register, what should the project manager do next? Top of Form
questions.option_c: Notify the PMO of the new stakeholder
questions.option_d: Execute the Perform Qualitative Risk Analysis process
questions.explanation: The question suggests that the project manager is performing the Identify Stakeholders process by updating the stakeholder register to reflect the addition of a new stakeholder. The question also indicates that the new stakeholder will assume the responsibilities as a risk owner from the previous stakeholder. The risk owner is the person responsible for monitoring the risks and for selecting and implementing an appropriate risk response strategy. The name of the nominated risk owner for each identified risk should be captured in the risk register. The incorrect answer choices are all unnecessary steps in this scenario. Therefore, of the available choices, revising the risk register is what the project manager should do next.

questions.prompt: You are managing a project to build a 50 story residential building. When the project was in planning, the truck drivers threatened to strike, which would prevent the timely delivery of materials and supplies to the worksite. This risk was identified, and a response plan was developed. Fortunately, the labor dispute was settled, and the physical resources should be available as originally planned. With the threat of the strike averted, what should you do next? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The question describes a situation where a risk had been identified that would have affected resource availability. With the strike averted, the physical resources will be available as originally planned. The risk register is a repository in which outputs of risk management processes are recorded. The status of each identified risk is typically included in the risk register. With the risk of a strike averted, the next logical step is to close out the risk in the risk register. The incorrect answer choices represent actions that will not be necessary since the risk was not realized. Therefore, of the available choices, revising the risk register is the best response.

questions.prompt: A project manager is preparing for a meeting to identify risks associated with the project. As part of the preparations, the project manager is looking for a document that captures a comprehensive list of factors that could either benefit or constrain the project. Which of the following would help the project manager the most? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The question states that the project is in the planning stage and implies that the project manager is carrying out the Identify Risks process. One input to the Identify Risks process is the assumption log. An assumption is a factor in a planning process that is considered to be true, real, or certain, without proof or demonstration. A constraint is a limiting factor that affects the execution of a project, program, portfolio, or process. The assumption log is a project document used to record all assumptions and constraints throughout the project life cycle. The assumption log is the best source for a comprehensive list of assumptions that have been made about the project up to that point.

questions.prompt: Per organizational policy, vendors should be selected by an evaluation committee. The project manager schedules a meeting to discuss the proposals submitted by prospective vendors but, based on past experience, is concerned that the committee members may have preconceived opinions about which vendor should be selected based on personal preferences and relationships. What is the best course of action for the project manager to select a vendor? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Remove vendors' identifying information from their proposals and let the committee evaluate the
questions.option_b: Hold the meeting as scheduled and let the committee evaluate the vendors' proposals against the
questions.option_c: Escalate the issue to the project sponsor since preconceived opinions of the committee members
questions.option_d: Cancel the meeting to avoid bias and select the vendor based on the merits of the proposals and
questions.explanation: The project manager should ensure that vendors are selected objectively by establishing a fair and objective vendor proposal evaluation process. Although using the established source selection criteria will set objective selection standards, it may be unrealistic for the source selection criteria to cover every nuance of the submitted proposals. Thus, even with the use of the source selection criteria, the risk of personal biases influencing the vendor selection process may still exist. By removing any identifying information from the proposals and using the established source selection criteria, the project manager can ensure that each proposal is evaluated objectively and fairly. Therefore, of the available options, the best course of action for the project manager is to remove vendors' identifying information from their proposals and let the committee evaluate the proposals against the source selection criteria.

questions.prompt: The project team has spent a great deal of time reviewing a set of significant changes that a vendor proposed to the specifications of a project deliverable. Everyone eventually agrees that the changes would improve the deliverable without any net effect on the cost. What should be done next? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Implement the changes since no additional costs are involved
questions.option_b: Issue a change request to update the procurement management plan
questions.option_c: Issue a change request to update the project management plan
questions.option_d: Deduct the cost of the evaluation process from the vendor's invoice
questions.explanation: Since the change seems to be substantial, there are likely many areas of the project that will be affected. Even if the cost baseline is not impacted, the scope and schedule baselines may need to be revised. Additionally, other elements of the project management plan may be affected including the procurement management plan, requirements management plan, quality management plan, risk management plan, and perhaps others. Therefore, a change request needs to be issued to review the total impact of the proposed change. Since the project management plan, including baselines, is considered a configuration element, an approved change request will be required to implement the proposed changes. Therefore, of the available options, issuing a change request to update the project management plan is what should be done next.

questions.prompt: A project manager who works for a city government is planning a project to build a new community center. The project will ultimately be paid for with tax revenues, but the city lacks the immediate funds that will be required to get the project going. To determine how the project will be paid, the team is investigating various sources of funding including municipal bonds and federal grants. What tool or technique is the team using in this scenario? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The scenario implies the project is in the Determine Budget process. One of the tools and techniques used during this process is financing. Financing entails acquiring funding for projects. It is common for long-term infrastructure, industrial, and public services projects to seek external sources of funds. The question states that to determine how the project will be paid, the team is investigating various sources of funding including municipal bonds and federal grants. This approach is best described by financing, making it the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: Your team has been spending an unusual amount of time arguing in meetings. You have conducted team training to improve meeting discipline and listening skills. Which document should be updated to reflect the new team standards of conduct? Top of Form
questions.explanation: This scenario indicates that you are in the Develop Team process. Developing the team's emotional intelligence and meeting skills are part of this process. The new team standards for meeting behavior should be recorded as an update to the team charter, which is an output of the process. This document records team values, agreements, and operating guidelines, as well as establishes clear expectations for acceptable behavior by the team.

questions.prompt: You are the product owner for a retail mobile shopping application developed using an agile approach. You have envisioned seasonal promotional content and new features based on user requests. At an upcoming user conference, your customers expect to learn what features have already been released and what is planned going forward. What should you present? Top of Form
questions.option_a: The features being developed in the iteration plan
questions.option_b: The user stories included in the iteration plan
questions.option_c: The developer tasks that comprise the user stories
questions.option_d: The product roadmap of features in each iteration
questions.explanation: This scenario describes agile release planning, which is a tool for the Develop Schedule process. When presenting a timeline of features to stakeholders at a user conference, the product owner wants a level of detail that is appropriate for the intended audience. Telling stories is an important presentation skill. However, a 'user story' is an agile term meaning a brief description of value of the deliverable to a specific user. The product roadmap describes the depth of information that customers seek, which is an easily understood schedule of features available at the end of each iteration in each release. The current roadmap would describe features that were recently released, and what features will be released going forward.

questions.prompt: A supplier the project is using to subcontract some of the project work has delivered all the contracted work. Which value does the project manager attribute to the accepted deliverable? Top of Form
questions.explanation: When project work is being completed and accepted - whether performed by the project team or component of the project team in the form of a supplier or third party - those results are considered earned values or EV. The EV is tracked to monitor gains that have been earned over the course of the project life cycle. The EV can be measured against the planned values (PV) and the actual costs (AC) of the work to monitor and control the project and to communicate the state of the project. The EV is an example of the data analysis techniques that can be used as part of the Control Costs process described by the scenario.

questions.prompt: A project manager is conducting the initial process of identifying stakeholders for a new infrastructure development project. The project manager has just compiled a list of potential stakeholders' names. What should the project manager do next? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Invite the identified stakeholders to the project kick-off meeting
questions.option_c: Complete the Project Stakeholder Management process
questions.explanation: The question suggests that the project is still in the initiation stage, with the initial iteration of the Identify Stakeholders process, and has yet to progress to project planning. At this point, the project manager has only compiled a list of potential stakeholders' names. With only a list of individual and group names, the next logical step is to perform stakeholder analysis. Stakeholder analysis, an example of the data analysis technique which can be used during the Identify Stakeholder process, results in a list of stakeholders and relevant information such as their positions in the organization, roles on the project, expectations, attitudes, and their interest in information about the project. Therefore, of the available choices, conducting stakeholder analysis is what the project manager should do next.

questions.prompt: Your enterprise software integration project has just begun execution when your organization acquires a new subsidiary and involves them in the project. The new subsidiary recently finished a similar integration project using a compatible tool. Using the tool may accelerate both your project and the new subsidiary's assimilation into the organization. However, the schedule and cost variances to take this opportunity do not quite meet agreed-upon risk thresholds. What should you do? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Do nothing, because the opportunity does not meet your existing stakeholders' risk thresholds as
questions.option_b: Ask your project sponsor for permission to switch to the tool that has been used by the new
questions.option_c: Submit a change request to adjust the risk thresholds that consider the new stakeholders' risk
questions.option_d: Train your team to use the new tool based on the expert judgment of your new subsidiary's
questions.explanation: As you are considering an opportunity to take advantage of a new tool for a project that has just gotten underway, the scenario indicates you are in the Implement Risk Responses process. You have five strategies to consider for dealing with specific opportunities: escalate, exploit, share, enhance, and accept. Considering that your organization has just acquired a new subsidiary, you should exploit opportunities to capture the benefits of assimilating them into the organization. Using new technologies to reduce cost and duration are a key example of exploiting an opportunity. When it comes to overall project risk, there is a similar set of five strategies to consider: avoid, exploit, transfer/share, mitigate/enhance, and accept. Where the level of overall project risk is significantly positive and outside the agreed-upon risk thresholds for the project, an exploit strategy may be adopted. An example of exploiting at the overall project level would include the addition of high-benefit elements of scope to the project to add value or benefits to stakeholders. Alternatively, to embrace the opportunity, the risk thresholds for the project may be modified if the key stakeholders are in agreement. Note, the risk thresholds are recorded in the risk management plan to reflect the acceptable level of overall project risk exposure. Since the risk management plan is a component of the project management plan, a change request is required to modify any information included in the plan, such as the risk thresholds.

questions.prompt: Your marketing project started with a team comprised of junior graphics designers. Throughout the project, you have invested heavily in courses to strengthen the proficiencies of the team members. Now, you are evaluating the professional knowledge of each team member. What would be most likely updated as a result of your efforts? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The scenario implies that you are involved in the Develop Team process, focusing on the skill development of your team which is comprised of junior designers. After conducting the intensive training, you want to validate whether or not the training has achieved its objectives. In other words, you apply various tools and techniques associated with the Develop Team process to assess the skills of the team members. The question is asking, "What would be most likely updated as a result of your efforts?" In other words, the question is asking to select an answer choice that represents one of the outputs of the Develop Team process. Enterprise environmental factors (EEFs) that can be updated as a result of the Develop Team process include skill assessments, making this choice the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: A new project manager is brought onto the project. She wants to understand the process by which this particular project formally accepts deliverables. Which of the following will she have to reference? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The scope management plan is a component of the project management plan that contains information and guidance on how the scope and the scope-related processes are to be managed. What is included varies, but should contain procedures and processes specifying how the formal acceptance of the completed project deliverables will be obtained. Acceptance of project deliverables is influenced by other project management plans, such as the stakeholder management plan and the quality management plan. Nevertheless, the scope management plan is what specifies the process most directly.

questions.prompt: After activities were estimated and assigned, you created a resource histogram below to show how many hours each resource was allocated over a series of weeks. Assume they all can do the same tasks and that it is possible to advance and delay these tasks without violating a scheduling constraint. To reduce the risk of burnout and costs of overtime, which of the following choices will address some of the overallocation displayed? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Use available float in the critical path activities to smooth allocation of all team members
questions.option_b: Adjust Etienne's start date so that his tasks for Week 2 begins during Week 1
questions.option_c: Adjust Suri's Week 3 start dates to start in Week 2 and level her utilization for those weeks and
questions.option_d: Reassign non-critical staff from another department in the organization
questions.explanation: The scenario implies you are performing the Develop Schedule process and creating the resource histogram as one of its outputs. A resource histogram is a model of schedule data that displays resource requirements and allocations by time period. It often shows the constraint of a 40-hour work week, which can be used to pinpoint the need for resource optimization in a schedule. The goal of resource optimization is to reduce the risk of burnout and the costs of overtime. In the resource histogram, Suri, Kamal, and Robin have the highest weekly over-allocations beyond 40 hours, and Suri also has two weeks of under-allocation. Adjusting the start and finish dates and sharing workloads (also known as crashing) are two adjustments that can be used for resource optimization in this scenario. Therefore, of the available choices, adjusting Suri's Week 3 start dates to start in Week 2 and leveling her utilization for those weeks as well as having her crash some of Kamal's Week 1 tasks is the best answer to the question asked. While there are other ways to optimize resources and help Robin who is also over-allocated, these were not provided as choices. Therefore, one should select the choice that is available and best addresses the question asked.

questions.prompt: A project manager is highly experienced but is new to the organization and industry. He seeks assistance from another project manager within the organization that has worked on similar projects. He meets with that individual in order to get a better understanding of the organization’s approach to managing risk and the types of risk that are likely to be encountered on the project. Which one of the tools or techniques is being used in this scenario? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The choices of Perform Qualitative Risk Analysis and Perform Quantitative Risk Analysis can be eliminated as they are project management processes rather than tools and techniques. The probability and impact matrix is associated with the Perform Qualitative Risk Analysis process rather than the Plan Risk Management process. Familiarity with the organization’s approach to managing risk and the types of risk likely to be encountered on the project are examples of expert judgment.

questions.prompt: The equipment that is used to connect parts has broken. The maintenance crew has begun repairs and ordered replacement parts per the response plan. The crew expects the equipment to be down for at least three days. What should the project manager do next? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Submit a change request to update the procurement management plan
questions.option_b: Inform all affected stakeholders of the situation and the response plan
questions.option_c: Order extra parts to store in the warehouse in case the machine breaks again
questions.option_d: Document the knowledge gained in the lessons learned register
questions.explanation: As known project risks are realized, the risk response plans are implemented as part of the Implement Risk Responses process. Risk report is a project document developed progressively throughout the Project Risk Management processes, which summarizes information on individual project risks and the level of overall project risk. Updates to the risk report are among the outputs of the Implement Risk Responses process. The project manager must communicate what is happening and the impacts of the project to stakeholders. Sharing the risk report with the stakeholders is an example of communications that can be done as part of the process described in the scenario. It is the project manager's responsibility to ensure information is sent out appropriately and understood by the recipients. Hence, of the choices provided, informing all affected stakeholders of the situation and the response plan is the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: You are in the process of obtaining the necessary equipment for your construction project. A review of the resource calendar indicates that there is a scheduling conflict with the excavator needed for the site preparation. You have decided to resolve the conflict by substituting the excavator with a larger model which is currently available. What should you do next? Top of Form
questions.option_d: Submit a change request to adjust the cost baseline
questions.explanation: The question implies that the project manager is performing the Acquire Resources process by obtaining an excavator to be used for the site preparation. The resource requirements is one of the project documents that may be updated during this process. The resource requirements document the types and quantities of resources required for each activity in a work package. Since the excavator was substituted with a larger model, the resource requirements need to be updated to reflect the resource actually acquired for use on the project. Therefore, of the available choices, revising the resource requirements is what you should do next.

questions.prompt: The project you are managing is well underway, and you are currently actively collecting and storing information about the progress of project deliverables, creating forecasts for your sponsor, distributing work performance reports, and presenting project status to key stakeholders. Which process are you performing? Top of Form
questions.explanation: To answer this question, first, determine under which knowledge area the described activities fall. With reference to sponsors and stakeholders, you may guess that it is Stakeholder Management. However, the activities of collection, creation, distribution, storage, and monitoring project information fall under Communications Management. Next, determine what process group is related to these activities. Is it the Planning Process Group or Monitoring and Controlling Process Group? No, the task is not to develop the communication models (which would have been part of project planning) in planning, nor is the task to determine if the planned communications artifacts and activities have had the desired effect (which would have been part of monitoring and controlling). Instead, the current activities are mostly collection, creation, distribution, and disposition of project information, which take place while the work of the project is being executed. In other words, you are in the Executing Process Group, under the Project Communications Management Knowledge Area, i.e., you are carrying out the Manage Communications process.

questions.prompt: The project is not getting the marketing team to update the content of the online magazine website as scheduled. The outdated content is causing confusion among readers. What can the project manager do to engage the marketing team and make the needed changes as soon as possible? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Force the marketing manager to make the changes immediately
questions.option_b: Communicate the importance and urgency of the change
questions.option_c: Assign any another project team member to perform the work
questions.option_d: Complain to other managers about the lack of response time
questions.explanation: Managing stakeholder engagement throughout a project is a difficult, yet important, process. Communicating and working with project stakeholders to meet project and stakeholder needs and expectations require interpersonal skills and leveraging other tools and techniques. In this scenario, for unknown reasons, the stakeholders in the marketing team are not available for the project as scheduled. Of the choices available, communicating the urgency and importance of the project to the stakeholders falls within the authority of the project manager and is likely to be the best course of action for the project manager to take under current circumstances. Forcing or threatening or making others perform the work without proper communication with the stakeholders risks breakdown of trust, cooperation, and shared values.

questions.prompt: A project manager is currently in the process of prioritizing individual project risks by assessing their detectability, proximity, and impact value. The project manager would like to create a graphical representation of these three dimensions. What is the best course of action? Top of Form
questions.option_d: Create a probability and impact matrix
questions.explanation: Perform Qualitative Risk Analysis is the process of prioritizing individual project risks for further analysis or action by assessing their probability of occurrence and impact as well as other characteristics such as detectability, proximity, and impact value. Thus, the project manager is conducting the Perform Qualitative Risk Analysis process and needs to create a graphical representation of project risks along three dimensions. A data representation technique that may be used in support of this process includes hierarchical charts, of which bubble charts are an example. A bubble chart displays three dimensions of data, where each risk is plotted as a disk (bubble), and the three parameters are presented by the X-axis value, the Y-axis value, and the bubble size. In this scenario, the detectability and proximity can be plotted on the X and Y axes, and the impact value represented by the bubble size. The incorrect answer choices describe charts that can only display two parameters rather than three.

questions.prompt: As a project manager, you will need the assistance of other stakeholders in order to identify the risks associated with your project. You want to determine who are the individuals that might participate in the process of identifying risks to the project, as well as those who are available to act as risk owners. Where should you look? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The question implies that the project team is performing the project management process of Identify Risks. One input to this process is the stakeholder register. The stakeholder register is a project document that lists project stakeholders along with their identification and assessment information, and classification. The project manager can use this information to find out which individuals or groups might participate in identifying risks to the project as well as to determine those stakeholders who are available to act as risk owners. The stakeholder register is the best place to find which stakeholders should be included in the Identify Risks process.

questions.prompt: To complete the Plan Risk Management process and establish risk thresholds for your project, you want to get a better understanding of the key stakeholder’s attitudes toward risk. Where is the best place to find this information? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The question states that you are performing the Plan Risk Management process. One input to this process is the stakeholder register. The stakeholder register contains details of the project’s stakeholders and provides an overview of their project roles and their attitude toward risk on the project. This information is useful in setting risk thresholds for the project.

questions.prompt: A project has been closed for a week. The project manager for that project has been notified that resources are still charging to the project's account. Which of the following is the most likely reason for this situation? Top of Form
questions.option_a: While closing the project, the project manager did not release all the resources from the project
questions.option_b: While planning the project, the project manager did not estimate how long the resources were to
questions.option_c: While closing the project, the project manager did not acquire stakeholder acceptance
questions.option_d: While initiating the project, the project manager did not clarify the expected end to the project
questions.explanation: When closing a project, the project manager is responsible for ensuring that all personnel, equipment, materials, and other resources are released and no longer assigned to the project. Not properly releasing the resources risks resources remaining on the project even after the project is closed and billing to the project.

questions.prompt: You are managing an engineering design project. The vendor has created 50 engineering drawings. Since the project is on a tight schedule and budget, checking every drawing for accuracy and completeness would be impossible. You direct the team to randomly select 20 drawings for inspection. This exercise can best be described as an example of: Top of Form
questions.explanation: This scenario is an example of statistical sampling since it involves the team selecting 20 out of 50 engineering drawings at random to measure accuracy and completeness. Statistical sampling is among the data gathering techniques that can be used as part of the Control Quality process. Statistical sampling is often applied when inspecting the entire population would be too costly or time-consuming. In this instance, given that the project is on a tight schedule and budget, statistical sampling makes sense to use to inspect the engineering drawings.

questions.prompt: A domestic vendor that is supplying the most critical component for a project you are leading is acquired by a major foreign conglomerate. The conglomerate has a history of replacing the executive team in the companies they acquire with new people from their corporate office. The only change that affects your project is that invoices are now payable to the conglomerate's corporate office. Which of the following project artifacts will you least likely need to update? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The question describes a situation where a foreign company has acquired an important vendor and will likely replace the executive management of the company acquired. These changes may impact the current project, which might require updates to components of the project management plan. Note, the question is asking to select an item that will 'least' likely need to be updated. The contract with the vendor is still valid as the acquiring company inherits all of the assets and liabilities of the company being acquired. Thus, the vendor is still legally obligated to provide the deliverables according to the existing schedule as contracted. While the change in executive structure may introduce new risks that might eventually affect the schedule, those risks should be captured in the risk register rather than directly reflected in the schedule baseline. Therefore, of the available choices, the schedule baseline is the least likely project artifact to be updated.

questions.prompt: You are leading a project for a client. A vendor you contracted with to supply materials for the project was late on delivery on two separate instances. This caused you to delay the deliverables to the client, who inflicted penalty costs on your project as specified in the terms and conditions of the contract with the client. What is your best course of action? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Cancel the contract with the client
questions.option_c: Cancel the contract with the vendor
questions.explanation: Monitoring and controlling the project requires observing and learning from what has happened, what is happening, and what could be happening in the project. Discussing and capturing the lessons learned from the interactions with the vendor and the penalties incurred by the client can guide the project team on how to manage things better and complete the project. Especially in this situation where the issue has occurred more than once. Much can be gleaned from the two deliveries in this project, whether or not additional deliveries from this vendor are expected or planned. Learning from what goes well and what goes wrong is useful for the project and future projects. Revising the lessons learned register is another way of saying capturing the lessons learned from the experience the project has had with the vendor. Updates to the lessons learned register are among the outputs of the Control Costs process implied by the scenario.

questions.prompt: You are planning a project to upgrade the software for your company's retail point-of-sale system. You need to assign an approximate value to the investment that will be required for the project. After finishing the necessary analysis using a bottom-up approach, you are forecasting that a $50,000 investment will be needed for the project plus an additional $5,000 for a contingency reserve, and $5,000 as a management reserve. What have you created in this scenario? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The question suggests that the Estimate Costs process is being carried out. One output of the Estimate Costs process is cost estimates. Cost estimates include quantitative assessments of the probable costs required to complete project work, as well as contingency amounts to account for identified risks, and a management reserve to cover unplanned work. Cost estimates have been produced based on the description provided by the question.

questions.prompt: As an employee of a large organization, you have been building a business case for a community outreach project. The project selection steering committee is meeting in a few weeks. What is the best action for you to get your project chartered? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Find a potential sponsor who can champion your project at the meeting
questions.option_b: Use pre-assignment to secure the best available team in the organization
questions.option_c: Prepare the list of key project deliverables and milestone schedule
questions.option_d: Offer to act as both the project sponsor and the project manager
questions.explanation: To develop and greenlight a project to initiate via a project charter, a project sponsor is needed to authorize and allocate necessary resources to fulfill project objectives. The project sponsor may be an individual or a group of individuals. Whatever the makeup of the entity, a sponsor must be willing to back a project and its alignment to business needs and strategy. In this scenario, there is a project selection steering committee. Having someone on that committee to evangelize the benefits, merits, and business case of a project proposal increases the chances of the project being selected and chartered. No other work on the project, such as pre-assignment of resources, should occur until a project is chartered.

questions.prompt: It comes to the attention of the project manager that some of the vendors do not receive project communications, although the project management plan has specific instructions as to how project information should be shared with vendors. What might be the most likely reason that has caused this issue? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Neglecting to include vendors in the communications management plan
questions.option_b: There is no issue; vendors are not supposed to receive project communications
questions.option_c: Forgetting to perform the Develop Project Management Plan process
questions.option_d: Failing to review the agreements as part of the Identify Stakeholders process
questions.explanation: If the vendors were not captured in the stakeholder register as part of the Identify Stakeholders process, the result could be missed communications. The agreements serve as an input to the Identify Stakeholders process since the parties to an agreement are stakeholders by definition. If the project team failed to review the agreements when the stakeholder register was created, the result could be an incomplete stakeholder register. The stakeholder register, in turn, serves as an input to the Plan Communications Management process by providing the list of stakeholders with whom project information can be shared. Obviously, if some stakeholders are not recorded in the stakeholder register, they would not receive project communications. Therefore, of the available choices, the most likely cause of the issue described by the question is the failure to review the agreements as part of the Identify Stakeholders process.

questions.prompt: You are facilitating a sprint planning meeting. The attendees include seven developers, a scrum master, and a product owner. The team has already decomposed the selected highest-priority user stories from the product backlog into actionable tasks with estimates in hours. To establish the sprint backlog, you call for the fist-of-five technique. Five developers hold up three fingers, and two developers hold up two fingers. What should you do next? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Close the meeting, because the sprint plan is done
questions.option_b: Set the team's capacity for the current sprint to 19 hours
questions.option_c: Ask the two developers to explain their objections
questions.option_d: Ask the five developers to pull three stories to their queue
questions.explanation: According to the scenario, the team is involved in the Estimate Activity Durations process. Decision Making is among the tools an techniques of this process and is typically used during meetings. The question describes a sprint planning meeting where the team has been estimating the duration of each activity required to complete the work for the upcoming sprint. The next step is to validate that the selected amount of work is achievable within the time available during the sprint, based on the team's capacity, availability, skill, resources, and past performance. In this scenario, five developers held up three fingers. If this group of five had been the entire development team, their vote of three fingers would be enough to achieve a consensus. Notice that none of them fully support the currently selected sprint backlog. If any voters had held up five fingers, that would indicate complete support. The developers who held up less than three fingers should voice their objections. The team can then adjust the sprint backlog to resolve those objections. The team repeats the vote until a consensus is achieved, where all votes are three fingers or more. With a consensus, the team commits to the product owner that the sprint backlog will be complete in the upcoming sprint.

questions.prompt: After losing a member of her high performing team, the project manager implements the response planned for this risk by hiring an independent contractor. However, as a result of adding the new team member, the team is thrown back to the storming stage of team development, and the project falls behind schedule. How might the project manager have avoided this situation? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Developed a response plan for the residual risk
questions.option_c: Developed a response plan for the secondary risk
questions.explanation: A secondary risk is a risk that arises as a direct result of implementing a risk response. In this case, the risk response of replacing a team member introduced the secondary risk of the team member disrupting the cohesiveness of the high performing team represented by the team reverting to the storming stage of team development. Thus, the team falling behind schedule is the result of the secondary risk posed by the introduction a new member to the team. One cannot assume that a newly hired team member will be able to replace a member of a high performing team without some disruption. The project manager should have anticipated this secondary risk and developed an appropriate risk response. For example, the project manager could have included schedule reserves during project planning to address this secondary risk, which may have prevented the project from falling behind schedule. Therefore, of the choices provided, identifying the secondary risk and developing a response plan to address it could have helped avoid the situation described in the scenario.

questions.prompt: Your project team functions as a well-organized unit. The team members are interdependent and work through issues smoothly and effectively. What is your best course of action? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Provide clear direction on the project objectives and the behavior expected from the team
questions.option_b: Help the team to get through their struggle by ensuring they listen to each other and understand
questions.option_c: Ensure the team resolves conflicts quickly and continues to work collaboratively and step in only
questions.option_d: Serve as a gateway between the project team and the stakeholders and delegate decision making
questions.explanation: The question is in reference to the Tuckman ladder, which is one of the models used to describe team development. This model includes five stages of development that teams may go through including forming, storming, norming, performing, and adjourning. The question suggests that the project team had reached the performing stage of team development. Teams that reach the performing stage function as a well-organized unit, and the team members are interdependent and work through issues smoothly and effectively. This makes the life of project managers relatively easy and requires from them to mostly serve as a gateway between the project team and the stakeholders and delegate decision making to the team members.

questions.prompt: As a project manager for a small university, you are currently in the process of selecting a vendor to develop a textbook and other training materials for a new course. Several vendors are interested in bidding on the contract. During a bidder conference, one of the prospective suppliers asks about ownership of the intellectual property that will be created from the project. Which project document should have this information at this stage of the project? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The requirements documentation may include technical and nontechnical requirements that the seller is required to satisfy. Nontechnical requirements include requirements with contractual and legal implications including intellectual property rights among others. The question implies that the project manager is carrying out the Conduct Procurements process. Information on ownership of the intellectual property can be included in the requirements documentation which is an input to the Conduct Procurements process or the procurement contracts which are an output from the process. Based on the scenario described, a bidder conference takes place; therefore, it is reasonable to assume that no contracts have been awarded yet. This means that at this stage of the project, of the available options, the requirements documentation is the best place to find information on intellectual property rights.

questions.prompt: To fulfill project requirements, the performing organization must partner with an outside firm. The project manager revises the stakeholder register to reflect the new arrangement and marks the partner as downward in the classification under the directions of influence. What does that indicate? Top of Form
questions.option_a: The project manager and partner can share resources and information.
questions.option_b: The partner can influence the project with their specialized knowledge in a temporary capacity.
questions.option_c: The project manager can hold the partner in low esteem.
questions.option_d: The partner can influence the project manager to make decisions and acquire additional
questions.explanation: When identifying stakeholders on a project, a project manager can evaluate the stakeholders and their needs, expectations, involvement, impact, and many other aspects, including influence. 'Directions of influence' is an example of a data representation technique that can be used as part of the Identify Stakeholders, which is the process described in the scenario. Classifying the direction of a stakeholder's influence in relation to the project provides the project manager a conceptual understanding of how that stakeholder could shape or sway the project and the project team. Downward direction can refer to the team or to specialists that contribute knowledge or skills in a temporary capacity such as the partner described in the question. By classifying the partner as downward, the project manager indicates that the inclusion of the partner within the bounds of the performing organization enables the partner to influence from their downward point of reference the larger project and project organization. Apart from downward, other directions of influence, includes upward, sideward, and outward.

questions.prompt: You are beginning to develop a project schedule for the construction of a new pizza vending machine. When you reach the activity labeled as 6.5.3, you are unsure if a refrigeration specialist is needed for the installation of the cooling mechanism for the pizza dough. What project document would you reference in order to get information on the resources required for installing the cooling mechanism? Top of Form
questions.explanation: Activity attributes is a project document containing the resource requirements for a particular activity. The activity attributes for activity 6.5.3 would indicate what credentials, if any, are required to install the cooling mechanism. The resource requirements project document would also be a good answer, but since resource requirements are not provided as a choice, "activity attributes" is the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: You are executing an IT infrastructure project which requires a team with a variety of complex skillsets. Since many of the team members do not possess all of the skills required, you have spent a lot of time and money to get the team trained. However, as the training unfolds, your team reports that the training they are receiving is not a good match for the real needs of the project. How can you prevent this for future projects? Top of Form
questions.option_b: Perform updates to employee development plan records
questions.option_c: Rewrite the project's training and evaluation documentation
questions.option_d: Update training requirements in the company's learning and development system
questions.explanation: Based on the scenario, you are carrying out the Develop Team process. The source of the problem is that the training requirements were not correctly specified, resulting in wasted time and money. The immediate fix may require additional training with the associated loss of time and funds. But, in order to prevent the same issue from happening in the future, you need to revise the training requirements which are considered organizational process assets and are updated as an output of the Develop Team process. Note: It is likely that you will also want to add an entry in the lessons learned register; however, this option is not provided among the answer choices.

questions.prompt: According to the communications management plan, the project should use a dashboard to communicate information to stakeholders. Some team members are struggling to keep their project data current. What is the best action for the project manager? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Encourage team members to enter any information whenever they can
questions.option_b: Offer help to those team members who are struggling to report on time
questions.option_c: Have the team focus on their work and report the status on their behalf
questions.option_d: Reduce or adjust the format of those not able to keep their data current
questions.explanation: Manage Communications is the process of ensuring timely and appropriate collection, creation, storage, distribution, retrieval, monitoring, and disposal of project information. The question states that according to the project communications plan the project status and other information should be communicated on a project dashboard for all stakeholders to view. The data on the dashboard needs to be current enough to be relevant and valuable to stakeholders. Some team members are trying to do this but are having trouble. The project manager can lead assistance to help the team with their communications. This may be in many forms. If the assistance or removal of barriers helps, then changes might be needed. But it also starts with understanding the issues, the root causes, and seeking solutions.

questions.prompt: The project manager and the team decide that the project will be divided into five-week iterations with two weeks in between for retrospectives and iteration planning. Where should this approach be documented? Top of Form
questions.explanation: How the project is to manage and lay out its schedule is part of what is included in the schedule management plan. The Plan Schedule Management process pulls together and determines how the project is to manage the time and scheduling of a project. That schedule management plan can contain many components dependent on the project, the needs of the project, and the extent of documentation and thoroughness of the project and the project organization. In this scenario, the inclusion of the timeboxing format of the iterations would definitely be involved. Projects managed in an agile methodology aim to retain a consistent and repeatable iteration pattern. Those decisions and other relevant information are documented in the schedule management plan.

questions.prompt: You are currently in the process of determining, documenting, and managing stakeholder needs and requirements to meet project objectives. A project stakeholder has suggested that it would be helpful to have a visual depiction showing how people and other systems will interact with the new payroll system. You like the idea and want to address it. What is the best way to implement the stakeholder’s suggestion? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The question suggests that the project manager is performing the Collect Requirements process, which is the process of determining, documenting, and managing stakeholder needs and requirements to meet project objectives. A context diagram is one of the tools and techniques that might be used during the Collect Requirements process. A context diagram is a visual depiction of the product scope showing a business system (process, equipment, computer system, etc.), and how people and other systems (actors) interact with it. Of the available choices, developing the context diagram is the best way to implement the stakeholder’s suggestion.

questions.prompt: The updated version of the product is to be at the customer's location by the end of the week, but the deliverable, despite the team's best efforts will not be complete by then. The deployment team who is supposed to install the product at the customer site is under a lot of stress to meet the deadline and will be furious that the update will not be there on time. What is the project manager's best course of action? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Wait until the deadline hoping the team eventually makes it
questions.option_b: Send the status report to the customer
questions.option_c: Make the phone call to the customer to inform them of the delay
questions.option_d: Cancel the installation at the customer site
questions.explanation: Managing and monitoring the communications between project stakeholders, including the employees at the customer site, must benefit the project and its stakeholders. Withholding information that can be useful to stakeholders is a disservice. Project reporting is part of the tools and techniques of the Manage Communications process implied by the scenario. To communicate effectively, project managers have to employ their interpersonal and team skills, which are also among the tools and techniques of this process. In the scenario described, the project team has determined that they cannot meet the deadline, despite their best efforts. This inevitable outcome must be communicated to the customer, even if the conversation is uncomfortable. No communication or late communication would cause more damage.

questions.prompt: A key stakeholder has been resistant to the project's objectives, and the support of this stakeholder is vital to the success of the project. The project manager has employed various engagement strategies which have been ineffective and needs to determine the underlying reason that stakeholder engagement is not having the planned effect. What should the project manager do next? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Develop a stakeholder engagement assessment matrix
questions.option_c: Submit a change request to update the stakeholder engagement plan
questions.explanation: The scenario suggests that the Monitor Stakeholder Engagement process has identified a variance in the current and desired engagement level of a key stakeholder. The question asks about root cause analysis which is a data analysis technique that may be useful in performing the Monitor Stakeholder Engagement process. As it applies to this process, root cause analysis can be used to determine the basic underlying reason that stakeholder engagement is not having the planned effect. Thus, the project manager should perform a root cause analysis in this scenario. What makes this question challenging is that root cause analysis is not listed as an answer choice. There will likely be instances on the actual PMP exam where the obvious answer is not available, and the best option of the available responses must be selected.
