In this case, root cause analysis is a form of data analysis. Therefore, among the available choices, conducting a data analysis is the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: You are a project manager for an engineering services company, in the process of bidding to work on a solar energy project for a utility company. What is the first thing you should do to position your company as the best fit for the project? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Attend the bid conference to learn all you need to know about the utility company's
questions.option_b: Request an independent cost estimate to include in your seller response.
questions.option_c: Look for the selection method in the procurement documents of the utility company's bid
questions.option_d: Re-use a proposal that won selection by a telecommunications company.
questions.explanation: When being faced with procurement-related questions on the PMP exam, a prospective PMP aspirant has first to determine what side of the procurement the scenario is talking about. While the majority of procurement-related questions on the exam describe the situation from a buyer's point of view, some questions are worded from the seller's perspective. This question is an example of the latter: you are the seller (or a bidder, a vendor). Therefore, some of the answer choices can be eliminated as they refer to processes, inputs, outputs, tools or techniques that are buyer-related. As it pertains to the scenario described, it is a good practice for buyers to include their selected evaluation method in the procurement documents, so bidders know how they will be evaluated. Commonly used selection methods include least cost, qualifications only, quality-based/highest technical proposal score, quality and cost-based, sole source, or fixed budget. To position your company as the best fit for the project, it is essential for you to understand the requirements and selection criteria that you need to meet as a seller. Therefore, of the choices provided, looking for the selection method in the procurement documents of the utility company's bid package is the best answer to the question asked. Note, the complete source selection criteria is not always provided by the buyer to the seller. These criteria are often internal for the buying organization. However, some subset of the criteria can be exposed to the prospective sellers to provide them with a better understanding of what the buyer is looking for besides the technical details of the solution.

questions.prompt: To utilize a few employees who are not assigned to any projects this month, the functional manager can authorize a project specifically for them. There are two project ideas that have been vetted. One is an IT project to implement a new software tool. Another is a charity project for the community. Alas, there is only enough budget for one of the projects. How should the functional manager approach the project chartering? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Evaluate the business value of each of the projects to determine which one brings more value to
questions.option_b: Charter the IT project of the new software tool implementation since it is likely to provide a
questions.option_c: Charter the charity project for the community since it is likely to have a positive, long-lasting,
questions.option_d: Charter both projects with a reduced scope that can be covered by the available budget to gain a
questions.explanation: The question states that the functional manager can only sponsor and charter one project at this point. The functional manager then must select a project that makes the most sense for the functional area and the company. What is best is a factor of many things. In this scenario, since the project selection method is not provided, business value is the deciding factor. Business value is the net quantifiable benefit derived from a business endeavor. The benefit may be tangible, intangible, or both. Business value is considered the return, in the form of elements such as time, money, goods, or intangibles in return for something exchanged. As such, factoring in the value to be gained or lost by chartering a project is the most viable variable for the functional manager to consider.

questions.prompt: A project manager for a software development project has collected the work performance data and used the requirements documentation and requirements traceability matrix to generate the work performance information regarding variances in the project work compared to the baseline. What process is the project manager performing? Top of Form
questions.explanation: To answer this question correctly, it is important to understand how work performance data is transformed into work performance information. Work performance data is produced as a result of project execution. The work performance data can then be analyzed during various controlling processes to produce work performance information (see the image below). Work performance information is only produced as an output during the Monitoring and Controlling Process Group. In this scenario, the question suggests that a variance analysis has been performed using work performance data in order to produce work performance information. Thus, it can be concluded that the process underway is part of the Monitoring and Controlling Process Group, and the two answer choices representing executing processes can be eliminated. Both the Validate Scope and Control Scope processes produce work performance information as an output. However, only the Control Scope process uses variance analysis to produce work performance information regarding variances in the project work compared to the scope baseline. Therefore, of the available choices, the project manager must be executing the Control Scope process.

questions.prompt: A company stores all of its documentation on the corporate computer network. Each project is assigned a directory with folders. A team member has been tasked to send the request for proposal (RFP), source selection criteria, and procurement statement of work (SOW) to potential sellers. Which folder from the project directory should the team member access to find these documents? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The question suggests that the Conduct Procurements process is underway. Procurement documentation provides a written record used in reaching the legal agreement and can include bid documents, procurement statement of work, independent cost estimates, and the source selection criteria. Of the available options, only the procurement documentation contains all of the elements specified by the question. Note, source selection criteria may or may not be sent to prospective sellers. This would typically depend on the performing organization's policies. Sometimes, only part of the source selection criteria is sent to the prospective sellers while some criteria are kept confidential for internal use of the performing organization.

questions.prompt: You are planning a project to design and manufacture a new style of personal watercraft. The product will be developed and produced in-house as the company has the warehouse space and production facilities available to support the project. As you prepare the project budget, you need to know whether or not to include prorated rent, utilities, and other such costs. Where is the best place to find this information? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The question suggests that the Determine Budget process is being carried out. Inputs into the Determine Budget process include project documents such as the basis of estimates. Rent and utilities as described in the question are examples of indirect costs. Supporting detail for cost estimates in the basis of estimates should specify any basic assumptions dealing with the inclusion or exclusion of indirect or other costs in the project budget. Therefore, the basis of estimates is the best source to obtain a determination on the inclusion of rent and utilities.

questions.prompt: You are about to start the development of the assumption log for the project you are leading. What should you do first? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The assumption log is a project document used to record all assumptions and constraints throughout the project life cycle. The assumption log is created as an output of the Develop Project Charter process, as such limited information is available to create this project document. High-level strategic and operational assumptions and constraints are normally identified in the business case before the project is initiated and will flow into the project charter. Lower-level activity and task assumptions are generated throughout the project. The assumption log is used to record all assumptions and constraints throughout the project life cycle. Therefore, of the available options, the project business case is the best source of information to use in the development of the assumption log.

questions.prompt: During project execution, after investing in the training of your project team members, you notice that one of them consistently exceeds expectations while performing her tasks. To have the project benefit from the team member's skills and reduce the risk of missing defects in the project deliverables, you have tasked the team member to handle the most advanced quality inspections. Where would you record the new duties of this team member? Top of Form
questions.explanation: Develop Team is the process of improving competencies, team member interaction, and the overall team environment to enhance project performance. In the scenario, after you invest in team training, you notice a high performer and task her with advanced responsibilities to improve project performance, implying you are involved in the Develop Team process. When team development results in changes to assignments, the changes are recorded in the project team assignments documentation. Updates to project team assignments is among the outputs of the Develop Team process. While there are many other places to record individual capabilities, this question is focused on changes to assignments, making project team assignments the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: A team member reports that the risk she is assigned as an owner has occurred and will have a significant negative impact on the project. What should the project manager do first? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Use management reserves to cover the costs associated with the risk response plan
questions.option_b: Reprimand the team member for being unable to keep the risk from occurring
questions.option_c: Advise the team member to check project documents for the risk response plan
questions.option_d: Conduct the root-cause analysis to determine why the risk has happened
questions.explanation: The scenario indicates that the project manager is going to conduct the Implement Risk Responses process. Project documents are an input to this process. The risk register is a project document that records the agreed-upon risk responses for each individual risk and the nominated owners for each response plan. Ideally, if the risk planning has been done properly on the project, all or most of the risks are supposed to have their corresponding responses. While there is not enough information in the question to determine if a response has been planned for this risk, of the choices provided, advising the team member to check project documents for the risk response plan is the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: A vendor on a time and materials (T&M) contract to deliver corn for testing a new ethanol production facility is able to acquire the contracted materials at a dramatically lower price than expected due to a crash in the commodities market. The corn represents the single biggest expense for the project. What is the best course of action? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Allow the project to proceed significantly under budget
questions.option_b: Submit a change request to update the cost baseline
questions.option_c: Split the cost savings with the vendor
questions.explanation: The question describes a situation where project costs will be significantly less than originally planned. Updates to the cost baseline are typically the result of costs exceeding the established contingency reserves. However, although not common, the cost baseline can be impacted (positively) by significant cost savings. If the cost savings were relatively minor, then the project would likely be allowed to just proceed under budget, but the question describes a situation where the biggest single project cost has been cut dramatically. The main reason to update the cost baseline is to free up the extra capital. By updating the cost baseline, the cost savings can be transferred from the project to the performing organization where the funds can be used for other projects or priorities. Having resources tied up where they are not required is a form of waste, which can and should be eliminated. An approved change request is required to update the cost baseline. Therefore, of the available options, submitting a change request to update the cost baseline is the best course of action.

questions.prompt: As part of a larger initiative involving many projects, all the activities from this and all of the other projects have to be aligned and be carried out perfectly. What is the best course of action for the project manager? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Coordinate with the program manager to ensure accurate direction
questions.option_b: Execute the work according to the directions of the project management office (PMO)
questions.option_c: Follow the project team around to remove anyone not performing
questions.option_d: Submit a change request to merge the project management plans of all involved projects
questions.explanation: The scenario implies the projects are part of a program. Programs consist of projects that are better managed and coordinated with unified oversight than as only individuals projects. Program management in this scenario comes about due to the various projects underway all working on similar objectives, outcomes, and objectives. The question suggests that efforts and deliverables from multiple projects are planned to deliver one conceive entity. Coordination and communication between projects and between the project and the program manager are crucial for the success of this major initiative. Therefore, of the choices provided, coordinating with the program manager to ensure accurate direction is the best course of action for the project manager.

questions.prompt: Your organization has a dedicated procurement department. Per your request, the department prepares the procurement documentation and, during the next week, will request bids from the prospective vendors. You really want XYZ Incorporated to be selected. What can you say to XYZ Incorporated? Top of Form
questions.option_a: They should expect an invitation to bid by next week.
questions.option_c: You can say nothing related to the bid while the bid is being prepared.
questions.option_d: Price is the deciding factor in vendor selection.
questions.explanation: When conducting the procurement activities on a project, it is important that you remain fair, honest, and ethical. The PMI Code of Ethics and Professional Conduct expects this standard of behavior of every project manager. Procurement professionals should also conduct themselves in a fair, honest, and ethical manner. In buyer-seller transactions, it is extremely important to ensure that all parties are given equal treatment and equal footing. Providing any inside information, regardless of how small, could be interpreted as extending an unfair advantage to one potential vendor over the others. The bid documents are going to announce the terms and evaluation criteria of the procurement, including the price, timing, and other details. The procurement department will send all relevant bid documents all prospective vendors at the same time. No one should be receiving information that others are not. The communications should follow the rules and terms of the bidding procedures, which implies that you can say nothing related to the bid while the bid is being prepared.

questions.prompt: You are managing a hotel construction project, and you have asked your project team to calculate the current earned value metrics to determine the project performance. Your team reports back that the project's to-complete performance index (TCPI) is 0.9 based on the budget at completion (BAC). Which of the following is true regarding your project's current situation as reflected by this TCPI value? Top of Form
questions.option_d: The project is ahead of schedule
questions.explanation: The to-complete performance index (TCPI) is a measure of the cost performance that is required to be achieved with the remaining resources in order to meet a specified management goal, expressed as the ratio of the cost to finish the outstanding work to the remaining budget. A TCPI value equal to 1.0 means you are right on budget, and as long as you maintain the present cost performance index (CPI), you should not exceed the project budget. A TCPI value greater than 1.0 means you need to take a stricter cost management approach or you will exceed your budget at completion (BAC). A TCPI value less than 1.0 indicates you are within your budget and have done an excellent job of managing project costs.

questions.prompt: A project manager is starting the Plan Risk Management process. The project sponsor is risk averse, and this will affect the risk management plan. In preparing this plan, the project manager is seeking a high-level understanding of the project description and boundaries, high-level requirements, and risks. Where is the best place for the project manager to find this information? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The project charter is issued by the sponsor and documents the high-level project description and boundaries, high-level requirements, and risks. The project charter is an input to the Plan Risk Management process and is the best place for the project manager to gain a high-level understanding of the project.

questions.prompt: Due to favorable weather conditions, the heating equipment you have rented for your construction project is no longer required. Following the resource management plan, you are returning the equipment earlier than originally planned to realize cost savings. What should you do first? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Use the saved funds to hold a team building activity
questions.option_c: Let the project sponsor know about cost savings
questions.explanation: Control Resources is the process of ensuring that the physical resources assigned and allocated to the project are available as planned, as well as monitoring the planned versus actual utilization of resources and performing any corrective action as necessary. In this case, the actual utilization of the heating equipment was lessened by favorable weather conditions. The physical resource assignments represent one of the project documents that may be updated as part of the Control Resources process. Documentation of the physical resource assignments records the material, equipment, supplies, locations, and other physical resources that are used during the project. The incorrect answer choices represent actions which should not be necessary in this case. Therefore, of the available options, updating the physical resource assignments is what you, as the project manager, should do next.

questions.prompt: An organization has a policy that directs to use only prequalified vendors. A project manager finds a logistics company that offers much better prices than its competitors. The project manager wants to add the vendor to the list, but the procedure can take several months due to the rigorous qualification process. What should the project manager do? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Add the new logistics company to the prequalified vendors list and use their services for the
questions.option_b: Work with the purchasing department to add the new logistics company to the prequalified
questions.option_c: Select the new logistics company since their price is the best among all other prequalified vendors
questions.option_d: Ignore the new logistics company since they are not on the prequalified vendors list
questions.explanation: The scenario implies the Conduct Procurements process is underway. Organizational Process Assets (OPAs) are an input to this process. The OPAs that apply to this scenario include prequalified seller lists and established policies regarding the exclusive use of prequalified sellers. The question states that the performing organization's policy requires the use of prequalified vendors. The reason for prequalifying sellers is typically to check credit, delivery history, longevity, references, and the like to reduce the purchasing organization's risk exposure. Even though it takes a long time to get qualified, working with the purchasing department to get the new logistics company added to the preferred seller list is the only reasonable and fair solution that is consistent with company policy and is, therefore, of the choices given, the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: The work is getting done, and the project is on track. However, the project manager does not understand why so many team members keep requesting to be moved off the project. As far as the project manager is concerned, all she is trying to do is to ensure that the work is being completed as specified in the project management plan. What might be the most likely cause? Top of Form
questions.option_a: The project manager gives the team too much work.
questions.option_b: The team lacks the training needed to complete project activities.
questions.option_c: The project manager lacks emotional intelligence.
questions.option_d: The project manager does not clearly communicate the project's objectives.
questions.explanation: There can be many reasons team members wish to leave a project. But in this scenario, the trend of people asking to leave the project over the course of the project life cycle seems to be more than just the work or the understanding of the project objectives. The root cause may be the project manager herself. Emotional intelligence (EI) is a range of competencies around the ability to identify, assess, and manage the personal emotions of oneself and other people, as well as the collective emotions of groups of people. As such, the project manager may not be observing or recognizing the emotions of others and/or herself. The breakdown of relationships and the collapse of a healthy environment may lead to higher staff turnover. Investing more in EI may translate to less project team turnover.

questions.prompt: The project manager is concerned that the new system planned to be delivered by the project may not be adopted by the end users in the department due to their anti-change culture and resistance to new initiatives as seen in the past. What is the best course of action for the project manager? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Build an FAQ website and respond to every end user inquiry quickly
questions.option_b: Have the sponsor announce the value of the new system and how everybody is to benefit from it
questions.option_c: Meet with the end users and listen to what they have to say
questions.option_d: Get the new system in the hands of end users as early as possible and let them see its benefits
questions.explanation: Achieving and managing stakeholder engagement in the project can be easy in some cases, incredibly difficult in others, and every point in between. According to the scenario, there is a culture of hesitation and resistance to new programs and projects in the department. Based on the past projects, the end users in the department felt disenfranchised and uninvolved. The project manager must overcome these stakeholder engagement hurdles by encouraging more stakeholder involvement and buy-in. The first step towards achieving that starts with open dialogue and active listening. The other methods of communicating and engaging with these stakeholders can potentially be useful, if determined appropriate, at a later time. Communication skills as well as interpersonal and team skills are among the tools and techniques that can be used by project managers as part of the Manage Stakeholder Engagement process implied by the scenario.

questions.prompt: The project manager and project team have worked together for many years. There is a deep sense of community and collaboration. Everyone knows their stuff and gets better with every project. Which leadership style of the project manager is most effective in deepening those connections? Top of Form
questions.explanation: Project managers must lead and manage the project team. Dealing with people, like those on the project team, and getting things done requires a project manager to apply various leadership styles within a project and sometimes across projects. There is no one 'correct' way to lead a project. Circumstances in this scenario suggest that the project manager's style to best be associated with servant leadership. Servant leadership puts the team first and seeks to enhance the connections and collaboration of the team. The project manager can serve as an encourager. Pushing the team forward by clearing the way, removing distractions, and supporting the team would help the team and the project to achieve the project objectives. Applying this style of leadership then enhances the connections and community aspects of the team.

questions.prompt: The project sponsor is concerned about possible ethics violations between the project and vendors. What independent review can the sponsor order? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The scenario implies the Control Procurements process should be carried out to determine if any ethics violations between the project and vendors took place. To improve the procurement process and proceedings, organizations seek reviews independent of the project via procurement audits. Conducting these audits independent of the team intimately involved in the project enables the review of the procurement activities and process without influence. The organization's code of ethics, its legal counsel, and external legal advisory arrangements including any ongoing anti-corruption initiatives can contribute to proper procurement controls. A procurement audit reviews the contracts and the contracting process for completeness, accuracy, and effectiveness. If there are any ethical violations as the project sponsor speculates, it is possible those will come to life during a procurement audit. The violations would unlikely emerge from performance reviews, assessments of the project team, or a Monte-Carlo simulation.

questions.prompt: A vendor supplies highly specialized subject matter experts for the project you are leading. The vendor has just informed you that a key resource essential to the development of one of the project’s deliverables will not be available for one month due to unforeseeable circumstances beyond the vendor's control. The contract with the vendor contains a force majeure clause. What should you do first? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Contact corporate counsel to note the vendor is in breach of contract
questions.option_b: Tell the vendor they need to pay damages for lost time while the resource is out
questions.option_c: Revise the resource calendar to reflect resource availability
questions.option_d: Inform stakeholders that the project would not be able to deliver on schedule
questions.explanation: The circumstances that led to the absence of the subject matter expert are likely to be considered a force majeure. A force majeure can be described as an unforeseeable circumstance or event that prevents a party from fulfilling a contract. Contracts typically contain a force majeure clause which alleviates a party to the contract of their contractual obligations. Thus, it is unlikely that the performing organization will have any legal recourse with the vendor for a breach of contract. Regardless, this situation will require the project manager to update the resource calendar to account for the unavailability of the subject matter expert. A resource calendar is a calendar that identifies the working days and shifts upon which each specific resource is available. The situation described by the question will likely trigger several actions to mitigate the impact of the issue on the project. However, of the available answer choices, the only viable response is to revise the resource calendar to reflect resource availability. Note, while the topic of force majeure is not covered by the PMBOK® Guide, the PMP® Examination Content Outline assumes that the prospective PMP® aspirants are familiar with knowledge and skills associated with contract types, procurement planning, vendor management, conflict resolution, etc. that are required to perform to the industry-wide standard in the role of a project manager.

questions.prompt: The plan to have 80% of the 10,000 employees enrolled in the new system by the end of the month is nowhere near target. The project team has sent multiple emails to the entire company with the reminders to enroll. What should the project manager do? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Instruct the team to send another email stressing the importance of the deadline
questions.option_b: Evaluate project communications and the communication strategy
questions.option_c: Offer an incentive to managers who get 80% or greater of their employees enrolled
questions.option_d: Submit a change request to update the communications management plan
questions.explanation: Monitor Communications is the process of ensuring the information needs of the project, and its stakeholders are met. The poor results of the employees receiving and following through on the communications indicate the needs of the project and its stakeholders are not being met. Instead of increasing the currently ineffective communication methodologies and attempting another communications strategy without much thought or analysis, the project manager and the project team must evaluate the current communications and analyze why they are ineffective. Knowing what is not working and why the project team can develop better communications to fulfill the needs of the project and its stakeholders.

questions.prompt: You are planning a project to construct a luxury condominium complex that will include jacuzzi tubs among its numerous features. Due to technical constraints, it has been decided to eliminate the jacuzzi tubs from the final project requirements. You document the jacuzzi tubs as an explicit scope exclusion in the project scope statement as well as update the requirements documentation to reflect this change. What should you do next? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Reflect the scope exclusion in the requirements traceability matrix
questions.option_b: Document the status of the change in the change log
questions.option_c: Record the elimination of the jacuzzi tubs as an issue in the issue log
questions.option_d: Capture the exclusion of the jacuzzi tubs in the exclusion register
questions.explanation: The scenario suggests you are in the Define Scope process developing the scope statement for your project. As part of this process, final project requirements are selected, and a detailed description of the project and product is developed. Requirements that have been eliminated are explicitly documented as exclusions in the project scope statement. Updates of project documents, such as the requirements traceability matrix are among the outputs of the Define Scope process. Since you have added the jacuzzi tubs as an exclusion in the scope statement, of the choices available, only updates to project documents are required next. Therefore, reflecting the scope exclusion in the requirements traceability matrix is the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: You are planning the must-have features of a new product in detail so that work can begin and the product can be released to the market as early as possible. The future features are unknown at the moment and will depend on what the competitors release. You will plan these features in greater detail later on. As you and your team create the work breakdown structure (WBS), what will you use to indicate the future features? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The WBS is a hierarchical decomposition of the total scope of work to be carried out by the project team to accomplish the project objectives and create the required deliverables. The planned work is contained in work packages, the lowest level of WBS components, where work can be scheduled, estimated, monitored, and controlled. In projects using rolling wave planning, as described in the scenario, decomposition may not be possible for deliverables that will be completed later in the project. The future features are an example of this type of deliverable. You know there will be features added later in the project, but you are not able to decompose them down to the level that the work can be scheduled, estimated, monitored, and controlled. In this case, the future features will be added to the WBS as planning packages. When more information about the future features is available, the planning packages will be decomposed into work packages.

questions.prompt: An unexpectedly high turnover of contracted developers threatens the objectives of the project that you are managing. To address this issue, you want to increase the rate of pay for the developers and add a bonus for completing the project on scope and schedule. You estimate that the management reserves will be required to cover the cost. What is your best course of action? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Use contingency reserves as necessary to cover the extra costs
questions.option_b: Request the additional funds from the project sponsor
questions.option_c: Issue a change request to transfer the management reserves into the cost baseline
questions.option_d: Submit a change request to increase the project budget
questions.explanation: Control Resources is the process of ensuring that the physical resources assigned and allocated to the project are available as planned, as well as monitoring the planned versus actual utilization of resources and performing any necessary corrective action. Thus, the question implies that the Control Resources process is being performed in this scenario. Project management plan updates, including the cost baseline, are one of the outputs of the Control Resources process. Changes to the project cost baseline may be required to reflect the way project resources are being managed. In this case, you need to access the management reserves in order to meet the project's objectives. The management reserves are included in the project budget but are not part of the cost baseline. A project manager only has direct control over the funds in the cost baseline. An approved change request is required to transfer the management reserves into the cost baseline. Therefore, of the available options, your best course of action is to issue a change request to transfer the management reserves into the cost baseline. Note, the scenario does not provide enough information on how it was determined that the management reserves will be required to cover the cost. Therefore, this determination should be accepted as a fact and considered while selecting the correct answer.

questions.prompt: You are managing an IT infrastructure development project. The project is being monitored for several performance indicators such as the percentage of tasks completed on time, the number of defects identified per day, and the number of change requests that have been submitted. How will you know if your project is compliant with these indicators? Top of Form
questions.option_a: By comparing the results to the quality management plan
questions.option_c: By comparing the results to the quality metrics
questions.explanation: The scenario suggests the project is in the Control Quality process as performance indicators are being monitored and compared against an acceptable range of variation. The quality metrics are developed as an output of the Plan Quality Management process and used as an input to the Control Quality process. Quality metrics define which areas of the project will be measured and what the acceptable range of variation will be. Therefore, to determine if the project is compliant in the areas that are monitored in the scenario, the results should be compared to the quality metrics.

questions.prompt: As a project manager, you are leading a century-old building renovation project, where theft of equipment, materials, and supplies has been a problem. You have found that moving anything of value that is portable into a single secured room of the building has significantly reduced the losses with minimal cost. What should you do next? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Include the information in the lessons learned repository
questions.option_b: Add the daily security task to the milestone schedule
questions.option_c: Include the information in the lessons learned register
questions.option_d: Discuss the situation with the team during the next retrospective
questions.explanation: The question describes the adoption of a best practice which has been found to be effective in controlling project resources. The lessons learned register is a project document used to record knowledge gained during a project so that it can be used in the current project and then entered into the lessons learned repository at the end of the project. Although two of the incorrect answer choices represent actions that should be performed, the question is asking what should be done 'next' in this scenario. When knowledge is gained during a project, the information should be recorded in the lessons learned register to be available for the remainder of the project. Therefore, of the available choices, including the information in the lessons learned register is what you should do next.

questions.prompt: A new member joins the project team. The project manager wants to get the new member up to speed on how project communications are conducted. What is the best action for the project manager? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Have a project team member show her the communication methods and tools being used
questions.option_b: Provide her with a report of completed work packages and the current project status
questions.option_c: Forward her all project communications and share with her links to the online resources
questions.option_d: Add her name to the stakeholder register and introduce her to the project sponsor
questions.explanation: New people are added to projects at various points in the project lifecycle, especially very large projects spanning years. Whether small or large or anywhere in between, new project team members and other stakeholders need to be able to access and acquire the communications fitting for their needs and their roles. To be effective and to onboard a project quickly and understand what is going on, a new project team member needs more than past and present communications dumped on them. They need to understand the communication methods used and expected by the project. They need to comprehend the communication technologies leveraged by the project and how to use them. These are all aspects of managing the project communications and is an example of the Manage Communications process described in the scenario.

questions.prompt: During the project's iterations, the daily standup meetings have been held at 9 AM every day. Three of the project team members have missed or been late to the last seven meetings because the team members are running tests in another building at that time. These tests will most likely continue for the rest of the iteration. What is the project manager's best course of action? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Start the meeting once everyone is present
questions.option_c: Keep on holding the meetings as scheduled
questions.option_d: Move the meeting to a set time later in the day
questions.explanation: Meetings are a tool of the Monitor Communications process. As projects move through the iterations when managed in an agile manner, daily standup meetings are the cornerstone of good team communications. The structure of a standup meeting may vary greatly from one team or one project to the next. What is most important is that these meetings are held at the same time and place for a pre-determined cadence beneficial to the team. Whether every day or once a week or some other timing, the meeting is designed to include as many of the project team members to briefly meet, share updates, ask for assistance, and align efforts. If the timing of the meeting does not work for part of the team, the project manager should seek to accommodate the team by seeking more beneficial times. Canceling or continuously moving the meeting times does not help the team's communication in this scenario.

questions.prompt: An organization is considering rolling out a new technology that could enhance employee productivity and excitement. Some have warned that the new technology could be out-of-date soon after roll out and there would be substantial costs in material and end-user training. Management is unsure whether to green light this project. What is the best course of action for the organization to take before making a decision? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Validate the scope of the technology roll out to confirm that the technology increases employee
questions.option_b: Determine if the benefits of the project implementation will be greater than the costs in material
questions.option_c: Conduct a benefit analysis to validate the project's alignment with organizational strategy and
questions.option_d: Survey developers of the technologies under consideration to better understand all the technical
questions.explanation: There are many reasons to initiate a project. New technology is one such option. Nonetheless, what is most important is how a project aligns with business needs and strategies. Analyzing business needs and strategies helps determine whether or not a project brings value to the business. Other information and criteria may assist in making a more educated and comprehensive decision, but the most important aspect to consider is the business value to be gained or lost by initiating and completing a project, not the technology on its own.

questions.prompt: A project manager had originally planned to use two industrial 3D printers simultaneously for a project but has just learned that one of them will be unavailable. The project manager adjusts the schedule so that the components are created sequentially using one printer. Unfortunately, this action affects the critical path and delays the planned finish date of the project. What tool or technique has the project manager used in this situation? Top of Form
questions.explanation: Resource leveling is a resource optimization technique in which start and finish dates are adjusted based on resource constraints with the goal of balancing the demand for the resources with the available supply. Resource leveling can often cause the original critical path to change which is what happened in the scenario described. Adjusting the schedule to accommodate the loss of one of the 3D printers is an example of resource leveling. Therefore, of the choices provided, it can be reasonably assumed that the project manager performed resource leveling.

questions.prompt: A number of projects are under consideration for the upcoming quarter. The Director of Global Systems strongly believes that one of the projects, a system upgrade, is needed and aligned with the long-term business strategy. At the executive steering committee meeting scheduled to select projects for the upcoming quarter, what action should the Director take? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Advocate for the project with the highest NPV
questions.option_c: Present the merits of the system upgrade project
questions.explanation: Projects are selected for many reasons. Not all desired projects can be undertaken by an organization. Decisions as to which projects an organization is to take on are often part of an executive steering committee's responsibility. To get a certain project sponsored and chartered, the Director in this scenario must present the merits and value to the business to the steering committee. Upon selection and approval, the project charter for the project can be commissioned and the project made official.

questions.prompt: Labor costs during the winter months were far higher than expected. What is the best action for the project manager and project team? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Allocate additional funds to the project
questions.option_b: Record this information in the lessons learned register
questions.option_d: Schedule more work during the summer months
questions.explanation: There is plenty that can be learned during a project. Capturing and managing those lessons that have been learned is an important part of a project manager's, project team's, the project organization's growth and improvement. The lessons learned register could be updated for many reasons, such as the techniques that were or were not effective, measurement tools used, forecasting methods, and in the case of the scenario, learning from conditions and events that impacted the control of project costs. What is learned from the capturing and analyzing of the lessons turn into actions, such as trying to schedule more activities during non-winter months, allocating more funds, or preparing forecasts to be closer to actuals.

questions.prompt: An opportunity has triggered some previously approved risk responses. After the primary responses failed to produce the desired results, you discuss fallback plans with your team. The sponsor is concerned and is asking for the current overall risk exposure. What should you do? Top of Form
questions.option_b: Look in the lessons learned register
questions.option_c: Refer the sponsor to the risk register
questions.option_d: Provide the sponsor with the risk report
questions.explanation: In the scenario described, you are discussing with your team the results of implementing risk responses, implying you are in the Monitor Risks process. The risk report can be updated as an output of this process to provide an aggregated view of the collective impact of all identified risks on your project schedule, budget, quality, and other project constraints, which constitutes the current overall project risk exposure. The risk report may also specify the results of Monte Carlo S-curves, tornado diagram, criticality analysis to determine the contingency reserve required to account for risk exposure. To assist with the implementation of risk responses, this report also describes the agreed-upon risk response strategy and the major individual project risks with their planned responses.

questions.prompt: A project manager is leading a project to manufacture a new type of wheel for the automobile industry. The first lot of wheels has been created, and the project manager wants to know if the quality objectives have been met. What should the project manager do next? Top of Form
questions.option_c: Use the test and evaluation documents
questions.explanation: Test and evaluation documents are created in the Manage Quality process and provide a format with which the project team can evaluate whether quality objectives have been met. Examples of test and evaluation documents are checklists and detailed requirements traceability matrices. These documents are an output of the Manage Quality process and an input to the Control Quality process where they are used to evaluate the achievement of quality objectives. According to the scenario, the first lot of the wheels has been manufactured. Therefore, of the choices provided, to evaluate if the lot is compliant with the quality specifications, the next step for the project manager would be to use the test and evaluation documents created in the Manage Quality process.

questions.prompt: A project has just been approved and formally chartered. What is one of the first things the assigned project manager should do? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Start the development of the project management plan
questions.option_b: Acquire the resources necessary to perform the project work
questions.option_c: Inform stakeholders of the approved project charter
questions.option_d: Perform a project assessment based on available information
questions.explanation: Based on the scenario, it can be implied that the Develop Project Charter process has just been completed. The project charter formally authorizes the project and documents the high-level goals and business value, as well as other key information. Communicating the final, approved information captured in the charter to all affected stakeholders ensures a common understanding of the objectives, expected deliverables, milestones, and the stakeholder's expected involvement. Of the choices provided, informing stakeholders of the approved project charter is one of the first things the assigned project manager should do and is, therefore, the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: A project manager is determining a cost baseline for a project to develop a new sales training course. As part of this process, the project manager sums up the lower-level cost estimates from the work packages in the work breakdown structure (WBS). What tool or technique is the project manager using in this process? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The question suggests that the Determine Budget process is underway. One of the tools and techniques used during the Determine Budget process is that of cost aggregation. Cost aggregation is conducted by summing the lower-level cost estimates associated with the various work packages for a given level within the project’s work breakdown structure (WBS). Based on the description provided by the scenario, the project manager is performing cost aggregation.

questions.prompt: Management has called you in as they are evaluating and laying out the expectations of an upcoming project. They ask you if you want to be involved in the meetings since you are most likely to be the project manager assigned to the project. You are leading another project, and your schedule is very busy. What is your best response? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Accept the offer since a project manager must be involved in a project starting with the Develop
questions.option_b: Decline the offer since the involvement of a project manager in a new project does not begin until
questions.option_c: Accept the offer since it is beneficial for a project manager to understand the project objectives
questions.option_d: Decline the offer since you are leading another project, and project managers are not allowed to
questions.explanation: The role of the project manager does not formally start until the project has been approved and chartered. Nevertheless, it is highly advantageous for a potential project manager to be involved as much as possible and as early as possible in the crafting and direction of the project charter. Having the project manager collaborate with the project initiators allows the project manager to better understand the project objectives, vision, purpose, expected outcomes, and benefits. The best decision is for the project manager to be involved in the discussions and assist in chartering the project.

questions.prompt: The various teams working on your project have gone through an extensive array of training and development activities specified in the resource management plan to ensure optimum performance. However, one of the teams has seen substantial turnover since the training was completed. You realize that for this particular team, you will have to identify specific training and coaching to align the competencies of these new team members with the rest of the teams. What is the best approach for you to take in this situation? Top of Form
questions.option_b: Repeat the training regime for the team
questions.explanation: Team performance assessments are the output of the Develop Team process. The best way to measure team performance is by assessment. In this case, the assessment should be given to the high-turnover team to ensure that you can most effectively target any shortcomings. It will not be useful to base your adjustments on past performance, because a substantial part of the team consists of new members.

questions.prompt: A project to design and develop a new prototype medical device will require several vendors to develop the subcomponents. As you review the responses to the RFP, you realize that all the vendors plan to re-use your competitors' designs. This "re-use" is unacceptable for your company. What is your best course of action? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Select a vendor based on the source selection criteria as specified by the approved project
questions.option_b: Add the intellectual property clause to the requirements documentation and ask the vendors to
questions.option_c: Select a vendor based on the source selection criteria and then add the intellectual property
questions.option_d: Notify the legal department about the breach of contract and initiate alternative dispute
questions.explanation: According to the scenario, you are reviewing the responses to the RFP, implying the project is in the Conduct Procurements process. During this process, the requirements documentation may be updated to incorporate requirements with contractual and legal implications including intellectual property rights. The relevant criteria from the requirements documentation are then included in the bid documents including the request for proposal (RFP). In this scenario, the question suggests that the retention of intellectual property rights was not included as a requirement in the request for proposal (RFP), and the vendors based their proposals on the assumption that they would be able to re-use the design with competing companies. Since the proposals were based upon an invalid assumption, it will be necessary to update the requirements documentation and ask the vendors to revise their bids accordingly.

questions.prompt: Midway into project execution, the director of marketing leaves the company and is replaced by an external candidate. After meeting with the new stakeholder, the project manager learns that the new director of marketing has a much greater interest in the project, and would like to be updated on progress and project specifics with greater frequency. With the stakeholder register updated to reflect the relevant information, what should the project manager do next? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Review and revise the stakeholder engagement plan as necessary
questions.option_b: Perform stakeholder analysis for the newly identified stakeholder
questions.option_c: Update the project documents with the information for the new stakeholder
questions.option_d: Submit a change request to update the communications management plan
questions.explanation: The communications management plan is a component of the project management plan that describes how, when, and by whom information about the project will be administered and disseminated. The communications management plan includes information on who will receive project information, including information about their needs, requirements, and expectations. The new director of marketing would like to be updated on progress and project specifics with greater frequency than her predecessor. This information should be reflected in the communication management plan. However, since the project is underway, implying the project management plan has been approved, a change request is required for any subsidiary plan to be modified. The incorrect answer choices either have already been completed or cannot be done without first seeking an approved change request. Therefore, of the available options, submitting a change request to update the communications management plan is what the project manager should do next.

questions.prompt: A project manager is currently managing a manufacturing process improvement project which will be completed in five phases. The project manager has prepared the following chart to review the status of the project. Based on the information provided, what is the earned value of the project? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The question is asking for the current earned value (EV) of the project thus far. Earned value (EV) is the sum of the planned value of completed work. The define and measure phases are complete with planned values of $1,000 and $3,000 respectively. The analyze phase is 75% complete with a planned value of $5,000, which means the EV for the analyze phase is $3,750. Therefore, the sum of the planned values of the completed work is $1,000 + $3,000 + $3,750 which is a total EV of $7,750.

questions.prompt: A project manager is leading a project to streamline the manufacturing process of modular homes. The project manager researches and discovers a set of technical guidelines that will make the homes easier and cheaper to manufacture without compromising quality. The team incorporates these guidelines by designing a new modular home that reduces the total number of parts and uses more standard and multi-functional components. This is an example of: Top of Form
questions.explanation: Design for X (DfX) is a set of technical guidelines that may be applied during the design of a product for the optimization of a specific aspect of the design. In this scenario, technical guidelines are being applied to design a new modular home that optimizes its manufacturability. DfX provides the most accurate and precise definition of the technique being used in this scenario and is, therefore, of the available choices, is the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: The project manager is ready to transition the final project's deliverable and close the project. Which of the following would ensure the smoothest transition? Top of Form
questions.option_c: Submission of a final project report
questions.option_d: Constant communications with the project sponsor
questions.explanation: Close Project or Phase is the process of finalizing all activities for the project, phase, or contract. One of the outputs from this process is the transitioning of the final product, service, or result to the customer, end user, and/or operations. The question states that the project is ready to transition its final deliverable. There is not enough information in the question to determine who is the receiving end of this transition. However, one of the answer choices mentions an operations manager while other answer choices do not describe a party that would have been considered an entity receiving the final project deliverable. Therefore, it is reasonable to assume that the deliverable is being transitioned into the organization's operations. Hence, of the choices provided, working closely with the operations manager would likely increase the odds of a better transition and project closure and is, therefore, the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: A project manager is estimating the number of work periods needed to complete individual project activities with estimated staff. To complete this process, the project manager should consider all of the following factors except for: Top of Form
questions.explanation: Estimating the number of work periods needed to complete individual project activities with estimated staff is the definition of the Estimate Activity Durations process. There are many factors to consider while carrying out this process. Some of them include staff motivation, number of resources, the law of diminishing returns, and advances in technology. Political awareness is not among these factors. Political awareness refers to the recognition of power relationships, and the willingness to operate within these structures. Political awareness may help the project manager to plan and manage communications, manage and monitor stakeholder engagement, and manage project knowledge. However, among the choices provided, political awareness is the least likely factor to be considered as part of the Estimate Activity Resources process and is, therefore, the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: Stakeholders are not sure what to do about the multiple failures the project has been experiencing recently. Changes need to be made quickly. Otherwise, the project may be terminated, as it is consistently missing its objectives. What should the project manager do first? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Recommend terminating the project as its objectives cannot be met
questions.option_b: Consult with the project sponsor due to the urgency and the severity of the situation
questions.option_c: Implement the most promising change immediately and transparently
questions.option_d: Facilitate a discussion amongst the stakeholders on what should be changed
questions.explanation: Any changes that may potentially modify a deliverable, baseline, or control document must be reviewed, evaluated, and passed via the Perform Integrated Change Control process. That process is triggered by a change request. No action regarding implementation or reaction to a change can take place until the change need is understood and crafted into a change request. That change request(s) - in whichever format determined or suitable for the project - is then evaluated and decided on according to the organization's change control process established for the project. Hence, the first action is to facilitate the discussion of the change request and suggestion responses. One may argue that since the project is in critical condition, engaging several stakeholders with varying degree of influence and interest in the project doesn’t look as the measure to bring the needed results. Consulting with the sponsor, on the other hand, means engaging the person who has authority and resources to make the required changes. While this is true, one should remember that the sponsor is a project stakeholder. Therefore, facilitating a discussion amongst the stakeholders implies involving the sponsor as well.

questions.prompt: A microprocessor manufacturer has released its new chip to the market. The company tried to balance chip performance versus manufacturing expense. The project manager wants to determine whether the performance and features that sophisticated consumers demand from modern processors were met by the new chip. What is the best course of action? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The project manager should gather data that indicates whether or not the project deliverables meet the expectations of the client or end user. The best way to accomplish this task is to ask the client or end-user directly. Questionnaires and surveys are an example of the data gathering technique that can be used in this situation to solicit information about customer satisfaction after the deployment of the product or service. Typically, with the expectations identified in the quality management plan, the project manager needs more data to address any changes or updates to the deliverables that may be needed to meet the quality expectations.

questions.prompt: A project team member ran an earned value analysis (EVA) report that showed an unexpected spike in actual costs over the last three weeks. The team member showed the report to the project manager and based on her analysis presents four possible reasons for the spike. Which reason is most probable? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Soaring steel prices have impacted the fixed price with economic price adjustments procurement
questions.option_b: There was a strike at the loading dock which delayed many needed steel shipments until today
questions.option_c: Fewer employees are showing up for work the past two weeks causing a backlog of unfinished
questions.option_d: Much higher than expected quarterly bonuses were given to executives during that three week
questions.explanation: Earned value analysis (EVA) is an example of the data analysis techniques that can be used as part of the processes in the Monitoring and Controlling Process Group. In the context of this question, the team member has applied the technique to the Control Costs process. By analyzing the earned values and associated costs of the project, the project team can react or make adjustments or request changes. To know the appropriate action to take, the project manager must know how to interpret the data. In the scenario, the variability of the steel prices would be a probable cause of the actual costs spiking beyond the planned values. The other three options do not directly impact the actual costs of the project within the scenario's circumstances.

questions.prompt: The project manager and members of the project team start an Ishikawa diagram for a quality issue. Someone writes down 'source material' as a potential cause. What is the next best action for the project manager? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Instruct the production department to stop using the source material
questions.option_b: Request the team to brainstorm other possible causes of the quality issue
questions.option_c: Suggest switching from the Ishikawa diagram to the cause-and-effect diagram
questions.option_d: Direct the team to focus on issues from the customer perspective of quality
questions.explanation: An Ishikawa diagram is a quality management tool that can be used as part of the Manage Quality process or Control Quality process to assist in the brainstorming, discussion, and examination of potential causes to a problem, issue, or other quality concern. Ishikawa diagrams are also referred to as cause-and-effect diagrams, why-why diagrams, and fishbone diagrams - due to the resemblance of a fish skeleton with the problem at the head and possible causes branching out. Similar to a mind map, Ishikawa diagrams can extend out and are shaped by the input of the team. There is no correct path. It is an exercise to brainstorm and ask how and why. After the tool stimulates enough thinking, the targeted causes from the diagram can be examined further with follow up actions suggested. Until then, the project manager should encourage and facilitate more brainstorming and good discussion.

questions.prompt: A project manager is leading an infrastructure development project and is just starting the process of prioritizing individual project risks for further analysis. A stakeholder has expressed concern that the existing information about individual project risks may not be accurate and reliable. What is the best course of action? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Perform the Identify Risks process again
questions.option_b: Capture the concern in the stakeholder register
questions.option_c: Perform a risk data quality assessment
questions.option_d: Conduct a risk probability and impact assessment
questions.explanation: The question implies that the Perform Qualitative Risk Analysis process is underway. A risk data quality assessment is an example of the data analysis technique that can be used in this process. A risk data quality assessment evaluates the degree to which the data about individual project risks is accurate and reliable as a basis for qualitative risk analysis. The use of low-quality risk data may lead to a qualitative risk analysis that is of little use to the project. If data quality is unacceptable, it may be necessary to gather better data. Risk data quality may be assessed via a questionnaire measuring the project's stakeholder perceptions of various characteristics, which may include completeness, objectivity, relevance, and timeliness. A weighted average of selected data quality characteristics can then be generated to provide an overall quality score. Therefore, of the available choices, to address the stakeholder's concern, performing a risk data quality assessment is the best course of action. Note, ideally, the project manager would have to meet with the stakeholder to better understand the concerns expressed by the stakeholder before spending project resources to perform the risk data quality assessment. However, this answer choice is not provided.

questions.prompt: The project charter for a cross-functional project has already been approved. The project manager wants to identify stakeholders as soon as possible so that the project planning can start. The project manager quickly creates a document listing the project manager, project sponsor, and the project customer as the only project stakeholders. What has the project manager done wrong? Top of Form
questions.option_a: The project manager did not identify all of the project stakeholders.
questions.option_b: The project manager has done nothing wrong, as the people that are documented are all project
questions.option_c: The project manager should have identified all project stakeholders before the approval of the
questions.option_d: The project manager should have done this exercise after developing the project management
questions.explanation: A stakeholder is an individual, group, or organization that may affect, be affected by, or perceive itself to be affected by a decision, activity, or outcome of a project, program, or portfolio. Identifying project stakeholders is an iterative activity that involves identifying both the positive, as well as negative stakeholders and documenting their relevant interests, involvement and impact in the project. Although the people the project manager listed are indeed project stakeholders, it is clear from the scenario that the project manager did not identify all of the project stakeholders. For example, potential stakeholders include team members as well as groups, departments and other people and organizations who may be impacted by the project. The project charter is one of the inputs to the Identify Stakeholders process. Therefore, the project manager could not identify the project stakeholders before the approval of the project charter. The stakeholder register is used for planning that will help shape the project management plan.

questions.prompt: You are leading a project to construct a new runway at an existing commercial airport. After completion of the project, the residents under the flight path of the new runway are making noise complaints and want the airport to compensate them for the diminished values of their homes. What might you have missed earlier in the project? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Including in the stakeholder register those with a contribution stake in the project
questions.option_b: Stakeholder analysis including consideration for those with an interest stake in the project
questions.option_c: A thorough review of the agreements prior to the development of the stakeholder register
questions.option_d: The inclusion of the affected homeowners in the project charter
questions.explanation: The question indicates that the homeowners under the flight path of the new runway have been negatively impacted by the resulting noise as well as a potential reduction in property values. This situation implies that the impact of the project on the affected homeowners was not considered or was just disregarded. Stakeholder analysis is used during the development of the stakeholder register to identify relevant information about the stakeholders including their stakes in the project. In this case, the affected homeowners, whether they realized it at the time or not, had an interest stake in the project. Any person or group that is affected by a decision related to the project or its outcomes has an interest stake in a project. Had the impact of the project on the affected homeowners been fully considered, steps may have been taken to mitigate the impact of the project on the homeowners. Mitigation steps may have included noise abatement procedures for aircraft or perhaps a different runway orientation. Therefore, of the available options, stakeholder analysis including consideration for those with an interest stake in the project is what you likely missed during project initiation.

questions.prompt: The project requires frequent input from the senior management team. Some of the past projects succeeded to engage the team, however, most failed as senior management is notorious throughout the organization of responding slowly and not attending meetings. What is the best strategy for the stakeholder engagement plan of the current project? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Patiently wait until management indicates that they are available to provide their input
questions.option_b: Schedule regular, short update meetings at a constant time and convenient location
questions.option_c: Review lessons learned from other projects involving the management stakeholders
questions.option_d: Require one senior management representative to attend meetings or the project will be halted
questions.explanation: The scenario describes the Plan Stakeholder Engagement process. Lessons learned repository is an organizational process asset which can be used as an input to this process. When planning stakeholder engagement for the project, the project manager should take in the lessons learned from other projects that had to engage with similar stakeholders. In the scenario, the project stakeholders of the senior management team are known not to respond or attend meetings. Other projects have learned ways to gather feedback or other forms of engagement. Learning from their successes and failures will reduce the chances of the current project repeating failures and increase successes. Planning approaches without consulting past or current attempts and methods risks the project wasting time, money, resources, and more.

questions.prompt: At a steering committee meeting, the committee chair reprimands the project manager for not following the company’s guidelines for exchange, storage, and retrieval of project information. The project manager explains that she followed the team's recommendation as to how to manage project information. What most likely caused this situation? Top of Form
questions.option_a: The project manager did not research the enterprise environmental factors
questions.option_b: The project manager did not review the organizational process assets
questions.option_c: The project team did not investigate communication tools to be used for information sharing
questions.option_d: The project manager did nothing wrong
questions.explanation: Many companies have policies for the storage, retention, distribution, safeguarding, and access to company information, which includes project data. These standardized guidelines are considered organizational process assets (OPAs), which are defined as plans, processes, policies, procedures, and knowledge bases that are specific to and used by the performing organization. OPAs are among the inputs to all processes of the Project Communications Management Knowledge Area. The question indicates that the project manager did not follow the company’s guidelines for exchange, storage, and retrieval of project information. Had a review of the OPAs been conducted, the project manager would have realized that the company's guidelines required the use of the company's file sharing system and, likely, would have rejected the project team's recommendation to manage project information differently. Therefore, of the choices provided, the most likely cause for the situation presented in the scenario was the project manager's failure to review the organizational process assets.

questions.prompt: An organization is using multiple systems and tools from three different vendors. The organization wants to undertake a project that would integrate those systems and tools. Which is the best course of action by the organization? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Charter a project with all vendors involved
questions.option_b: Wait until procurement documents are written before involving vendors
questions.option_c: Make the vendors the customer of the project
questions.option_d: Separate the three vendors into three projects to be managed concurrently
questions.explanation: The scenario suggests that the project has not yet been chartered. The project is still undergoing feasibility studies and other initiating activities. Partnering with the owners of the various systems already in use that are targeted for integration is ensuring more success than not involving them. Crafting the project charter with the input from all key stakeholders - which includes the system vendors - can align the project objective with the abilities and interests of the suppliers. The inclusion of the vendors provides valuable information about goals, timing, assumptions, constraints, and other considerations included in a project charter.

questions.prompt: A project has been chartered with so many unknowns and little clarity as to how the products are to be built and designed even though it is extremely clear to all that something has to be done and fast. What is the best approach for the project manager to take? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Ensure all stakeholders are in agreement on the product and project scope at the start, then
questions.option_b: Establish an evolving product roadmap, then define and refine the scope as the project progresses
questions.option_c: Stabilize the primary project requirements before any project team members are acquired
questions.option_d: Create a program of different projects to see which one works best
questions.explanation: Defining project and product scope can be accomplished in many different ways depending on the needs of the project and other circumstances. The circumstances in this scenario are that there is a time sensitivity but lots of unknowns. Without clear knowledge of what needs to be done and how, deciding as a team all that needs to be done before started is difficult. Attempting to fully define the project work before starting work may be more time consuming compared to starting with a roadmap and some less clear requirements. Building off lesser requirements and gathering feedback and input provides fodder for the stakeholders to work from and define the scope further. This agile approach to project management enables stakeholders to progressively elaborate the project's outcomes and plans to achieve those outcomes.

questions.prompt: You are leading a project with some of the deliverables outsourced to a vendor. You have been working closely with your procurement administrator to ensure that all claims, invoices, and final payments have been finalized with the vendor. Terms and conditions of the contract have been met, and all deliverables have been accepted. What should you do next? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Notify the vendor that the contract has been completed
questions.option_b: Finalize all activities for the project and release the project team
questions.option_c: Conduct the final inspection of the vendor's deliverables
questions.option_d: Carry out a structured review of the procurement process
