questions.explanation: The scenario implies the Control Procurements process is underway. Closed procurements are an output of this process. When all claims, invoices, and final payments have been finalized with the vendor, when terms and conditions of the contract have been met, and all deliverables have been received, the buyer, usually through its authorized procurement administrator, provides the seller with formal written notice that the contract has been completed. The incorrect answer choices describe actions that either should have been already taken prior to this point in time or are premature. Of the choices provided, noticing the vendor that the contract has been completed is what you should do next.

questions.prompt: A project manager is leading a project to build a new model of all-terrain vehicle (ATV). The resource requirements documentation calls for the suspension parts to be painted. However, in response to an issue captured in the issue log, it has been recommended to use powder coating instead of paint for better corrosion resistance. What should the project manager do first? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Deny the recommendation as it represents gold plating
questions.option_c: Purchase the powder coating equipment and supplies
questions.explanation: If the recommendation to use powder coating instead of paint is accepted as corrective action, it will change the requirements that address corrosion resistance of suspension parts. To implement the recommended corrective action, the project manager will need to acquire the powder coating material and supplies, which may incur additional cost to the project. As it pertains to the acquisition of resources, an approved change request will be required to update the relevant components of the project management plan, such as the cost baseline, to permit the acquisition of the powder coating equipment and supplies. While the correct answer choice does not specifically mention what project artifact is supposed to be addressed by the change request, it can be reasonably assumed that the cost baseline is one of those artifacts. Although the cost baseline may eventually remain unchanged, a change request should be submitted to evaluate the impact. Otherwise, on what basis would a project manager ask the project team to spend time evaluating a request that is not even documented? Therefore, of the available answer choices, submitting a change request is what the project manager should do first.

questions.prompt: Which is an example of a positive schedule variance? Top of Form
questions.option_a: The ratio of work completed to work planned is greater than 1.0
questions.option_b: The ratio of work planned to work completed is greater than 1.0
questions.option_c: The actual costs of the project are lower than the work performed
questions.option_d: The actual costs of the project are lower than the work planned
questions.explanation: The schedule variance (SV) is a measure of schedule performance expressed as the difference between the earned value (EV) to planned value (PV): SV = EV - PV. The schedule performance index (SPI) is a measure of schedule efficiency expressed as the ratio of earned value to planned value: SPI = EV/PV, in other words, the amount of value that has been gained or earned to this point divided by what was planned to be earned up to this point. When the numerator (EV) is greater than the denominator (PV), the SPI ratio is greater than 1. When the minuend (EV) is greater than the subtrahend (PV), the difference is positive. If the ratio is greater than 1.0 (or the difference is positivie), the project is considered ahead of schedule. If the SPI is less than 1.0 (the SV is negative), the project is considered behind schedule. Ideally, a project aims to be equal to 1.0 which indicates that the project is performing as planned.

questions.prompt: Which of the tools and techniques listed below is employed during the Plan Stakeholder Engagement process?
questions.explanation: There are six tools and techniques employed during the Plan Stakeholder Engagement process: ● Expert judgment ● Data gathering (e.g., benchmarking) ● Data analysis ● Decision making ● Data representation ● Meetings

questions.prompt: Which of the following tools and techniques is used for both Control Resources and Manage Quality?
questions.explanation: Control Resources is the process of ensuring that the physical resources assigned and allocated to the project are available as planned, as well as monitoring the planned versus actual utilization of resources and performing corrective action as necessary. Control Resources has four tools and techniques: ● Data analysis (alternatives analysis, cost-benefit analysis, performance reviews, trend analysis) ● Problem solving ● Interpersonal and team skills (negotiation and influencing) ● Project management information systems (resource management or scheduling software) Problem solving is only explicitly listed as a tool and technique for the Control Resources and Manage Quality processes.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is an output of the Develop Schedule process?
questions.explanation: The Develop Schedule process has the following seven outputs: ● Schedule baseline ● Project schedule ● Schedule data ● Project calendars ● Change requests ● Project management plan updates ● Project documents updates The Develop Schedule process is defined as "the process of analyzing activity sequences, durations, resource requirements, and schedule constraints to create the project schedule model for project execution and monitoring and controlling".

questions.prompt: The Conduct Procurements process has six outputs, including: ● Selected sellers ● Change requests ● Project management plan updates ● Project documents updates ● Organizational process assets updates Which of the following outputs is missing from the list?
questions.explanation: Conduct Procurements is the process of obtaining seller responses, selecting a seller, and awarding a contract. The Conduct Procurements process has the following six outputs: ● Selected sellers ● Agreements ● Change requests ● Project management plan updates ● Project documents updates ● Organizational process assets updates

questions.prompt: Which of the following is an output of the Determine Budget process?
questions.explanation: The Determine Budget process has the following outputs: ● Cost baseline ● Project funding requirements ● Project documents updates (cost estimates, project schedule, risk register) Determine Budget is the process of aggregating the estimated costs of individual activities or work packages to establish an authorized cost baseline.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a tool and technique of the Identify Risks process?
questions.explanation: Identify Risks is the process of identifying individual risks as well as sources of overall risk and documenting their characteristics. This process employs six tools & techniques: ● Expert judgment ● Data gathering ● Data analysis (e.g., SWOT analysis) ● Interpersonal and team skills ● Prompt lists ● Meetings

questions.prompt: Updates to which of the following are an output of the Perform Quantitative Risk Analysis process? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The Perform Quantitative Risk Analysis process is where the effect of identified individual project risks and other sources of uncertainty on overall project objectives is numerically analyzed. This process has only one output, which is the project documents updates (e.g., the risk report).

questions.prompt: Which of the following is not a tool and technique used during the Monitor Stakeholder Engagement process?
questions.explanation: Monitor Stakeholder Engagement is the process of monitoring project stakeholder relationships, and tailoring strategies for engaging stakeholders through the modification of engagement strategies and plans. This process uses six tools and techniques: ● Data analysis (including alternatives analysis, root cause analysis, and stakeholder analysis) ● Decision making (including multicriteria decision analysis and voting) ● Data representation (e.g., the stakeholder engagement assessment matrix) ● Communication skills (including feedback and presentations) ● Interpersonal and team skills (including active listening, cultural awareness, leadership, networking, and political awareness) ● Meetings

questions.prompt: Which of the following is not a tool and technique of the Develop Schedule process?
questions.explanation: The Develop Schedule process has the following eight tools and techniques: ● Schedule network analysis ● Critical path method ● Resource optimization ● Data analysis (e.g., what-if scenario analysis) ● Leads and lags ● Schedule compression ● Project management information system ● Agile release planning

questions.prompt: Which of the following is not a tool or technique used during the Monitor Communication process?
questions.explanation: Monitor Communications is the process of ensuring that the information needs of the project and its stakeholders are met. This process has the following five tools and techniques: ● Expert judgment ● Project management information system ● Data analysis ● Interpersonal and team skills (e.g., observations/conversation) ● Meetings

questions.prompt: Which of the following is not an input to the Plan Communications Management process?
questions.explanation: Plan Communications Management is the process of developing an appropriate approach and plan for project communication activities based on the information needs of each stakeholder or group, available organizational assets, and the needs of the project. The Plan Communications Management process has the following five inputs: ● Project charter ● Project management plan ● Project documents (e.g., stakeholder register) ● Enterprise environmental factors ● Organizational process assets

questions.prompt: Which of the following is not an input to the Develop Team process?
questions.explanation: Develop Team is the process of improving competences, team member interaction, and overall team environment to enhance project performance. It has the following four inputs: ● Project management plan (resource management plan especially the team performance assessment criteria, as well as guidance on rewards, feedback, training, and disciplinary actions) ● Project documents (lessons learned register, project schedule, project team assignments, resource calendars, team charter) ● Enterprise environmental factors (human resource management policies, team member competencies, and geographic distribution) ● Organizational process assets (historical information and lessons learned repository) The issue log is not an input project document for the Develop Team process, which is focused on improving team competencies and interaction, and the overall team environment. Instead, the Manage Team process, which is focused on oversight and issue resolution, uses the issue log as an input for documenting and monitoring who is responsible for resolving issues by a target date.

questions.prompt: All of the following are inputs to the Close Project or Phase process except:
questions.explanation: Close Project or Phase is the process of finalizing all activities for the project, phase, or contract. The Close Project or Phase process has the following eight inputs: ● Project charter ● Project management plan ● Project documents ● Accepted deliverables ● Business documents ● Agreements ● Procurement documentation ● Organizational process assets

questions.prompt: All of the following are tools and techniques used in the Manage Project Knowledge process except:
questions.explanation: The Manage Project Knowledge process has the following four tools and techniques: ● Expert judgment ● Knowledge management ● Information management ● Interpersonal and team skills (e.g., facilitation, networking, political awareness) Manage Project Knowledge is the process of using existing knowledge and creating new knowledge to achieve the project's objectives and contribute to organizational learning.

questions.prompt: The Conduct Procurements process has six inputs, including: ● Project management plan ● Project documents ● Procurement documentation ● Enterprise environmental factors ● Organizational process assets Which of the following inputs is missing from the list?
questions.explanation: Conduct Procurements is the process of obtaining seller responses, selecting a seller, and awarding a contract. The Conduct Procurements process has the following six inputs: ● Project management plan ● Project documents ● Procurement documentation (e.g., source selection criteria, procurement statement of work) ● Seller proposals ● Enterprise environmental factors ● Organizational process assets

questions.prompt: Which of the following is not an input to the Plan Schedule Management process?
questions.explanation: The Plan Schedule Management process has the following four inputs: ● Project charter ● Project management plan (e.g., scope management plan, development approach) ● Enterprise environmental factors ● Organizational process assets

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a tool or technique used in the Perform Integrated Change Control process?
questions.explanation: Perform Integrated Change Control is the process of reviewing all change requests; approving changes and managing changes to deliverables, organizational process assets, project documents, and the project management plan; and communicating the decisions. The Perform Integrated Change Control process has the following five tools and techniques: ● Expert judgment ● Change control tools ● Data analysis ● Decision making (e.g., multicriteria decision analysis) ● Meetings

questions.prompt: Which of the following is not an input to the Plan Risk Responses process?
questions.explanation: Plan Risk Responses is the process of developing options, selecting strategies, and agreeing on actions to address overall project risk exposure, as well as to treat individual project risks. This process has the following four inputs: ● Project management plan (including the risk management plan, the resource management plan, and the cost baseline) ● Project documents (including the lessons learned register, the project schedule, project team assignments, resource calendars, the risk register, the risk report, and the stakeholder register) ● Enterprise environmental factors ● Organizational process assets

questions.prompt: All of the following are inputs to the Manage Project Knowledge process except:
questions.explanation: The Manage Project Knowledge process has the following five inputs: ● Project management plan ● Project documents (e.g., lessons learned register, project team assignments) ● Deliverables ● Enterprise environmental factors ● Organizational process assets The Manage Project Knowledge is the process of using existing knowledge and creating new knowledge to achieve the project's objectives and contribute to organizational learning./p>

questions.prompt: Which of the following is not a tool or technique of the Estimate Activity Resources process?
questions.explanation: Estimate Activity Resources is the process of estimating team resources and the type and quantities of material, equipment, and supplies necessary to perform project work. This process has the following seven tools and techniques: ● Expert judgment ● Bottom-up estimating ● Analogous estimating ● Parametric estimating ● Data analysis ● Project management information systems (PMIS) ● Meetings Using a range to account for uncertainty and risk, the three-point estimating technique is used only to estimate activity durations and costs. All the other estimating techniques create single-point estimates, which are appropriate for not only activity duration and cost but also human resources and discrete physical supplies.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a tool or technique of the Control Costs process?
questions.explanation: The Control Costs process has the following tools and techniques: ● Expert judgment ● Earned value management (EVM) ● Variance analysis ● Trend analysis ● Reserve analysis ● To-complete performance index (TCPI) ● Project management information systems Control Costs is the process of monitoring the status of the project to update the project costs and manage changes to the cost baseline.

questions.prompt: Which input of the Manage Stakeholder Engagement process describes the methods, formats, and technologies used to communicate with stakeholders?
questions.explanation: Manage Stakeholder Engagement is the process of communicating and working with stakeholders to meet their needs and expectations, address issues, and foster appropriate stakeholder involvement. The process has the following four inputs: ● Project management plan (including the communications management plan, stakeholder engagement plan, the risk management plan, the and the change management plan) ● Project documents (including the change log, the issue log, the lessons learned register, and the stakeholder register) ● Enterprise environmental factors ● Organizational process assets

questions.prompt: Which of the following statements is false?
questions.option_a: The Estimate Activity Durations process uses both decision-making techniques and
questions.option_b: The Estimate Activity Durations process uses the following organizational process assets:
questions.option_c: The risk register provides a list of risks, the results of risk analysis, and risk response
questions.option_d: The Estimate Activity Durations process can produce updates to the activity attributes.
questions.explanation: The Estimate Activity Durations process has the following four inputs: ● Project management plan ● Project documents ● Enterprise environmental factors (e.g., published commercial information) ● Organizational process assets (e.g., historical information, scheduling methodology) It is in this process that the number of work periods needed to complete individual activities with the estimated resources is estimated.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is not a tool or technique used in the Plan Schedule Management process?
questions.explanation: The Plan Schedule Management process has the following three tools and techniques: ● Expert judgment ● Data analysis (e.g., alternatives analysis) ● Meetings

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a tool and technique of the Perform Qualitative Risk Analysis process?
questions.explanation: Perform Qualitative Risk Analysis is the process of prioritizing individual project risks for further analysis or action by assessing their probability of occurrence and impact as well as other characteristics. This process has seven tools and techniques: ● Expert judgment ● Data gathering ● Data analysis ● Interpersonal and team skills ● Risk categorization ● Data representation (e.g., probability and impact matrix) ● Meetings

questions.prompt: The Sequence Activities process has four tools and techniques, including: ● Precedence diagramming method (PDM) ● Dependency determination and integration ● Project management information system Which of the following tools and techniques is missing from the list?
questions.explanation: The Sequence Activities process has the following four tools and techniques: ● Precedence diagramming method (PDM) ● Dependency determination and integration ● Leads and lags ● Project management information system This process is where the relationships among the project activities is identified and documented.

questions.prompt: There was a delay in the delivery of materials and equipment that have been purchased from a supplier. Where should you look for guidance on resolving this issue? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Resource management plan and procurement contracts
questions.option_b: Resource requirements and policies for resource control
questions.option_c: Issue escalation procedures and project schedule
questions.option_d: Work performance data and lessons learned repository
questions.explanation: The scenario suggests you are performing the Control Resources process. Control Resources is the process of ensuring that the physical resources assigned and allocated to the project are available as planned, as well as monitoring the planned versus actual utilization of resources and taking actions as necessary. While all answer choices can be valid inputs to the Control Resources process, only the resource management plan and procurement contracts specify actions that should be taken while resolving issues with suppliers.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a tool or technique of the Validate Scope process? Top of Form
questions.explanation: With only two tools and techniques, namely inspection and decision making, the Validate Scope process focuses on examining deliverables to decide whether the work meets the requirements and acceptance criteria.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is an output of the Develop Team process?
questions.explanation: Develop Team is the process of improving competences, team member interaction, and overall team environment to enhance project performance. The Develop Project Team process has the following six outputs: ● Team performance assessments (effectiveness in performing assignments, working as a team, reducing turnover, open sharing and helping one another) ● Change requests ● Project management plan updates (resource management plan) ● Project document updates (lessons learned register, project schedule, project team assignments, resource calendars, team charter) ● Enterprise environmental factors updates (employee development plan records and skill assessments) ● Organizational process assets updates (training requirements and personnel assessments)

questions.prompt: Which of the following is not an input used in the same process that uses work performance information to track, review, and report on progress to meet the performance objectives defined in the project management plan?
questions.explanation: The question is referring to the Monitor and Control Project Work process which has six inputs: ● Project management plan ● Project documents (e.g., schedule forecasts, costs forecasts, quality reports) ● Work performance information ● Agreements ● Enterprise environmental factors ● Organizational process assets Monitor and Control Project Work is the process of tracking, reviewing, and reporting overall progress to meet the performance objectives defined in the project management plan. It is helpful to note that work performance information is an input only to the Monitor and Control Project Work process.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is not a tool and technique of the Manage Stakeholder Engagement process?
questions.explanation: Manage Stakeholder Engagement is the process of communicating and working with stakeholders to meet their needs and expectations, address issues, and foster appropriate stakeholder involvement. This process has the following five tools and techniques: ● Expert judgment ● Communication skills (e.g., feedback) ● Interpersonal and team skills (including conflict management, cultural awareness, negotiation, observation/conversation, and political awareness) ● Ground rules ● Meetings

questions.prompt: Approved change requests are a result of which of the following processes?
questions.explanation: Perform Integrated Change Control is the process of reviewing all change requests; approving changes and managing changes to deliverables, organizational process assets, project documents, and the project management plan; and communicating the decisions. The Perform Integrated Change Control process has the following three outputs: ● Approved change requests ● Project management plan updates ● Project document updates

questions.prompt: Which of the following is an output of the Estimate Activity Resources process?
questions.explanation: Estimate Activity Resources is the process of estimating team resources and the type and quantities of material, equipment, and supplies necessary to perform project work. This process has four outputs: ● Resource requirements ● Basis of estimates ● Resource breakdown structure by category (labor, material, equipment, and supplies) and type (experience, education, certifications, and skills) ● Project documents updates

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a tool or technique of the Plan Resource Management process?
questions.explanation: Plan Resource Management is the process of defining how to estimate, acquire, manage, and utilize physical and team resources. The Plan Resource Management process has the following four tools and techniques: ● Expert judgment ● Data representation (hierarchical charts, responsibility assignment matrix, and text-oriented formats) ● Organizational theory ● Meetings

questions.prompt: Decomposition is a tool and technique of which two processes? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Create WBS and Estimate Activity Resources
questions.option_b: Create WBS and Plan Risk Management
questions.explanation: Decomposition is a technique used for dividing and subdividing the project scope and project deliverables into smaller, more manageable parts. It is explicitly listed as a tool and technique for both the Create WBS and the Define Activities processes.

questions.prompt: The outputs of the Create WBS process include the project scope statement, the work package(s), the planning package(s), the WBS dictionary, and updates to the assumption log and the requirements documentation. Which of the following outputs is missing from this list?
questions.explanation: The Create WBS process subdivides the project deliverables and project work into smaller, more manageable components. It has the following two outputs: ● The project scope baseline includes the project scope statement, the work package(s), the planning package(s), the work breakdown structure, and the WBS dictionary ● The updates to project documents including the assumption log and the requirements documentation.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is not a tool or technique of the Estimate Costs process?
questions.explanation: Estimate Costs is the process of developing an approximation of the monetary resources needed to complete project work. This process has the following ten tools and techniques: ● Expert judgment ● Analogous estimating ● Parametric estimating ● Bottom-up estimating ● Three-point estimates ● Alternatives analysis ● Reserve analysis ● Cost of quality ● Project management information systems ● Decision making

questions.prompt: The Plan Risk Responses process has three outputs, including: ● Project management plan updates ● Project documents updates Which of the following outputs is missing from the list?
questions.explanation: Plan Risk Responses is the process of developing options, selecting strategies, and agreeing on actions to address overall project risk exposure, as well as to treat individual project risks. It has the following three outputs: ● Change requests ● Project management plan updates (including the schedule, cost, quality, resource, and procurement management plans, and the scope, schedule, and cost baselines) ● Project documents updates (including the assumption log, cost forecasts, the lessons learned register, the project schedule, project team assignments, the risk register, and the risk report)

questions.prompt: Which of the following is not an output of the Monitor Risks process?
questions.explanation: Monitor Risks is the process of monitoring the implementation of agreed-upon risk response plans, tracking identified risks, identifying and analyzing new risks, and evaluating risk process effectiveness throughout the project. This process has five outputs, including: ● Work performance information ● Change requests ● Project management plan updates (any component) ● Project documents updates (including the assumption log, the issue log, the lessons learned register, the risk register, and the risk report) ● Organizational process assets updates

questions.prompt: Which output of the Perform Qualitative Risk Analysis process includes a prioritized list of all identified risks and a summary conclusion?
questions.explanation: Perform Qualitative Risk Analysis is the process of prioritizing individual project risks for further analysis or action by assessing their probability of occurrence and impact as well as other characteristics. This process has one output, project documents updates, which contains four documents that are updated: ● Assumption log ● Issue log ● Risk register ● Risk report

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a tool and technique of the Plan Risk Management Process?
questions.explanation: Plan Risk Management is the process of defining how to conduct risk management activities for a project. This process has the following three tools & techniques: ● Expert judgment ● Data analysis ● Meetings

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a tool or technique of the Develop Project Charter process?
questions.explanation: The Develop Project Charter process has the following four tools and techniques: ● Expert judgment ● Data gathering ● Interpersonal and team skills ● Meetings It is in this process where a document that formally authorizes the existence of a project is developed. The charter provides the project manager with the authority to apply organizational resources to project activities.

questions.prompt: Which of the below is not an input of the Plan Stakeholder Engagement process?
questions.explanation: Plan Stakeholder Engagement is the process of developing approaches to involve project stakeholders, based on their needs, expectations, interests, and potential impact on the project. This process has the following six inputs: ● Project charter ● Project management plan (including the communications management plan) ● Project documents (including the assumption log and the risk register) ● Agreements ● Enterprise environmental factors ● Organizational process assets

questions.prompt: Which of the following is an input to the Develop Project Charter process?
questions.explanation: The Develop Project Charter is the process of developing a document that formally authorizes the existence of a project and provides the project manager with the authority to apply organizational resources to project activities. It has four inputs: ● Business documents ● Agreements ● Enterprise environmental factors ● Organizational process assets

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a tool and technique of the Develop Schedule process?
questions.explanation: The Develop Schedule process has the following eight tools and techniques: ● Schedule network analysis ● Critical path method ● Resource optimization ● Data analysis ● Leads and lags ● Schedule compression ● Project management information system ● Agile release planning

questions.prompt: Which of the following techniques may need to use duration estimates? Top of Form
questions.explanation: In modeling and simulation, the project model is iteratively computed with input values chosen randomly from the cost or schedule estimates for each iteration and the probability distribution of these variables. SWOT analysis is used during the Identify Risks process. Probability and impact matrix is an example of a data representation technique used in the Perform Qualitative Risk Analysis process. Sensitivity analysis is used to compare the relative impact of risks on project objectives and also does not required to create duration estimates.

questions.prompt: The Estimate Costs process has three outputs, including: ● Cost estimates ● Project documents updates Which of the following outputs is missing from the list?
questions.explanation: Estimate Costs is the process of developing an approximation of the monetary resources needed to complete project work. It has the following three outputs: ● Cost estimates ● Basis of estimates ● Project document updates (assumption log, lessons learned register, and risk register)

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a tool or technique of the Estimate Activity Durations process?
questions.explanation: The Estimate Activity Durations Process is where the number of work periods needed to complete individual activities with the estimated resources are estimated. This process has the following eight tools and techniques: ● Expert judgment ● Analogous estimating ● Parametric estimating ● Three-point estimating ● Bottom-up estimating ● Data analysis ● Decision making ● Meetings

questions.prompt: The Perform Quantitative Risk Analysis process has five tools and techniques, including: ● Data gathering ● Expert judgment ● Interpersonal and team skills ● Data analysis Which of the following tools and techniques is missing from the list?
questions.explanation: Perform Quantitative Risk Analysis is the process of numerically analyzing the effect of identified individual project risks and other sources of uncertainty on overall project objectives. This process has five tools and techniques, including: ● Data gathering ● Expert judgment ● Representations of uncertainty ● Interpersonal and team skills ● Data analysis

questions.prompt: Which of the following processes uses expert judgment as a tool and technique?
questions.explanation: The Develop Project Management Plan is the process of defining, preparing, and coordinating all plan components and consolidating them into an integrated project management plan. This process has the following four tools and techniques: ● Expert judgment ● Data gathering ● Interpersonal and team skills ● Meetings

questions.prompt: What enterprise environmental factors can influence the Estimate Activity Resources process? Top of Form
questions.explanation: Resource location, resource availability, and skills are all enterprise environmental factors that can influence the Estimate Activity Resources process, along with organizational culture, published estimating data, and marketplace conditions. These factors help to identify the type and quantities of human resources, materials, equipment, and supplies necessary for members of your project teams to complete project activities in the environment where they work.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a tool or technique of the Manage Quality process?
questions.explanation: The Manage Quality process has the following eight tools and techniques: ● Data gathering ● Data analysis ● Decision making ● Data representation ● Audits ● Design for X ● Problem solving ● Quality improvement methods

questions.prompt: Which of the following is not a tool or technique of the Develop Team process?
questions.explanation: Develop Team is the process of improving competences, team member interaction, and overall team environment to enhance project performance. It has the following eight tools and techniques: ● Colocation ● Virtual teams ● Communication technology (shared portal, video conferencing, audio conferencing, email/chat) ● Interpersonal and team skills (conflict management, influencing, motivation, negotiation, team building) ● Recognition and rewards ● Training (competencies) ● Individual and team assessments (preferences, aspirations, information processing, decision making, work relationships) ● Meetings (project orientation, team building, team development)

questions.prompt: Which of the following is an input to the Perform Integrated Change Control process?
questions.explanation: The Perform Integrated Change Control process has the following six inputs: ● Project management plan ● Project documents ● Work performance reports ● Change requests ● Enterprise environmental factors ● Organizational process assets Perform Integrated Change Control is the process of reviewing all change requests; approving changes and managing changes to deliverables, organizational process assets, project documents, and the project management plan; and communicating the decisions.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is not an input to the Plan Resource Management process?
questions.explanation: The Plan Resource Management process has the following five inputs: ● Project charter ● Project management plan (quality management plan and scope baseline) ● Project documents (project schedule, requirements documentation, risk register, stakeholder register) ● Enterprise environmental factors or EEFs (culture, structure, geographic distribution, existing team’s competencies and availability, and market conditions) ● Organizational process assets or OPAs (policies and procedures for human resources, physical resource management, safety, security, templates for the resource management plan, and historical project information) Work performance information is not an input to the Plan Resource Management process. Work performance data is an output of the Executing process, Direct and Manage Project Work. Control Resources is one of the Monitoring and Controlling processes that analyze the data to output work performance information. In turn, this information serves as an input to Monitor and Control Project Work so that data analysis can produce work performance reports. These reports feed into the Perform Integrated Change Control, Manage Team, Manage Communications, and Monitor Risks processes.

questions.prompt: Which of the following components of the project management plan is an input to the Plan Risk Management process? Top of Form
questions.option_d: All components of the project management plan
questions.explanation: In planning risk management, all approved subsidiary management plans should be taken into consideration to make the risk management plan consistent with them. The methodologies outlined in the various project management plan components might influence the Plan Risk Management process.

questions.prompt: The Manage Communications process has five inputs, including: ● Project documents ● Work performance reports ● Enterprise environmental factors ● Organizational process assets Which of the following inputs is missing from the list?
questions.explanation: Manage Communications is the process of ensuring timely and appropriate collection, creation, distribution, storage, retrieval, management, monitoring, and the ultimate disposition of project information. This process has the following five inputs: ● Project management plan (e.g., resource management plan) ● Project documents ● Work performance reports ● Enterprise environmental factors ● Organizational process assets

questions.prompt: Which element of the project management plan is not typically updated as an output of the Conduct Procurements process?
questions.explanation: Conduct Procurements is the process of obtaining seller responses, selecting a seller, and awarding a contract. This process has the following six outputs: ● Selected sellers ● Agreements ● Change requests ● Project management plan updates (e.g., communications management plan, scope baseline, procurement management plan) ● Project documents updates ● Organizational process assets While change requests can be submitted as a result of carrying out the Conduct Procurements process, the change management plan itself is not typically updated during that process. All the other choices represent elements of the project management plan that may be updated during the Conduct Procurements process.

questions.prompt: The Control Schedule process has five outputs, including: ● Schedule forecasts ● Change requests ● Project management plan updates ● Project documents updates Which of the following outputs is missing from the list?
questions.explanation: The Control Schedule process has the following five outputs: ● Work performance information ● Schedule forecasts ● Change requests ● Project management plan updates ● Project documents updates This process is defined as "the process of monitoring the status of the project to update the project schedule and manage changes to the schedule baseline".

questions.prompt: Which of the following is an output of the Monitor and Control Project Work process?
questions.explanation: The Monitor and Control Project Work process has the following four outputs: ● Work performance reports ● Change requests ● Project management plan updates ● Project documents updates Monitor and Control Project Work is the process of tracking, reviewing, and reporting overall progress to meet the performance objectives defined in the project management plan.

questions.prompt: The Define Activities process has four tools and techniques, including: ● Decomposition ● Expert judgment ● Meetings Which of the following tools and techniques is missing from the list?
questions.explanation: The Define Activities process has the following four tools and techniques: ● Expert judgment ● Decomposition ● Rolling wave planning ● Meetings

questions.prompt: The Determine Budget process has nine inputs, including: ● Cost management plan ● Resource management plan ● Scope baseline ● Basis of estimates ● Cost estimates ● Project schedule ● Risk register ● Benefits management plan ● Enterprise environmental factors ● Organizational process assets Which of the following inputs is missing from the list?
questions.explanation: The Determine Budget process has the following inputs: ● Cost management plan ● Resource management plan ● Scope baseline ● Basis of estimates ● Cost estimates ● Project schedule ● Risk register ● Benefits management plan ● Agreements ● Enterprise environmental factors ● Organizational process assets Determine Budget is the process of aggregating the estimated costs of individual activities or work packages to establish an authorized cost baseline.

questions.prompt: Which of the following processes assesses the quality of risk data? Top of Form
questions.explanation: Risk data quality assessment is part of the Perform Qualitative Risk Analysis process to evaluate risk data in terms of the accuracy, reliability, and integrity. It is very important because low-quality risk data may lead to poor qualitative risk analysis and subsequent response planning. An evaluation of the quality of the risk data also helps to clarify the assessment of each risk's importance to the project.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a tool or technique of the Control Scope process? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The Control Scope process is where the status of the project is monitored. It is also where the product scope and changes to the scope baseline are managed. The process has only one tool and technique, data analysis, which includes variance analysis and trend analysis.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is not an input to the Develop Project Management Plan process?
questions.explanation: The Develop Project Management Plan process has the following four inputs: ● Project charter ● Outputs from other processes ● Enterprise environmental factors ● Organizational process assets It is in this process where all plan components are defined, prepared, coordinated, and consolidated into an integrated project management plan.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is not a tool or technique used in the Define Activities process?
questions.explanation: The Define Activities process has the following four tools and techniques: ● Expert judgment ● Decomposition ● Rolling wave planning ● Meetings

questions.prompt: The Manage Stakeholder Engagement process has three outputs, including: ● Change requests ● Project documents updates Which of the following outputs is missing from the list?
questions.explanation: Manage Stakeholder Engagement is the process of communicating and working with stakeholders to meet their needs and expectations, address issues, and foster appropriate stakeholder involvement. The Manage Stakeholder Engagement process has three outputs, including: ● Change requests ● Project management plan updates (e.g., the communications plan, the stakeholder engagement plan) ● Project document updates (including the change log, the issue log, the lessons learned register, and the stakeholder register)

questions.prompt: One of the project management processes results in only one output. This output specifies the level of precision. What output is that? Top of Form
questions.explanation: Level of precision is defined in the cost management plan. An important distinction should be noted. The 'level of precision' is the degree to which cost estimates will be rounded up or down (e.g., $9,995.59 to $10,000), based on the scope of the activities and magnitude of the project. The 'level of accuracy' is the acceptable range (e.g., 10%) used in determining realistic cost estimates is specified and may include an amount for contingencies.

questions.prompt: If change requests can occur throughout a project but must be approved by the change control board, all of the following are true except? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Change requests are outputs of many processes of the Executing Process Group and Monitoring
questions.option_b: New change requests are inputs to the Perform Integrated Change Control process, while
questions.option_c: New change requests are inputs to the Direct and Manage Project Work, while approved change
questions.option_d: Approved change requests are inputs to three processes: Direct and Manage Project Work,
questions.explanation: Many processes produce change requests as outputs, but only the Perform Integrated Change Control process has change requests as inputs. Likewise, only the Perform Integrated Change Control process has approved change requests as outputs. Approved change requests are inputs to the Direct and Manage Project Work, Control Quality, and Control Procurements processes. During the Direct and Manage Project Work process, approved changes are implemented, and during the Control Quality and Control Procurements processes, implementation of the approved changes is verified.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is an input to the Estimate Activity Durations process?
questions.explanation: The Estimate Activity Durations process has the following four inputs: ● Project management plan ● Project documents (e.g., resource calendars) ● Enterprise environmental factors ● Organizational process assets

questions.prompt: The project has reached a stage where work is being done in seven countries. Project team members need to know in which currency they are to report their cost data. Which of the following should they reference? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The cost management plan provides information and guidance on how the project manager and project team are to plan, structure, account, and control the project costs. The contents of the plan vary based on the needs of the particular project. The project in the scenario is operating in many countries and currencies. To align all the project documents and communications, it is important for this project's cost management plan to detail the currencies to use or not use, when, in which circumstances, and other pertinent requirements of the project. The plan is the go-to location for the team to use the proper cost denotations.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is often required by the buyer and should be supported by the seller to verify compliance of the seller's work processes and deliverables? Top of Form
questions.explanation: Inspections and audits are tools and techniques of the Control Procurements process, which are used to verify compliance and the seller's work processes or deliverables. An inspection is a structured review of the work being performed by the seller. This may involve a simple review of the deliverables or an actual physical review of the work itself. Audits are a structured review of the procurement process. Rights and obligations related to audits should be described in the procurement contract.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is an output of the Monitor Communications process? Top of Form
questions.explanation: Monitor Communications is the process of ensuring that the information needs of the project and its stakeholders are met. It has the following four outputs: ● Work performance information ● Change requests ● Project management plan updates ● Project documents updates

questions.prompt: Which of the following is not an input to the Validate Scope process? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The Validate Scope process is where the completed project deliverables are formally accepted. The process has the following five inputs: ● the project management plan (especially the scope management plan, requirements management plan, and scope baseline), ● project documents (especially the lessons learned register, quality reports, requirements documentation, and requirements traceability matrix), ● verified deliverables (from the Control Quality process), and ● work performance data from the Direct and Manage Project Work process (including the degree of compliance with requirements, number of nonconformities, the severity of the nonconformities, or the number of validation cycles performed in a period). In contrast, a business case is a phase gate document including an economic feasibility study, business needs and requirements, basic scope, project objectives, and benefits, normally used in Initiating a project. Therefore, a business case serves as an input business document for the Develop Project Charter, Close Project or Phase, Collect Requirements, Determine Budget, Plan Procurement Management and Identify Stakeholders processes, but not in the Validate Scope process.

questions.prompt: The Monitor Risks process has four inputs, including: ● Project management plan ● Project documents ● Work performance reports Which of the following inputs is missing from the list? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The Monitor Risks process has four inputs, including: ● Project management plan (e.g., the risk management plan) ● Project documents (including the issue log, the lessons learned register, the risk register, and the risk report) ● Work performance data ● Work performance reports

questions.prompt: Which of the following is an output from the Develop Project Management Plan process? Top of Form
questions.option_b: Component plans from other processes in the Planning Process Group
questions.explanation: The Develop Project Management Plan is the process of defining, preparing, and coordinating all plan components and consolidating them into an integrated project management plan. This process has only one output, which is the Project Management Plan.

questions.prompt: The project manager in charge of the construction of a manufacturing plant in Asia is concerned about the communications flow in the project because there are stakeholders from different countries and cultures. Which of the following should she use to be more efficient at facilitating discussions with timely data? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The scenario suggests the project manager is performing the Manage Communications process. Work performance reports are an input to this process. Work performance reports are a collection of project performance and status information that may be used to facilitate discussion and create communications. Reports that are comprehensive, accurate, and available promptly may help the project manager in facilitating discussions with timely data as asked by the question, making work performance reports the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: You are looking for information on personnel rates, travel costs, and other costs that are needed to estimate the overall project budget. Where is the best place to find this information? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The scenario suggests you are carrying out the Determine Budget process. The resource management plan is one of the inputs into that process. The resource management plan provides information on rates (personnel and other resources), estimation of travel costs, and other foreseen costs that are necessary to estimate the overall project budget. Therefore, the resource management plan is the best place to find information on costs related to estimating the project budget.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a tool or technique of the Control Procurements process? Top of Form
questions.explanation: Control Procurements. The process of managing procurement relationships, monitoring contract performance, making changes and corrections as appropriate, and closing contracts. It has the following five tools and techniques: ● Expert judgment ● Claims administration ● Data analysis (e.g., earned value analysis) ● Inspections ● Audits

questions.prompt: Which of the processes below do not use meetings as a tool or technique? Top of Form
questions.explanation: Meetings are used as a tool and technique in many project management processes. However, as far as the Project Scope Management Knowledge Area is concerned, meetings are only used in the Plan Scope Management process. None of the other five processes of this Knowledge Area, including the Define Scope process, uses meetings as their tools or techniques.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a tool or technique of the Direct and Manage Project Work process? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The Direct and Manage Project Work is defined as the process of leading and performing the work defined in the project management plan and implementing approved changes to achieve the project's objectives. This process has the following three tools and techniques: ● Expert judgment ● Project management information system ● Meetings

questions.prompt: Which of the following is an output of the Control Costs process? Top of Form
questions.explanation: Control Costs is the process of monitoring the status of the project to update the project costs and manage changes to the cost baseline. The Control Costs process has the following outputs: ● Work performance information ● Cost forecasts ● Change requests ● Project management plan updates ● Project documents updates

questions.prompt: Which of the following is an output of the Estimate Activity Durations process? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The Estimate Activity Durations process has the following three outputs: ● Duration estimates ● Basis of estimates ● Project documents updates

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a tool or technique used in the Develop Project Charter process? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The Develop Project Charter is the process of developing a document that formally authorizes the existence of a project and provides the project manager with the authority to apply organizational resources to project activities. This process has the four following tools and techniques: ● Expert judgment ● Data gathering ● Interpersonal and team skills (e.g., conflict management) ● Meetings

questions.prompt: Which of the following is an input to the Monitor and Control Project Work process? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The Monitor and Control Project Work process is the process of tracking, reviewing, and reporting overall progress to meet the performance objectives defined in the project management plan. It has the following six inputs: ● Project management plan ● Project documents ● Work performance Information ● Agreements ● Enterprise environmental factors ● Organizational process assets

questions.prompt: Throughout the project, the stakeholders have been raising various problems, gaps, inconsistencies, and conflicts. Which of the following should be used to capture this information? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The problems, gaps, inconsistencies, and conflicts are collectively known as issues. Issues are recorded in the issue log. The issue log is created as an output of the Direct and Manage Project Work process as a place to document problems, gaps, inconsistencies, or conflicts that occur unexpectedly and may threaten the project objectives. Data on issues may include the issue type, issue originator, description, priority, issue owner, target resolution date, status, final solution, etc. Issues should be proactively monitored and tracked, and their status communicated to stakeholders.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is not an input to the Control Costs process? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The Control Costs process has the following four inputs: ● Project management plan (primarily the cost management plan, cost baseline, and performance measurement baseline) ● Project funding requirements ● Project documents (primarily lessons learned register) ● Work performance data ● Organizational process assets

questions.prompt: During the activities performed to carry out project work, observations and measurements are recorded and then become an input to all of the following processes except: Top of Form
questions.explanation: The Direct and Manage Project Work process is the process of leading and performing the work defined in the project management plan and implementing approved changes to achieve the project's objectives. It has the following seven outputs: ● Deliverables ● Work performance data ● Issue log ● Change requests ● Project management plan updates ● Project document updates ● Organizational process assets updates The Direct and Manage Project Work process is the 'only' process that has work performance data as an output. Work performance data is then used, as an input, in all the Monitoring and Controlling processes 'except' the Perform Integrated Change Control process and the Monitor and Control Project Work process.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a tool or technique of the Collect Requirements process? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The Collect Requirements process has the following tools and techniques: ● Expert judgment ● Data gathering (brainstorming, interviews, focus groups, questionnaires and surveys, and benchmarking) ● Data analysis ● Decision making ● Data representation ● Interpersonal and team skills ● Context diagram ● Prototype Collect Requirements is the process of determining, documenting, and managing stakeholder needs and requirements to meet project objectives.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is not a tool and technique used during the Monitor Risks process? Top of Form
questions.explanation: Monitor Risks is the process of monitoring the implementation of agreed-upon risk response plans, tracking identified risks, identifying and analyzing new risks, and evaluating risk process effectiveness throughout the project. The Monitor Risks process has the following three tools and techniques: ● Data analysis (including technical performance analysis and reserve analysis) ● Audits (e.g., risk audits) ● Meetings (e.g., risk review meetings)

questions.prompt: Which of the following is an output of the Plan Stakeholder Engagement process? Top of Form
questions.explanation: Plan Stakeholder Engagement is the process of developing approaches to involve project stakeholders based on their needs, expectations, interests, and potential impact on the project. The single output of this process is the stakeholder engagement plan, which is a component of the project management plan that identifies the strategies and actions required to promote the productive involvement of the project's stakeholders in both decision making and execution.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is an input to the Plan Quality Management process? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The Plan Quality Management process has the following five inputs: ● Project charter ● Project management plan ● Project documents (e.g., stakeholder register) ● Enterprise environmental factors ● Organizational process assets Plan Quality Management is the process of identifying quality requirements and/or standards for the project and its deliverables, and documenting how the project will demonstrate compliance with quality requirements and/or standards.

questions.prompt: What components of the project management plan may encourage iterative practices, such as a product backlog and a sprint review, to be included in the scope management plan? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Management reviews and the schedule baseline
questions.option_b: The project life cycle description and development approach
questions.option_c: The requirements management plan and the project charter
questions.option_d: The quality management plan and the cost management plan
questions.explanation: The scope management plan is a component of the project or program management plan that describes how the scope will be defined, developed, monitored, controlled, and validated. The development of the scope management plan begins with the analysis of information contained in the project charter, the latest approved subsidiary plans of the project management plan, as well as EEFs and OPAs. However, the question is asking specifically for components of the project management plan, which rules out items that are not project management plan components. Then, you must narrow the choices to components that may drive the use of iterative practices such as a product backlog and a sprint review. Of the project management plan components shown, the project life cycle description and the development approach are most influential regarding iterative practices in scope management.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is an output of the Sequence Activities process? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The Sequence Activities process is where relationships among the project activities are identified and documented. The process has the following two outputs: ● Project schedule network diagrams ● Project documents updates (e.g., activity list)

questions.prompt: Which of the following is an output of the Develop Project Charter process? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The Develop Project Charter process has the following two outputs: ● Project charter ● Assumption log This process is where a document that formally authorizes the existence of a project is developed. The charter provides the project manager with the authority to apply organizational resources to project activities.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is an input to the Estimate Costs process? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The Estimate Costs process has the following inputs: ● Scope baseline ● Quality management plan ● Cost management plan ● Lessons learned register ● Project schedule ● Resource requirements ● Risk register ● Enterprise environmental factors ● Organizational process assets Estimate Costs is the process of developing an approximation of the monetary resources needed to complete project work.

questions.prompt: Using the decision matrix shown below, what is the EMV for the upgrade option? Top of Form
questions.explanation: Decision trees are used to support the selection of the best of several alternative courses of action. Typically, the option or branch with the highest calculated expected monetary value (EMV). To calculate the branch EMV, the payoff minus costs are calculated for each path within the option. In this example, that would be $70M for the Upgrade Plant, Strong demand path ($70M = $120M - $50M), and $10M for the Upgrade Plant, Weak Demand path ($10M = $60M - $50M). Then each path value is multiplied by the probability of the path, and all path values are summed. For the Upgrade/Strong Demand path the result is $42M ($42M = $70M x 0.60), and the Upgrade/Weak Demand path result is $4M ($4M = $10M x .040). When the two are added, the resulting EMV for the Upgrade Plant option is $46M.

questions.prompt: Which tool or technique used during the Monitor Stakeholder Engagement process helps the project manager understand the strategies of the organization and determine who wields power and influence? Top of Form
questions.explanation: Political awareness is a powerful technique that concerns the recognition of formal and informal power relationships, and the willingness to operate within these relationships. An understanding of the strategies of the organization, knowing who wields power and influence, and developing an ability to communicate with key stakeholders are all aspects of this technique.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is an output of the Identify Risks process? Top of Form
questions.explanation: Identify Risks is the process of identifying individual risks as well as sources of overall risk and documenting their characteristics. This process has three outputs: ● Risk register ● Risk report ● Project documents updates

questions.prompt: Which of the following is an output of the Collect Requirements process? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The Collect Requirements is the process of determining, documenting, and managing stakeholder needs and requirements to meet project objectives. This process has the following two outputs: ● Requirements documentation ● Requirements traceability matrix

questions.prompt: During the past few weeks, there have been many changes to the project deliverables and the project manager is not sure which changes add real business value. Which of the following could the project manager use to evaluate the impact that changes may have on the project objectives? Top of Form
questions.explanation: Serving as an output of the Collect Requirements process, the requirements traceability matrix is a grid that links product requirements from their origin to the deliverables that satisfy them. The implementation of a requirements traceability matrix helps ensure that each requirement adds business value by linking it to the business and project objectives. It provides a means to track requirements throughout the project life cycle, helping to ensure that requirements approved in the requirements documentation are delivered at the end of the project. Finally, it provides a structure for managing changes to the product scope.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is not an input to the Implement Risk Responses process? Top of Form
questions.explanation: Implement Risk Responses is the process of implementing agreed-upon risk response plans. Implement Risk Responses is the process of implementing agreed-upon risk response plans. It has the following three inputs: ● Project management plan (e.g., the risk management plan) ● Project documents (including the lessons learned register, the risk register, and the risk report) ● Organizational process assets

questions.prompt: What is the purpose of resource calendars? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Resource calendars are created during the Develop Team process to show when team members
questions.option_b: Resource calendars are outputs of the Acquire Resources process to show when team members
questions.option_c: When estimating activity resources, you create resource calendars to present the resource
questions.option_d: Resource calendars track when you are assigned to the project but do not account for
questions.explanation: The resource calendars identify the working days and shifts when each specific resource is available. After you acquire the project team, you are ready to document the time periods when each team member is available to work on the project.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is not an input to the Identify Risks process? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The cost management plan, the schedule management plan, and the quality management plan are all inputs to the Identify Risks process. Of the choices provided, only the scope management plan is not an input to the Identify Risks process and is, therefore, the best answer to the question asked. Note, the scope baseline is an input to the Identify Risks process because it includes deliverables and criteria for their acceptance, some of which might introduce risk. However, the scope baseline is not part of the scope management plan. The scope baseline is part of the project management plan which includes the subsidiary plans, such as the scope management plan, the baselines, such as the scope baseline, and some other components.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is an output of the Identify Stakeholders process? Top of Form
questions.explanation: Identify Stakeholders is the process of identifying project stakeholders regularly and analyzing and documenting relevant information regarding their interests, involvement, interdependencies, influence, and potential impact on project success. The Identify Stakeholders process has the following four outputs: ● Stakeholder register ● Change requests ● Project management plan updates ● Project documents updates

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a tool or technique of the Manage Team process? Top of Form
questions.explanation: Manage Team is the process of tracking team member performance, providing feedback, resolving issues, and managing team changes to optimize project performance. The Manage Team process has the following two tools and techniques: ● Interpersonal and team skills (conflict management, decision making, emotional intelligence, influencing and leadership) ● Project management information system

questions.prompt: As a project manager, you are developing a resource management plan for your software development project, and you need to find a way to document and communicate the detailed descriptions of the team members' roles and responsibilities. What tool will best meet your needs? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The question implies that the Plan Resource Management process is underway. Data representation techniques that may be used for this process include charts. Various formats exist to document and communicate team member roles and responsibilities, but most fall into hierarchical, matrix, or text-oriented formats. A hierarchical format may be used to represent high-level roles, while a text-based format may be better suited to document the detailed team member responsibilities. Text-oriented documents provide detailed information such as responsibilities, authority, competencies, and qualifications. These documents are known by various names including position descriptions and role-responsibility-authority forms. Since the question is asking to find a way to document and communicate the 'detailed' descriptions of the team members' roles and responsibilities, using a role-responsibility-authority form is the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: All of the following are included in the scope baseline except: Top of Form
questions.explanation: The scope baseline is a principal output of the Create WBS process. It encompasses the approved version of a scope statement, work breakdown structure (WBS), and its associated WBS dictionary. A baseline can be changed only through formal change control procedures and is used as a basis for comparison during Monitoring and Controlling. In contrast, the requirements traceability matrix is a project document that serves as an output of the Collect Requirements process. The requirements traceability matrix is used to link each requirement with the deliverables that should fulfill the requirement. Unlike the scope baseline, this matrix needs to be updated throughout both the Execution and the Monitoring and Controlling process groups as the project progresses and the status of the requirement fulfillment changes.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a tool or technique used in the Manage Project Knowledge process? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The Manage Project Knowledge Manage is the process of using existing knowledge and creating new knowledge to achieve the project's objectives and contribute to organizational learning. This process has the following four tools and techniques: ● Expert judgment ● Knowledge management ● Information management ● Interpersonal and team skills

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a tool or technique of the Control Schedule process? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The Control Schedule process is where project status is monitored to update the project schedule and manage changes to the schedule baseline. It has the following six tools and techniques: ● Data analysis (e.g., performance reviews) ● Critical path method ● Project management information system ● Resource optimization ● Leads and lags ● Schedule compression

questions.prompt: Which of the following is an example of a result of the Determine Budget process? Top of Form
questions.option_a: $100,000 is required by the project on a quarterly basis.
questions.option_b: The services of a trusted shredding company are required every month to handle sensitive paper
questions.option_c: The project is going to follow a similar structure as a similar implementation project previously
questions.option_d: A signed agreement with a financial institution will be provided to finance the project.
questions.explanation: The Determine Budget process has the following outputs: cost baseline, project funding requirements, and project document updates (namely project schedule, cost estimates, and risk register). The correct answer lists quarterly project funding requirements. The rest of the choices represent different examples of inputs to the Determine Budget process.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is not an output of the Control Quality process? Top of Form
questions.explanation: Control Quality is the process of monitoring and recording results of executing the quality management activities to assess performance and ensure the project outputs are complete, correct, and meet customer expectations. The Control Quality process has the following six outputs: ● Quality control measurements ● Verified deliverables ● Work performance information ● Change requests ● Project management plan updates ● Project documents updates

questions.prompt: Expert judgment is a tool and technique used in all of the following processes except: Top of Form
questions.explanation: Manage Communications is the process of ensuring timely and appropriate collection, creation, distribution, storage, retrieval, management, monitoring, and the ultimate disposition of project information. The Manage Communications process has the following seven tools and techniques: ● Communication technology ● Communication methods ● Communication skills ● Project management information system ● Project reporting ● Interpersonal and team skills ● Meetings The PMBOK® Guide does not include expert judgment as a tool and technique under Manage Communications.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is not an output of the Manage Stakeholder Engagement process? Top of Form
questions.option_b: Updates to the stakeholder engagement plan
questions.option_c: Updates to the communications management plan
questions.explanation: Manage Stakeholder Engagement is the process of communicating and working with stakeholders to meet their needs and expectations, address issues, and foster appropriate stakeholder involvement. There are three outputs of the Manage Stakeholder Engagement process: ● Change results ● Project management plan updates (including the communications management plan and the stakeholder engagement plan) ● Project documents updates (including the change log, the issue log, the lessons learned register, and the stakeholder register)

questions.prompt: Which of the following is not one of the tools and techniques for the Acquire Resources process? Top of Form
questions.explanation: The Acquire Resources process has the following four tools and techniques: ● Decision making (multi-criteria decision analysis) ● Interpersonal and team skills (negotiation) ● Pre-assignment ● Virtual teams In the context of acquiring resources, multicriteria decision analysis serves as the decision-making technique. Using a decision matrix to systematically evaluate several characteristics (such as availability, cost, features, experience, knowledge, skills) shared among the choices, you then rank the choices by the resulting mathematical scores. In the context of quantifying risks, the decision tree analysis uses a diagram and specific formulas to evaluate the impact of a chain of multiple options for the purpose of prioritizing risks and their responses.

questions.prompt: Which of the following data analysis techniques would be best for determining the resources needed at upcoming stages of the project and to see if performance has been improving or deteriorating over time? Top of Form
questions.explanation: Trend analysis is a data analysis technique for several processes in the Monitoring and Controlling process group. In the Control Resources process, the project team may use trend analysis on current performance information to determine the resources needed at upcoming stages of the project. Trend analysis examines project performance over time and can be used to determine whether performance is improving or deteriorating.

questions.prompt: The Manage Communications process has seven tools and techniques, including: ● Communication technology ● Communication skills ● Project management information system ● Performance reporting ● Interpersonal and team skills ● Meetings Which of the following tools and techniques is missing from the list? Top of Form
questions.explanation: Manage Communications is the process of ensuring timely and appropriate collection, creation, distribution, storage, retrieval, management, monitoring, and the ultimate disposition of project information. The Manage Communications process has the following seven tools and techniques: ● Communication technology ● Communication methods ● Communication skills (e.g., communication competence) ● Project management information system ● Performance reporting ● Interpersonal and team skills ● Meetings

questions.prompt: Which of the following tools or techniques is used by buyers during the Control Procurements process to verify the compliance of the seller's deliverables to the contract? Top of Form
questions.explanation: Control Procurements is the process of managing procurement relationships, monitoring contract performance, making changes and corrections as appropriate, and closing out contracts. The process has the following five tools and techniques: ● Expert judgment ● Claims administration ● Data analysis ● Inspections ● Audits

questions.prompt: Which of the following is an input to the Acquire Resources process? Top of Form
questions.explanation: Acquire Resources is the process of obtaining team members, facilities, equipment, materials, supplies, and other resources necessary to complete project work. The Acquire Resources process has the following four inputs: ● Project management plan (resource management plan, procurement management plan and cost baselines) ● Project documents ● EEFs (costs, availability, locations, competence levels, experience, market conditions, organizational structure) ● OPAs

questions.prompt: The financial business analyst of an initial public offering (IPO) project meets with the stakeholders to agree on how business requirements will be documented. What is the expected result of this meeting? Top of Form
questions.option_a: Updates to the requirements management plan
questions.option_b: Updates to the business analysis plan
questions.explanation: In this context, the requirements management plan is the most relevant outcome of the meeting described in the question. A critical output of the Plan Scope Management process, the requirements management plan describes how requirements will be analyzed, documented, and managed.
